title: PRD-001 System Core & Daemon Engine — Conformance Audit
summary: The core daemon engine (supervisor-loop.sh, install-daemon.sh, autonomous-dev.sh) implements the majority of P0 requirements. The bash supervisor loop, heartbeat, lock file, circuit breaker, cost ledger, kill switch, launchd/systemd templates, config loading, and most lifecycle commands are all present and substantially conformant. Key gaps are: (1) the request ID format in production code is REQ-NNNNNN (6 digits, no date/hex) instead of REQ-{YYYYMMDD}-{4-char-hex} as specced; (2) turn_count is never incremented at runtime, defeating FR-508's per-phase budget tracking; (3) the cancel command does not delete git worktrees or remote branches, only updates the DB status; (4) the daemon status command (FR-404) omits uptime, iteration count, active request count, and cost burn for the billing period; (5) FR-405 kill-switch does not send SIGTERM to the running daemon — it only writes the flag file; (6) FR-505 API rate-limit detection has a library (lib/rate_limit_handler.sh) with tests but is never wired into the main supervisor loop; (7) FR-503 per-request cost cap and FR-504 concurrent request cap are configured in defaults but not enforced in the supervisor loop's check_gates(); (8) FR-308 context window management (80% threshold, checkpoint and fresh session) is present in config defaults but has no implementation in the supervisor loop or spawn-session; (9) NFR-05 world-readable config warning for webhook URLs is absent; (10) FR-402 systemd WantedBy uses default.target instead of specced multi-user.target. The overall system is production-capable for the happy path but several governance and safety circuit-breakers that are described as P0 in the PRD are not wired into the running loop.

findings.description: The PRD specifies the format REQ-{YYYYMMDD}-{4-char-hex} (e.g., REQ-20260408-a3f1). The actual implementation uses REQ-NNNNNN (6 sequential digits), validated with ^REQ-[0-9]{6}$. This is a deliberate deviation in the runtime system that contradicts the PRD format everywhere the ID is validated or displayed.
findings.recommendation: Either update the PRD to reflect the 6-digit sequential format that ships, or document the divergence as a deliberate simplification and ensure all tooling (tests, docs) consistently uses one format.

findings.requirement: FR-508 — Track total turn count per request and per phase; fail phase on turn budget exhaustion
findings.description: turn_count is initialized to 0 in the state file and is read for the portal action view, but is never incremented during runtime. The supervisor loop detects turn exhaustion (exit code 2) and treats it as a soft error, but does not write the actual turns used back into state.json. Per-phase turn budget tracking for the 'fail the phase if turns exceeded' contract exists only at the session-spawning level (resolve_max_turns), not at the accounting level.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2082 — only reads turn_count; no write-back of actual turns_used anywhere in update_request_state() or advance_phase()
findings.recommendation: After each session, extract the turn count from the claude JSON output and accumulate it into state.json.turn_count. Track per-phase turn counts in phase_history entries.

findings.requirement: FR-305 — Cancel command SHALL delete git worktrees, delete remote branches (with confirmation), archive state directory
findings.description: The cancel_handler in intake/handlers/cancel_handler.ts only sets status='cancelled' in the database and emits an event. It does not delete git worktrees, does not delete remote branches, and does not archive the state directory. The cleanup_engine.sh supports those operations but is not called from the cancel path.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/handlers/cancel_handler.ts:87 — `this.db.updateRequest(requestId, { status: 'cancelled' });` — no git worktree or branch operations follow
findings.recommendation: Wire cleanup_engine.sh cleanup_worktree() and cleanup_remote_branch() calls into the cancel handler, or add a post-cancellation hook in the supervisor loop that triggers cleanup when it encounters a newly-cancelled request.

findings.requirement: FR-404 — daemon status SHALL report: running/stopped, PID, uptime, iteration count, crash count, circuit-breaker state, active request count, cost burn for current billing period
findings.description: cmd_daemon_status() reports running/stopped, lock PID (alive/stale), circuit breaker tripped/ok with crash count, kill switch state, and last heartbeat timestamp. It does NOT report uptime, iteration count, active request count, or cost burn for the current billing period.
findings.recommendation: Read iteration_count from heartbeat.json, compute uptime from heartbeat timestamp vs. now, count active requests from the allowlist repos, and read today's spend from the cost ledger.

findings.requirement: FR-405 — kill-switch SHALL: (a) write flag file, (b) send SIGTERM to running daemon, (c) future iterations exit until flag cleared
findings.description: cmd_kill_switch() writes the flag file (FR-405a) and the supervisor loop checks for the flag on every iteration (FR-405c). However, it does NOT send SIGTERM to the running daemon process (FR-405b). If the daemon is mid-session when the kill switch is set, it will only stop at the next iteration boundary.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/autonomous-dev.sh:631-648 — cmd_kill_switch writes flag file only, no SIGTERM
findings.recommendation: Read the PID from daemon.lock and send SIGTERM after writing the flag file, similar to how a graceful shutdown is initiated.

findings.requirement: FR-505 — Detect API rate-limit responses and implement exponential backoff (30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 480s, then pause and escalate)
findings.description: A rate_limit_handler.sh library exists with detect_rate_limit(), handle_rate_limit() functions, and tests in tests/unit/test_rate_limit_handler.sh. However, this library is never sourced or called from supervisor-loop.sh. The main loop has no rate-limit detection after session exit.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/lib/rate_limit_handler.sh exists; supervisor-loop.sh sources phase-legacy.sh, phase-helpers.sh, gate-check.sh, typed-limits.sh — never rate_limit_handler.sh
findings.recommendation: Source lib/rate_limit_handler.sh in supervisor-loop.sh and call detect_rate_limit() on the session output file after each session exit to enable the backoff logic.

findings.requirement: FR-503 — Per-request cost cap enforcement (default $50, pause and escalate on breach)
findings.description: The per_request_cost_cap_usd is defined in config_defaults.json (governance section) and referenced in tests and docs, but check_gates() in supervisor-loop.sh only checks daily and monthly caps. There is no per-request cap check in the main loop or in update_cost_ledger()/update_state_cost().
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2713-2756 — check_cost_caps() reads only daily and monthly caps, no per-request check; config/defaults.json has no per_request_cost_cap_usd (only config_defaults.json does)
findings.recommendation: In check_gates() or after each cost update, compare the request's cost_accrued_usd against the configured per_request_cost_cap_usd and call escalate_to_paused() if exceeded.

findings.requirement: FR-504 — Enforce maximum concurrent requests cap (default 3)
findings.description: The max_concurrent_requests setting is documented in config_init.sh comments but is not present in config/defaults.json (the file actually loaded by the daemon), and there is no enforcement of a concurrent request cap anywhere in the supervisor loop or request selection logic. The daemon processes one request at a time per iteration, but does not queue requests in intake beyond the cap.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/config/defaults.json — no max_concurrent_requests field; supervisor-loop.sh select_request() does not count active requests
findings.recommendation: Add max_concurrent_requests to config/defaults.json and enforce it in select_request() or check_gates() by counting requests in running/gate status across the allowlist.

findings.requirement: FR-308 — Context window management: track token usage, at 80% threshold checkpoint and spawn fresh session
findings.description: context_window_threshold_pct: 80 appears in config_defaults.json, and the state_machine section of config_init.sh documents it. However there is no implementation in spawn-session.sh or supervisor-loop.sh that tracks token usage during a session or triggers a checkpoint+fresh-session handoff when approaching the context limit.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/config_defaults.json:59 — `"context_window_threshold_pct": 80`; supervisor-loop.sh has no token tracking or threshold check
findings.recommendation: Implement token-count parsing from claude JSON output and add a mid-session checkpoint mechanism when the 80% threshold is approached, or document that this is deferred to a future phase.

findings.description: The PRD specifies WantedBy=multi-user.target for the systemd unit. The actual template uses WantedBy=default.target. For a user-level systemd service (--user), default.target is the correct value, but it contradicts the PRD literally.
findings.recommendation: The implementation uses default.target because the service is a user unit (systemctl --user). Update the PRD to say WantedBy=default.target or note that user units always use default.target, not multi-user.target.

findings.requirement: FR-207 — Event log fields: timestamp, event_type, from_state, to_state, metadata, session_id
findings.description: Events written to events.jsonl use keys 'event', 'timestamp', 'request_id', 'from', 'to' — not the specced keys 'event_type', 'from_state', 'to_state', 'session_id', 'metadata'. The session_id field from Claude Code sessions is never captured or written to the event log.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2462 — `event: "phase_advance", from: $from, to: $to` — uses 'event' not 'event_type', 'from' not 'from_state', no session_id
findings.recommendation: Align event log field names with the PRD schema, and add session_id capture from the claude --output-format json response.

findings.requirement: FR-605 — Notifications: channels array with file, webhook, escalation_channels, summary_schedule
findings.description: The notifications configuration schema exists in config_init.sh comments and in src/notifications/. However, the daemon's emit_alert() writes only to a local alerts directory (file-based), never dispatching to webhook, Slack, Discord, or email channels configured in the config. The full notification delivery pipeline in src/notifications/ is not called from supervisor-loop.sh.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:1441-1470 — emit_alert() writes to ALERTS_DIR only; no webhook/channel dispatch
findings.recommendation: Wire emit_alert() to the TypeScript notification framework or add shell-level webhook dispatch using curl when notification channels are configured.

findings.requirement: FR-506 — Monitor disk usage, block new requests when threshold exceeded, notify operator
findings.description: disk_usage_limit_gb is defined in config_defaults.json and in config_init.sh comments, but there is no disk usage check in check_gates(), select_request(), or anywhere in supervisor-loop.sh.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2758-2780 — check_gates() checks only kill switch, circuit breaker, and cost caps; no disk check
findings.recommendation: Add a disk usage check in check_gates() using du or df against the worktree root directory.

findings.requirement: FR-607 — Trust levels L0-L3 controlling autonomy degree
findings.description: The trust system is implemented in src/trust/ (TrustLevel type 0-3, gate matrix, trust-engine, trust-change-manager). However, the trust level is not read or enforced in the daemon supervisor loop's session dispatch. Review gates in the supervisor do not consult the trust config to decide human-vs-system approval. The gate matrix implementation exists in TypeScript but is decoupled from the bash pipeline.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/trust/gate-matrix.ts:14-51 — TRUST_GATE_MATRIX defined; supervisor-loop.sh dispatch_phase_session() has no trust level lookup
findings.recommendation: Connect the TypeScript trust engine to the bash supervisor loop, either via a node CLI call or by replicating the gate-matrix logic in bash to gate human-review requirements.

findings.requirement: FR-214 / US-16 — Request dependencies: blocked_by field prevents advance past intake
findings.description: The blocked_by field is present in state files and select_request() skips requests where blocked_by count > 0. However there is no mechanism to automatically unblock a request when its blocking request reaches the specified state. The operator must manually clear blocked_by.
findings.recommendation: Add a reconciliation step that checks blocked requests against their blocking requests' states and automatically clears the blocked_by entry when the blocking request reaches the required state.

findings.requirement: US-07 — PR description SHALL include tracking ID and pipeline phase
findings.description: The code phase prompt instructs the agent to create a PR with a title and body summary, but neither the prompt template nor the instructions mandate including the request tracking ID or pipeline phase in the PR body. The branch is named autonomous/${request_id} but the PR description content is left to the agent.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/lib/phase-helpers.sh:263 — `gh pr create --base main --head 'autonomous/${request_id}' --title <conventional-title> --body <summary>` — body is freeform, no tracking ID requirement
findings.recommendation: Add explicit instructions in the code phase prompt requiring the PR body to include the request_id and current pipeline phase as structured metadata (e.g. a footer section).

findings.requirement: FR-202 — State file SHALL contain phase_history array with per-phase entry details
findings.description: phase_history is initialized as an empty array in new state files and the spec calls for appending phase entries with session_id, turns_used, cost_usd. In practice, the supervisor appends events to events.jsonl but does not populate phase_history[] entries with the per-session accounting fields. The array remains empty or agents optionally append to it.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/myorg/api/.autonomous-dev/requests/REQ-000001/state.json:14 — `"phase_history": []`; update_request_state() in supervisor-loop.sh does not append to phase_history
findings.recommendation: In advance_phase() when marking a phase complete, append a structured entry to phase_history[] containing the phase name, start/end timestamps, turns_used (if available), and cost_usd for that phase.

findings.requirement: NFR-05 — Warn if config files with sensitive values (webhook URLs) are world-readable
findings.description: The PRD requires that configuration files containing webhook URLs emit a warning if world-readable. install-daemon.sh sets 600 permissions on daemon state files, but config_validate.sh and config_show.sh do not check or warn about world-readable permissions on the config file itself.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/commands/config_validate.sh — no permission check; install-daemon.sh:212 applies 600 to state files but not to ~/.claude/autonomous-dev.json
findings.recommendation: In config_validate.sh or config_show.sh, if a webhook URL is present in the config and the file mode is 0644 or wider, emit a warning.

findings.requirement: FR-408 — Exponential backoff on idle (base 30s, max 15m) with reset when new work arrives
findings.description: idle_backoff_sleep() doubles IDLE_BACKOFF_CURRENT each time no work is found, capped at IDLE_BACKOFF_MAX (900s = 15m). idle_backoff_reset() resets to base when work is found. This matches FR-408.

findings.requirement: FR-100 through FR-107 — Bash supervisor loop core
findings.description: The supervisor loop is implemented in bash (FR-100). Heartbeat written atomically each iteration (FR-103). Stale heartbeat detection with sleep/crash classification (FR-104). Circuit breaker after 3 consecutive non-zero exits (FR-105). Crash counter reset on success (FR-106). SIGTERM/SIGINT handled gracefully with child process escalation (FR-107).

findings.requirement: FR-109 — Lock file with PID validation
findings.description: acquire_lock() creates a PID-based lock file, validates that the existing PID is alive, removes stale locks, and exits if another live instance holds the lock.

findings.requirement: FR-205 — Atomic state file writes via .tmp + mv
findings.description: All state file writes in supervisor-loop.sh use write-to-.tmp then mv pattern. Examples include write_heartbeat(), save_crash_state(), update_request_state(), and advance_phase().

findings.requirement: FR-206 — Append-only events.jsonl audit log for every state transition
findings.description: Events are appended to events.jsonl for session_complete, session_error, retry_exhaustion, phase_advance, completed, and phase_failed event types. The format differs from the PRD schema (see FR-207 finding) but the append-only mechanism is correctly implemented.

findings.requirement: FR-400/FR-401 — install-daemon command, launchd plist with KeepAlive + RunAtLoad
findings.description: install-daemon.sh generates and installs launchd plist (macOS) and systemd unit (Linux). The plist template includes KeepAlive=true and RunAtLoad=true with stdout/stderr routed to ~/.autonomous-dev/logs/.
autonomous-dev.sh routes 'daemon start', 'daemon stop', and 'daemon status' subcommands. Start/stop wrap launchctl/systemctl. Status calls cmd_daemon_status().

findings.requirement: FR-500/FR-501 — Cost tracking per request and global cost ledger
findings.description: Session costs are parsed from claude JSON output and recorded in both state.json.cost_accrued_usd and the global cost-ledger.json with daily bucketing. The ledger includes per-session entries with request_id, cost_usd, and timestamp.

findings.requirement: FR-502 — Daily and monthly cost caps enforced before each iteration
findings.description: check_cost_caps() reads today's daily total and the current month's aggregate from the cost ledger and compares against DAILY_COST_CAP and MONTHLY_COST_CAP. check_gates() is called at the top of every iteration before work selection.

findings.requirement: FR-507 — Repository allowlist enforced at intake
findings.description: The allowlist is enforced at two levels: path_security.ts in the TypeScript intake layer checks that target repos are in the configured allowlist and throws SecurityError on violation; the supervisor loop's select_request() only scans repos in the allowlist.

config_show.sh implements four-layer merge (defaults, global, project, CLI) with source map. config_init.sh generates commented config with all sections (daemon, state_machine, governance, notifications, repositories, trust). Load_config() in supervisor-loop.sh merges defaults with user config.

cleanup_engine.sh implements archive_request() creating .tar.gz archives in ~/.autonomous-dev/archive/, worktree and remote branch cleanup, age-based retention, and the cleanup command supports --dry-run.

findings.requirement: FR-212 — schema_version field in state files
findings.description: schema_version: 1 is present in all new state files created by the intake layer. migrate-state-files.sh handles 1.0 -> 1.1 migration and warns on unknown schema versions.

findings.requirement: FR-213 — Validate state file integrity on read, transition to failed with state_corruption reason
findings.description: validate_state_file() attempts JSON validation, falls back to checkpoint.json, and if both are corrupt writes a minimal failed state with failure_reason: state_corruption and emits an alert.

title: PRD-002 Document Pipeline & Review Gates — Conformance Audit
summary: The core pipeline infrastructure (document types, frontmatter schema, review gate mechanics, versioning, decomposition, traceability, backward cascade, flow control, and audit logging) is substantially implemented and closely follows PRD-002. All P0 MVP requirements are present in code. Key gaps are: (1) the PRD template diverges from the required section list in §5.1.1 — it adds "Executive Summary" and "Scope and Constraints" but omits "Non-Goals", "Success Metrics", and "Open Questions"; (2) the TDD template omits "Trade-off Analysis" and "Integration Points" that §5.1.2 mandates; (3) the Plan template omits "Work Units", "Dependency Graph", "Agent Assignments", and "Parallel Execution Strategy" required by §5.1.3; (4) the Spec template omits "Code Patterns" and "Dependencies" required by §5.1.4; (5) FR-005 (operator-configurable templates via config file) is not implemented — customSectionsAllowed is hardcoded false in every non-CODE template and no config path exists to add/modify sections without code changes; (6) FR-063/FR-062 gap detection is defined and generates critical-severity gaps but the gapDetectionAtGates flag is never read by the review gate service — gaps are never injected as blocking review findings; (7) FR-064 code traceability with validated file:line references is not implemented; (8) FR-056 side-by-side version comparison API is not implemented; (9) the backward cascade config key require_human_confirmation exists in config_defaults.json but is absent from PipelineConfig type and is never read by the CascadeController; (10) NFR-006 content-addressable section deduplication is not implemented — each version stores its full content; (11) FR-021 CalibrationTracker exists as a class and is tested but is not wired into the ReviewGateService pipeline; (12) the Plan/Spec approval threshold in DEFAULT_REVIEW_GATE_CONFIGS is 85 but PRD Appendix D specifies 80 for Plan and Spec.

findings.description: The code PRD template (prd-template.ts) diverges from the §5.1.1 specification. The spec requires: Problem Statement, Goals, Non-Goals, User Stories, Functional Requirements, Non-Functional Requirements, Success Metrics, Risks & Mitigations, Open Questions. The code adds 'Executive Summary' and 'Scope and Constraints' (not in spec) but omits 'Non-Goals', 'Success Metrics', and 'Open Questions'.
findings.recommendation: Add Non-Goals, Success Metrics sections as required. Make Open Questions optional (matches PRD spec). Remove or fold 'Executive Summary' into Problem Statement or rename to match spec language. Rename 'Scope and Constraints' to 'Non-Goals' or add both.

findings.description: The TDD template omits 'Trade-off Analysis' (required) and 'Integration Points' (required) that §5.1.2 mandates. The template adds 'Error Handling', 'Deployment', 'Performance', 'Monitoring', 'Dependencies', 'Migration' which have no equivalent in the spec's TDD section list.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Trade-off Analysis' and 'Integration Points' as required sections. The extra sections are not disqualifying additions, but the two missing required sections need to be present.

findings.description: The Plan template uses 'Tasks' (instead of the spec's 'Work Units'), omits 'Dependency Graph', omits 'Agent Assignments', and omits 'Parallel Execution Strategy' (optional). The existing template has 'Objective', 'Scope', 'Tasks', 'Dependencies', 'Testing Strategy', 'Risks', 'Definition of Done' — these merge or rename several required sections but miss mandatory PRD-002 section IDs.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Work Units' (or rename 'Tasks' to match), 'Dependency Graph', and 'Agent Assignments' as required sections.

findings.description: The Spec template omits 'Code Patterns' (required by §5.1.4) and 'Dependencies' (required). It uses 'Implementation Details' instead of 'File Manifest' plus a separate 'Code Patterns' section. The 'Files to Create/Modify' section partially covers 'File Manifest'. 'Dependencies' is absent.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Code Patterns' and 'Dependencies' as required sections. Consider renaming 'Implementation Details' to 'Acceptance Criteria' alignment or ensure FR-002's required sections are all present.

findings.requirement: FR-005 — Operator-configurable templates without code changes
findings.description: FR-005 requires operators to add optional sections, modify section descriptions, and adjust rubric weights via configuration without changing plugin code. All five templates hardcode customSectionsAllowed=false (except CODE which allows custom sections). No config file path exists in config-loader.ts for template section customization. The PipelineConfig type has no template overrides field.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'templates' section to PipelineConfig and config.yaml that allows per-type section additions, description overrides, and rubric weight adjustments loaded at startup via config-loader.ts.

findings.requirement: FR-062 / FR-063 — Gap detection blocks review gate approval
findings.description: Gap detection code exists and correctly assigns 'critical' severity to gaps (gap-detector.ts:54). The config flag gapDetectionAtGates=true exists. However, the ReviewGateService never reads gapDetectionAtGates and never invokes detectGaps or injects gap findings as blocking critical findings into the review pipeline. The config flag is defined in PipelineConfig but is a dead reference — no caller reads it in the review-gate path.
findings.recommendation: Wire gapDetectionAtGates into ReviewGateService.submitForReview: after pre-review validation (Step 1), if the config flag is enabled, regenerate the traceability matrix and inject any gaps as critical:blocking findings before score aggregation.

findings.requirement: FR-064 — Code traceability with validated file:line references
findings.description: FR-064 (P1) requires code traceability to reference specific file paths and line ranges (e.g., src/pipeline/gate.ts:45-92) and validate that these references exist. The TraceabilityMatrix has a CODE-level chain entry (trace-types.ts) but there is no file path/line range field, no validation step, and no implementation of reference existence checks.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/traceability/trace-types.ts (no file_path or line_range fields in TraceChain entries), /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/traceability/matrix-regenerator.ts (no code reference validation)
findings.recommendation: Add file_path and line_range fields to the CODE-level TraceChain entry, populate them during CODE document creation, and add a validation step in matrix-regenerator.ts that checks the referenced files/lines exist.

findings.requirement: FR-056 — Side-by-side version comparison with score comparison
findings.description: FR-056 (P2) requires a version comparison API: given any two versions, produce a side-by-side diff with review score comparison. The VersioningEngine.computeDiff() method exists and computes section-level diffs. However, there is no side-by-side rendering, no score comparison between the two versions, and no public API that bundles both.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/versioning/versioning-engine.ts:84-94 (computeDiff exists but returns raw SectionDiff array, no score comparison)
findings.recommendation: Add a compareVersions() method to VersioningEngine that retrieves both diffs and review feedback scores for the two specified versions and returns a combined comparison result.

findings.requirement: FR-021 — Reviewer calibration tracking wired into pipeline
findings.description: CalibrationTracker is fully implemented with recordConfirmedFinding/recordMiss/getCalibrationRecord methods and is covered by tests. However it is never instantiated or called from ReviewGateService or any other pipeline component. It exists as an isolated module with no wire-up to the live review gate flow.
findings.recommendation: Inject CalibrationTracker into ReviewGateService and call recordMiss when an approved document later triggers a backward cascade, and recordConfirmedFinding when a reviewer's finding is confirmed downstream.

findings.requirement: FR-011 / Appendix D — Default approval thresholds: Plan=80, Spec=80
findings.description: PRD-002 Appendix D specifies approval thresholds: PRD=85, TDD=85, Plan=80, Spec=80, Code=85. The code DEFAULT_REVIEW_GATE_CONFIGS sets Plan=85 and Spec=85 (same as PRD/TDD), not 80 as specified.
findings.recommendation: Change PLAN and SPEC approvalThreshold to 80 in DEFAULT_REVIEW_GATE_CONFIGS to match the PRD specification.

findings.requirement: FR-073 (backward cascade scoping) — require_human_confirmation config not wired
findings.description: PRD §5.6 and Appendix D specify require_human_confirmation as a configurable backward cascade option (default false). The config_defaults.json has this field, but PipelineConfig type (config.ts) only has maxDepth and autoApproveUnaffected — require_human_confirmation is absent. The CascadeController never reads this flag. The config.yaml loader would silently drop it.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/config_defaults.json:186-189, /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/types/config.ts:32-35 (backwardCascade block has no require_human_confirmation field), /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/cascade/cascade-controller.ts (no require_human_confirmation read)
findings.recommendation: Add require_human_confirmation to the PipelineConfig.backwardCascade interface and read it in CascadeController.initiate() to conditionally emit a human_escalation event before proceeding.

findings.requirement: NFR-006 — Content-addressable section deduplication for storage efficiency
findings.description: NFR-006 (P1) requires unchanged sections across versions not be duplicated via content-addressable storage. The version writer stores full document content per version (v1.0.md, v1.1.md, etc.) with no section-level deduplication. The diff engine tracks which sections changed but the storage layer has no content-addressable mechanism.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/storage/version-writer.ts:57-91 (writes full content to each version file), /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/versioning/diff-engine.ts (diffs computed but not used for storage optimization)
findings.recommendation: This is a P1 requirement noted as aspirational in the PRD. Consider implementing section-level content hashing and storing only changed sections by reference for large documents, or explicitly document the trade-off.

findings.requirement: NFR-008 — backward_cascade_rate and smoke_test_pass_rate metrics not populated
findings.description: The PipelineAggregator computes most NFR-008 metrics but explicitly sets backward_cascade_rate=0 and smoke_test_pass_rate=0 with inline TODO comments noting they require external event data that is not yet wired.
findings.recommendation: Connect backward cascade events from the audit log (cascade_initiated events) and decomposition smoke test records to populate these metrics fields.

findings.requirement: FR-041 — Explosion warning alert to human operator
findings.description: FR-041 requires the system to alert the human operator before proceeding when decomposition would exceed 75% of the total node limit. The limits-checker detects explosionWarning correctly and the DecompositionResult includes the flag. However, no escalation event is emitted to the PipelineEventEmitter and no human notification path exists — the warning is returned to the caller but is not actively surfaced.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/pipeline/decomposition/decomposition-engine.ts:204-210 (explosionWarning returned), no human_escalation event emitted in decompose()
findings.recommendation: When explosionWarning is true, emit a human_escalation event via PipelineEventEmitter before creating children, so the operator gets a notification and can confirm before proceeding.

findings.requirement: FR-001, FR-003, FR-004 — Five document types with frontmatter and rubrics
findings.description: All five document types (PRD, TDD, PLAN, SPEC, CODE) are defined as an enum and PIPELINE_ORDER constant. DocumentFrontmatter includes all required fields (id, parent_id, type, status, version, author_agent, created_at, updated_at, traces_from). Quality rubrics are defined for all five types with weighted categories and per-category minimum thresholds.

findings.requirement: FR-006, FR-002 — Document validation before gate submission
findings.description: TemplateEngine.validateDocument() checks required sections present and non-empty, minimum word counts, guidance comments removed, and unknown sections. PreReviewValidator integrates this into the review gate (Step 1 of 21-step flow).

findings.requirement: FR-010 through FR-017 — Review gate mechanics
findings.description: ReviewGateService implements the full 21-step gate lifecycle: pre-review validation, rubric retrieval, panel assembly (configurable per type), blind scoring, disagreement detection (FR-020), approval evaluation, finding tracking, iteration control (max 3 per FR-017), quality regression detection (FR-054), trust level checking, and human escalation. Three gate outcomes (approved/changes_requested/rejected) are all produced.

findings.requirement: FR-030 through FR-037 — Decomposition engine with smoke test
findings.description: DecompositionEngine.decompose() validates parent approval, checks child type, enforces child limits (default 10) and total node limits (default 100), validates DAG, runs smoke test (coverage completeness + scope creep check), creates child documents, and writes decomposition records. Dependency graph edges are annotated with blocks/informs types.

findings.requirement: FR-050 through FR-055 — Document versioning with rollback
findings.description: Version calculator implements major.minor scheme with INITIAL/REVIEW_REVISION/BACKWARD_CASCADE/ROLLBACK reasons. VersioningEngine auto-computes and stores section-level diffs on non-initial versions. Rollback creates a new version (preserving audit trail) with the target version's content. Regression detection checks score delta against configurable margin.

findings.requirement: FR-060, FR-061 — Traceability matrix with forward chains
findings.description: Every document carries a traces_from field (required non-empty for depth > 0). Matrix regenerator builds full forward chains from PRD sections through TDD/PLAN/SPEC/CODE levels, writes a traceability.yaml file, and includes gap and orphan detection.

upstream_defect flag is present in Finding type and surfaced by reviewer prompt. CascadeController implements 9-step flow: validate target, scope affected documents, mark stale, re-open parent, resolve with re-evaluation. Depth limit enforced (default 2) with circuit breaker for repeated section cascades. Section-scoped cascade (FR-073) implemented via cascade-scoper.

findings.requirement: FR-080 through FR-083 — Pipeline flow control (no skipping, pause, cancel)
findings.description: progression-rules.ts enforces no-skip rule (parent must be approved before creating children), gate-required rule, and parallel sibling execution. pausePipeline/resumePipeline implement serializable pause state with ready-document identification on resume. cancelPipeline supports full and subtree modes with terminal state preservation.

findings.requirement: NFR-002 — Append-only audit log with hash chain
findings.description: AuditLogger writes JSONL events with SHA-256 hash chain linking each event to the previous. Integrity is verified on read. All 25 pipeline event types are defined and emitted through PipelineEventEmitter.

ReviewGateService supports crash recovery from three checkpoint stages (review_started, review_completed, decision) via IterationController. Atomic file writes (atomicWrite, atomicSymlink) prevent corrupt state on crashes. Parallel progress-tracker has crash recovery detection.

title: PRD-003 Agent Factory & Self-Improvement — Conformance Audit
summary: The Agent Factory infrastructure is substantially implemented: registry loading, integrity checking, metrics engine, anomaly detection, improvement lifecycle, A/B validation, canary runner, gap detection, rate limiting, promotion, rollback, and audit logging all have real code. Phase 1 through Phase 3 subsystems exist. However, the system has two critical regressions that prevent any agent from loading via the production code path, the runtime tool-enforcement hooks are not wired into actual agent execution, three CLI commands are unimplemented in the exposed entry point, FR-22 (automated new agent generation via LLM) is missing, and FR-29/30 (agent compatibility tracking) are config-only with no tracking logic.

findings.requirement: FR-01, FR-03, FR-35 — Agent registry loads only valid, schema-conformant agents with role-correct tools
findings.description: Every reviewer- and meta-role agent in agents/ carries a 'Write' tool in its frontmatter (added by commit 5725b5c 'fix(agents): add Write tool to author + reviewer frontmatter'). The validator's TOOL_ALLOWLIST explicitly forbids Write for reviewer and meta roles (validator.ts:33-34). Additionally, all 18 agents use models 'claude-opus-4-7' or 'claude-sonnet-4-6', which are absent from the DEFAULT_MODEL_REGISTRY (validator.ts:41-45 lists only the 20250514/20250414 model IDs). registry.load() does not pass a custom modelRegistry to the ValidationContext (registry.ts:166-168). Consequently, every agent fails RULE_005 or RULE_009 and would be rejected when registry.load() is called in production. tests/agent-factory/agents.test.ts confirms: 7 of 9 tests fail with 'RULE_005_TOOL_ALLOWLIST: Tool Write is not allowed' and 'RULE_009_MODEL_REGISTRY: Model claude-opus-4-7 is not in the approved model registry'.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/validator.ts:30-35 (TOOL_ALLOWLIST), plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/validator.ts:41-45 (DEFAULT_MODEL_REGISTRY), plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/registry.ts:166-168 (no modelRegistry in ctx), plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/quality-reviewer.md:tools includes Write, tests/agent-factory/agents.test.ts:7 failures
findings.recommendation: Either add Write to the reviewer/meta/author allowlists in TOOL_ALLOWLIST (and update the PRD's tool policy table), or remove Write from the reviewer/meta agent files. Update DEFAULT_MODEL_REGISTRY to include claude-opus-4-7 and claude-sonnet-4-6.

findings.requirement: FR-04 — Tool access enforced at runtime; FR-33 — No agent can modify agents/ directory
findings.description: ToolAccessEnforcer and PathFilter are fully implemented in runtime.ts (lines 49-147). The agents/ directory is listed as a PROTECTED_PATTERN (runtime.ts:34). However, AgentRuntime is never imported or instantiated in the daemon's execution pipeline (src/engine/, src/runner/ contain zero references to AgentRuntime or checkToolCall). Actual agent sessions are spawned via spawn-session.sh / Claude CLI directly, bypassing all runtime hooks. The enforcement code exists but has no effect on production invocations.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/runtime.ts:49,93,173, grep of src/engine/ and src/runner/ returns no hits for AgentRuntime or checkToolCall
findings.recommendation: Wire AgentRuntime.checkToolCall() as a pre-tool hook in the agent session spawner (spawn-session.sh or the Claude subprocess wrapper) so the enforcement is exercised in production.

findings.requirement: FR-11, FR-12, FR-19 — CLI commands: agent metrics, agent dashboard, agent rollback
findings.description: commandMetrics, commandDashboard, and commandRollback are fully implemented in src/agent-factory/cli.ts (lines 339, 425, 594). The public CLI entry point bin/agent-cli.ts only exposes: inspect, freeze, unfreeze, shadow, unshadow, promote, list (lines 186-302). The autonomous-dev.sh dispatcher routes 'agent' to agent-cli.ts exclusively (line 953). Consequently, 'autonomous-dev agent metrics', 'autonomous-dev agent dashboard', and 'autonomous-dev agent rollback' are unreachable from the command line despite being implemented. FR-20 (agent rollback) command is similarly absent.
findings.recommendation: Add metrics, dashboard, rollback, analyze, compare, reject, and gaps verbs to bin/agent-cli.ts's switch statement and inject the required dependencies (metricsEngine, rollbackManager, etc.).

findings.requirement: FR-22 — Dynamic agent creation: generate new agent definition using WebSearch and structural patterns
findings.description: FR-22 requires that when a domain gap is detected, the system SHALL 'generate a proposed new agent definition based on: the detected domain, best practices research (via WebSearch), and structural patterns from existing agents in the same archetype.' The DomainGapDetector (gaps/detector.ts) only logs gap records to domain-gaps.jsonl; it has no code to generate a new agent definition. The 'agent accept' command in cli.ts (line 1564) reads from data/proposed-agents/ but only as a placeholder: 'Note: Agent acceptance is a placeholder for PLAN-005-5 dynamic creation.' No LLM-based agent-definition generator exists in src/agent-factory/.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/gaps/detector.ts:123-204 (only logs gap records), plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/cli.ts:1608 ('placeholder for PLAN-005-5 dynamic creation'), grep for generateNewAgent/createSpecialist in src/ returns no results
findings.recommendation: Implement an AgentCreator class that, given a GapRecord, uses WebSearch to research best practices, selects the appropriate archetype template, and generates a new agent .md file for human review.

findings.description: PRD FR-19 explicitly states the rollback command 'reverts the agent file to its previous committed version via git revert'. The implementation in rollback.ts (lines 141-177) uses 'git show <commit>:<path>' to restore file content, writes it directly, then runs 'git add' and 'git commit' (lines 461-467) with message 'revert(agents): rollback...' — this is a forward commit, not a git revert. The distinction matters for auditability: git revert preserves the complete forward-and-revert history while this approach loses the intermediate versions in the revert path.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/rollback.ts:141-177,461-477 (git show + new commit), PRD FR-19 ('via git revert')
findings.recommendation: Either use 'git revert <commit>' to match the PRD, or update the PRD to document the forward-commit restore approach as intentional.

findings.requirement: FR-02 — Refuse to load agent files not in committed git state
findings.description: FR-02 requires refusing any agent file not in committed git state. integrity.ts implements a two-phase check (git status + SHA-256 comparison) but includes an explicit bypass: when tryGetGitRoot() returns null (non-git context, plugin cache, CI), the file is passed with diskHash only, skipping the committed-state check entirely (integrity.ts:184-189, batchGitStatus:240-242). The PRD security requirement is unconditional; the code degrades silently in headless environments.
findings.recommendation: Add a configuration flag to make non-git-context bypass opt-in rather than default, and log a warning when the bypass is triggered.

findings.requirement: FR-29, FR-30 — Agent compatibility tracking in data/agent-compatibility.json
findings.description: FR-29 requires tracking which versions of cooperating agents have been validated together in data/agent-compatibility.json; FR-30 requires verifying this before promotion. The config defines the file path at config.ts:91 ('compatibility': 'data/agent-compatibility.json') but no code reads from or writes to this file. The promoter.ts and orchestrator.ts have no compatibility-verification logic. These are P2 requirements but are entirely absent beyond the config path definition.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/src/agent-factory/config.ts:91 (path defined), grep of src/ for agentCompatibility/compatibilityStore/track.*compatible returns only config.ts and unrelated parallel/ files
findings.recommendation: Implement a CompatibilityTracker that records (agent, version, cooperating-agent, cooperating-version, validated-at) entries and is checked by the promoter before promotion.

findings.requirement: US-01, G2 — Human-readable dashboard exposing approval rate, quality score, trend (portal)
findings.description: The PRD goal G2 and user story US-01 require a dashboard visible through the portal. The CLI dashboard (FR-12) is implemented. The portal agents page (agents.tsx, wiring/agents-readers.ts) exposes only name, version, status (baseline/frozen/shadow), mode, and optional lastDispatchAt/runs30d/fpRate. The render.ts comments explicitly acknowledge 'none of the rich metrics the kit screenshot implies (runs30d, fpRate, lastDispatchAt) are persisted today' (render.ts:733). The approval_rate, avg_quality_score, and trend_direction from AggregateMetrics are not surfaced in the portal.
findings.recommendation: Wire the MetricsEngine.getAggregate() into the portal's agents-readers.ts and add approval_rate, avg_quality_score, and trend to AgentRow.

findings.description: FR-07 lists 'time to approval (wall clock)' as a required field. InvocationMetric has wall_clock_ms (overall execution time) and review_iteration_count, but no dedicated time_to_approval field tracking the elapsed time from first submission to final approval decision. wall_clock_ms covers execution time, not the full review cycle duration including human review wait time.
findings.recommendation: Add a time_to_approval_ms field to InvocationMetric to track wall-clock from first submission to final review outcome, distinct from wall_clock_ms.

findings.requirement: FR-01 — G1: 12+ foundation agents at launch
findings.description: 18 agent .md files are present in agents/: 4 authors, 3 executors, 9 reviewers, 2 meta. All 13 PRD-specified agents (prd-author, tdd-author, plan-author, spec-author, code-executor, test-executor, deploy-executor, quality-reviewer, security-reviewer, architecture-reviewer, doc-reviewer, agent-meta-reviewer, performance-analyst) are present, plus 5 additional reviewers. Catalog count satisfies G1. Note: all agents currently fail schema validation due to the model registry and Write tool issues (separate finding above).

findings.requirement: FR-06 — CLI command: agent inspect; FR-05 — CLI command: agent list
findings.description: Both commandList and commandInspect are implemented in cli.ts and exposed via agent-cli.ts and the autonomous-dev dispatcher. commandInspect shows full configuration, SHA-256 hash, state, file path, and first 5 lines of system prompt.

findings.requirement: FR-08, FR-09 — Aggregate metrics and anomaly detection
findings.description: MetricsAggregator computes approval_rate, avg_quality_score, trend (via linear regression), and domain_breakdown. AnomalyDetector implements 6 rules including ANOMALY_003 (review_iteration_count at or above p95 for 3 consecutive invocations), ANOMALY_001 (approval_rate below threshold), configurable thresholds, alert deduplication, and auto-resolution.

findings.requirement: FR-13 to FR-16 — Improvement lifecycle: observation threshold, weakness analysis, proposal generation, A/B validation
findings.description: ObservationTrigger checks 10-invocation threshold (configurable). PerformanceAnalyzer invokes the performance-analyst LLM agent and produces a WeaknessReport. ProposalGenerator enforces immutable field constraints before meta-review. ABValidationOrchestrator runs blind scoring via BlindRunner with input selection, scoring, and comparison. All wired through commandAnalyze in cli.ts.

CanaryStateManager (canary/state-manager.ts) manages canary period state with default 7-day duration (configurable). CanaryShadowRunner runs proposed version alongside current on every invocation, scores both outputs, and triggers CanaryExitEvaluator for promotion/rejection decisions including auto-rollback on catastrophic regression.

findings.requirement: FR-18, FR-24 — Human-approved promotion; rate limits
findings.description: Promoter writes new agent definition, commits to git with conventional commit messages, reloads registry. ModificationRateLimiter enforces per-agent weekly modification limits and agentCreationsPerWeek=1 (configurable). AutoPromoter restricts autonomous promotion to patch-level changes for low-risk agents only, with 24-hour override window and auto-rollback.

findings.requirement: FR-27, FR-28 — Semantic versioning; version_history field
findings.description: RULE_003_VALID_SEMVER validates semver in validator.ts. VersionClassifier in improvement/version-classifier.ts classifies changes as patch/minor/major. All 18 agents have version_history with initial 1.0.0 entry.

findings.requirement: FR-31, FR-34 — SHA-256 hash integrity check; append-only audit log
findings.description: integrity.ts performs two-phase check: git status porcelain + SHA-256 disk-vs-committed comparison with security alerts logged to stderr. AuditLogger (audit.ts) writes append-only JSONL to data/agent-audit.log, opens in append mode, never truncates, and silently continues on write failure.

findings.requirement: FR-32 — agent-meta-reviewer gate on every proposed modification
findings.description: commandAnalyze (cli.ts:1134-1210) invokes invokeMetaReview on every proposal before UNDER_REVIEW transition. Proposals for the agent-meta-reviewer itself bypass meta-review with explicit self-referential guard (cli.ts:1114-1131). Meta-reviewer checks for tool access escalation, scope creep, and prompt injection.

findings.requirement: FR-21, FR-26 — Domain gap detection; gap log at data/domain-gaps.json
findings.description: DomainGapDetector detects and logs gaps to data/domain-gaps.jsonl (not .json as the PRD specifies). Rate limiting (1 per domain per week) and fallback-agent assignment are implemented. However FR-22 (generating a proposed new agent definition) is not implemented (see separate finding).

title: PRD-004 Parallel Execution Engine — Conformance Audit
summary: The parallel execution engine has substantial, well-structured implementation covering all Phase 1 MVP capabilities and most Phase 2 features. Core modules are present: worktree lifecycle (WorktreeManager), DAG construction and scheduling (dag-constructor, scheduler), agent spawning (AgentSpawner), merge-back (MergeEngine), conflict classification and resolution (ConflictClassifier, ConflictResolver), progress tracking with stall detection, state persistence, and an event bus with NDJSON audit logging. Unit tests exist for every major module. However, several significant gaps and contradictions exist: (1) the disk threshold configuration defaults are inverted relative to PRD Appendix D, making the 'warning' pressure state unreachable with default settings; (2) the default max_tracks is 5 instead of the PRD-specified 3; (3) two production stubs (agent session creation, conflict resolution in MergeEngine) throw or return no-ops, making the engine non-functional end-to-end; (4) the FilesystemIsolationHook is implemented but never wired into AgentSpawner, leaving NFR-008 unenforced at runtime; (5) the FR-034 ConflictRecord type is defined but never instantiated or persisted; (6) the entire parallel engine module (src/parallel/) is not imported by any production pipeline code — the engine exists as an isolated library with no entry point connecting it to the daemon or agent dispatch; (7) FR-046 dashboard, FR-009 shallow worktrees, and several P2 Phase 3 requirements are entirely absent. The PRD status is 'Draft', which is consistent with the engine being work-in-progress rather than production-ready.

findings.description: No production code outside src/parallel/ imports WorktreeManager, Scheduler, AgentSpawner, or MergeEngine. The parallel execution engine is a standalone library with no integration point into the daemon, pipeline orchestrator, or agent dispatch. A grep for these classes across all non-parallel src/ files returns zero results. The code-executor agent (agents/code-executor.md) runs sequentially with no parallel worktree coordination.
findings.recommendation: Create a top-level ParallelExecutionEngine orchestrator that ties WorktreeManager + Scheduler + AgentSpawner + MergeEngine together, and wire it into the pipeline's execution phase when execution_mode='parallel'.

findings.requirement: FR-018/FR-020 — Agent spawning is a no-op stub
findings.description: AgentSpawner.spawnAgent() delegates to defaultCreateSubagentSession, which is explicitly a stub returning a fake session ID with a no-op terminate(). No Claude Code SDK integration exists. The engine cannot actually spawn subagents. Similarly, MergeEngine.resolveConflicts() is a protected method that unconditionally throws 'Conflict resolution not implemented. SPEC-006-4-2 provides the resolution pipeline.' Any merge encountering a conflict will fail immediately.
findings.recommendation: Connect defaultCreateSubagentSession to the Claude Code SDK subagent API. Wire ConflictResolver into MergeEngine via subclass or constructor injection.

findings.requirement: FR-007/FR-006/NFR-006 — Disk threshold defaults inverted from PRD
findings.description: PRD Appendix D specifies disk_warning_threshold=2 GB and disk_hard_limit=5 GB (warn first, then hard stop at higher value). The code has the opposite: disk_warning_threshold_gb=5, disk_hard_limit_gb=2. The config validator enforces disk_hard_limit_gb < disk_warning_threshold_gb, which is semantically backwards and makes the 'warning' DiskPressureLevel unreachable at runtime: critical fires at 2 GB before the warning threshold at 5 GB is ever crossed. The checkAndEmitDiskEvents logic (critical first, then warning) requires warning < critical to be reachable.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/config.ts:40-53 (DEFAULT_PARALLEL_CONFIG); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/config.ts:93-98 (validation enforces hard < warning); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/worktree-manager.ts:629-635 (critical fires before warning in logic)
findings.recommendation: Swap the defaults: disk_warning_threshold_gb=2, disk_hard_limit_gb=5. Invert the validation constraint to require disk_warning_threshold_gb < disk_hard_limit_gb.

findings.requirement: FR-004/NFR-008 — Filesystem isolation not enforced at runtime
findings.description: FilesystemIsolationHook is fully implemented with path validation, symlink resolution, and null-byte protection. However, it is never imported or instantiated by AgentSpawner. No code connects the hook to agent tool calls. The isolation exists as an untriggered class tested in isolation only.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/isolation-hook.ts (full implementation); no import of FilesystemIsolationHook in agent-spawner.ts (confirmed by grep returning no results)
findings.recommendation: Instantiate FilesystemIsolationHook in AgentSpawner.spawnAgent() and register it as a PostToolUse callback on the subagent session.

findings.description: PRD FR-014 and Appendix D specify default max_tracks=3. The code sets DEFAULT_PARALLEL_CONFIG.max_tracks=5. This is a direct contradiction of the PRD's stated default.
findings.recommendation: Change DEFAULT_PARALLEL_CONFIG.max_tracks to 3 to match PRD Appendix D.

findings.requirement: FR-034 — Conflict resolution log never written
findings.description: FR-034 requires a persistent conflict resolution log recording each conflict's type, resolution strategy, and outcome for learning and auditing. The ConflictRecord interface is defined in types.ts but is never instantiated, populated, or written to disk anywhere in the production code. The escalation report (EscalationReport) IS written to .autonomous-dev/conflicts/ but that is distinct from the resolution log.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/types.ts:276-288 (ConflictRecord defined but never used); grep for ConflictRecord in src/ returns only the type definition
findings.recommendation: Implement a ConflictResolutionLog class that appends ConflictRecord entries to a NDJSON file after each resolution attempt, covering auto, AI, and human outcomes.

findings.requirement: FR-002 — Worktree path 'track-' prefix not enforced
findings.description: PRD FR-002 specifies the naming convention '.worktrees/{request-id}/track-{name}' (with 'track-' prefix). The worktreePath() function in naming.ts generates '{worktreeRoot}/{requestId}/{trackName}' without any enforcement of the 'track-' prefix. If callers pass 'auth-service' as trackName, the path is '.worktrees/req-id/auth-service' not '.worktrees/req-id/track-auth-service'. The 'track-' prefix is only present in tests because test code uses names like 'track-a'.
findings.recommendation: Enforce the 'track-' prefix in the worktreePath() function or in createTrackWorktree() before building the path.

findings.requirement: FR-015 — Resource-aware scheduling only checks disk, not CPU/memory
findings.description: FR-015 requires resource-aware scheduling based on CPU, memory, and disk. The ResourceMonitor only checks disk pressure level and free disk space (via df -k). There is no CPU or memory monitoring. The scheduler throttles only based on disk pressure and max_tracks/max_worktrees limits.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/resource-monitor.ts:68-134 (only disk checks); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/scheduler.ts:266-288 (only disk pressure and worktree count)
findings.recommendation: Add CPU and memory checks to ResourceMonitor.canDispatch() and adjust parallelism accordingly.

findings.requirement: FR-025 — Agent execution logs (turns, tool calls, decisions)
findings.description: FR-025 requires capturing agent execution logs (turns, tool calls, decisions) for post-mortem analysis. The EventBus writes an NDJSON event log per request, and turn counts are tracked in TrackAssignment. However, there is no per-turn/per-tool-call log capturing the agent's individual actions. No log file path is tracked in AgentSpawner or passed to the subagent session.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/parallel/agent-spawner.ts (no logDir, no per-turn recording); EventBus only gets high-level lifecycle events, not per-turn tool calls
findings.recommendation: Pass a log file path to the subagent session so each tool call is appended there. Wire turn-level events through the EventBus or a separate audit log.

findings.requirement: FR-009 — Shallow/sparse worktrees for large repos
findings.description: FR-009 (P2) specifies using 'git worktree add --detach' with shallow depth for large repositories. The WorktreeManager always uses 'git worktree add {path} {branch}' without any depth or detach option. No shallow checkout support exists.
findings.recommendation: Add a configurable shallow_depth option; when set, use 'git worktree add --detach' with an appropriate depth argument.

findings.description: FR-046 (P2) requires a summary dashboard showing active request ID, DAG visualization, per-track status, disk usage, and elapsed/estimated time. No dashboard or CLI status command exists in the portal or plugin for the parallel engine.
findings.evidence: No dashboard code found in /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/ or any CLI command in the plugin
findings.recommendation: Implement as a portal route or CLI command using ProgressTracker.getRequestProgress() and WorktreeManager.checkDiskUsage().

findings.requirement: FR-001/FR-005 — Worktree create/remove via git worktree add/remove
findings.description: WorktreeManager.createTrackWorktree() uses 'git worktree add' and removeWorktree() uses 'git worktree remove', matching FR-001 and FR-005 exactly. Cleanup is force-capable. Idempotency is implemented. Orphan cleanup on startup is provided via cleanupOrphanedWorktrees().

findings.requirement: FR-010/FR-011/FR-012/FR-013/FR-016 — DAG construction, cycle detection, mixed-mode scheduling
findings.description: dag-constructor.ts implements full DAG construction with three dependency extraction strategies (explicit, file-overlap, interface-contract), Kahn's algorithm cycle detection with Tarjan SCC reporting, transitive reduction, cluster assignment, and critical path computation. The Scheduler processes clusters sequentially with parallel dispatch within each cluster.

findings.requirement: FR-021/FR-022/FR-023 — Turn budget enforcement and retry
findings.description: AgentSpawner.trackTurn() enforces turn budgets, terminates agents at 100% and emits warnings at 90%. defaultTurnBudget() implements small=30/medium=60/large=120 turn budgets. RetryHandler implements exactly-once retry with partial work detection, then escalation on second failure.

MergeEngine.mergeTrack() uses 'git merge --no-commit --no-ff', detects conflicted files, and delegates to resolveConflicts(). ConflictResolver.autoResolve() performs 3-way merge for NonOverlapping conflicts. DAG-topological merge ordering is implemented via computeMergeOrder(). Note: resolveConflicts() in MergeEngine is a throw-only stub (see separate finding).

findings.requirement: FR-030/FR-031 — Integration testing and failure attribution
findings.description: IntegrationTester runs full test suite via ShellTestRunner on the integration branch in a dedicated worktree. Failure attribution uses three git strategies: merge commit log for test file, source import tracing, and git blame for line numbers. RevisionLimitExceededError enforces circuit breaking.

findings.requirement: FR-032/FR-033 — AI conflict resolution and human escalation
findings.description: ConflictResolver.aiResolve() spawns a conflict resolution subagent with a structured prompt containing base/ours/theirs and both specs, enforces a confidence threshold (default 0.85), accepts the resolution if above threshold, and throws ConflictResolutionBelowThresholdError for escalation. escalateConflict() writes a structured EscalationReport JSON to .autonomous-dev/conflicts/. Note: the spawnConflictAgent factory defaults to a stub.

findings.requirement: FR-041/FR-042/FR-043/FR-044/FR-045 — Progress tracking, stall detection, ETA
findings.description: TrackStateMachine enforces all 9 track states with valid transition adjacency set. StallDetector monitors tracks with configurable stall_timeout_minutes, emits warnings at 1x and terminates at 2x. ProgressTracker calculates per-track and request-level progress with rolling-average ETA and periodic event emission.

findings.requirement: NFR-004/NFR-010/NFR-011 — Crash recovery and idempotent resume
findings.description: StatePersister writes state atomically via write-to-tmp-then-rename, detects corrupt state files, and lists in-flight requests. WorktreeManager.cleanupOrphanedWorktrees() reconciles git worktree list against persisted in-flight state on startup. TrackStateMachine.restore() reconstructs state from persisted data. idempotency is implemented in createTrackWorktree() and removeWorktree().

FR-036 (no shared mutable state) is enforced architecturally via worktree isolation. FR-037 (migration sequencing) is implemented in MergeEngine.validateMigrationSequence() and ContractValidator.validateMigrationSequence(). FR-038/FR-039 (interface contract validation) is implemented in ContractValidator.validateContracts() with type-definition, function-signature, and api-endpoint checks. FR-040 (shared types pre-commit) is implemented in preCommitSharedTypes().

Scheduler.createExecutionPlan() sorts tracks by priority descending within each cluster. DAG constructor assigns critical path bonus (+10) and out-degree bonus (+5 per dependent). This satisfies FR-017 (P2).

title: PRD-005 Production Intelligence Loop — Conformance Audit
summary:
The codebase has a substantial, well-structured implementation of PRD-005. The complete monitoring adapter layer (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenSearch, Sentry), PII/secret scrubbing pipeline, error detection engine, anomaly/trend/adoption analytics, deduplication, false-positive filtering, observation report generation, file-based triage processor, PRD generator, governance (cooldown, oscillation, effectiveness), retention policy, weekly digest, Slack/Discord notifications, and Phase 3 auto-promote are all present and implemented with real logic.

However, three functional requirements remain as acknowledged stubs or are missing entirely: (1) FR-004 — HTTP GET health endpoint probing for application up/down status is not implemented anywhere in src/; the service config schema has no health_endpoint field and no HTTP client calls it; (2) FR-009 — PagerDuty/OpsGenie read-only cross-reference is entirely absent; and (3) the two store-lookup functions used by cooldown and oscillation governance (`findRecentFixDeploymentFromStore` and `findObservationsByServiceAndErrorFromStore` in governance-integration.ts:203–244) are explicitly stubbed to return null/[] with comments saying "the actual scanning logic is provided by SPEC-007-5-1" — meaning cooldown and oscillation detection are wired but will never fire in production since lookups always return empty. FR-041 (access control) has no implementation. NFR-002 (<2 s per 10K lines scrub latency) is tracked but not enforced as a hard limit.

findings.description: The runner enforces scrubCollectedData unconditionally before analysis. On scrub failure, fields are replaced with [SCRUB_FAILED:...] tokens rather than passing raw text through. The invariant is correctly implemented.
findings.recommendation: No action needed — this is correctly implemented.

findings.requirement: FR-004 — Application health endpoint HTTP GET
findings.description: FR-004 (P1) requires connecting to application health endpoints via HTTP GET to determine basic service availability. No implementation exists: the ServiceConfigSchema has no health_endpoint field, no HTTP client probes any health URL, and the connectivity.ts probe set only covers Prometheus, Grafana, OpenSearch, Sentry. The PRD config schema example includes health_endpoint per service but the code schema does not.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/config/intelligence-config.schema.ts:18–26 (ServiceConfigSchema — no health_endpoint field); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/adapters/connectivity.ts:107–113 (buildProbeDescriptors — only four MCP sources)
findings.recommendation: Add health_endpoint?: string to ServiceConfigSchema, implement an HTTP GET probe in connectivity.ts, and surface the result as a DataSourceStatus in ConnectivityReport.

findings.requirement: FR-032 / FR-033 cooldown and oscillation governance — store lookups stubbed
findings.description: The governance integration wires cooldown (FR-032) and oscillation (FR-033) correctly at a type level, but both store-lookup functions — findRecentFixDeploymentFromStore (for cooldown) and findObservationsByServiceAndErrorFromStore (for oscillation) — are stubs that always return null/[] with a comment 'Stub: in production this scans the deployment store / observations store. The actual scanning logic is provided by SPEC-007-5-1'. As a result, cooldown_active will never be true and oscillation will never fire in production.
findings.recommendation: Replace the stubs with real file-scanning logic. observation-store.ts already has findObservationsByServiceAndError and findRecentFixDeployment that could be used directly.

findings.description: FR-009 (P2) requires read-only integration with PagerDuty/OpsGenie to cross-reference active incidents and suppress duplicate observations. No such adapter, config field, or logic exists anywhere in the codebase.
findings.evidence: grep across src/ for 'pagerduty|opsgenie' returns only test fixtures for an unrelated escalation feature, not the production intelligence module
findings.recommendation: This is P2 (Phase 3 scope) so skipping it is acceptable for Phase 1–2. However, the PRD's status claims should be updated to note it is not yet implemented.

findings.requirement: FR-041 — Access control for observation reports
findings.description: FR-041 (P1) requires observation reports to respect repository-level access controls when in a team environment, and to be excluded from public-facing output. No access control layer, permission check, or ACL enforcement exists in reports/report-generator.ts or the triage processor.
findings.evidence: No matches for 'access_control', 'acl', or 'repository.*access' in src/ outside unrelated modules
findings.recommendation: Acknowledge as Phase 2 scope. Add a note to the PRD and track as future work.

findings.description: The scrubbing pipeline records processing_time_ms and line counts per invocation, but imposes only a 30-second global timeout — not a per-10K-line budget. The NFR requirement of < 2s latency per 10K lines is not actively enforced; the system will not warn or throttle if a batch exceeds this.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/safety/scrub-pipeline.ts:386–420 (30s timeout only); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/safety/types.ts:175 (lineCount tracked but not budgeted)
findings.recommendation: Add a per-batch latency check in scrubCollectedData: warn via auditLog if processing_time_ms / (lineCount/10000) exceeds 2000.

findings.requirement: FR-034 — Full lifecycle tracking (observation → PRD → deployment → metric)
findings.description: FR-034 requires tracking the complete lifecycle: observation → triage → PRD (if promoted) → deployment (if completed) → post-deployment metric change. Observation-to-PRD linkage is implemented (linked_prd field updated). However, the deployment linkage (linked_deployment) depends on the stubbed findRecentFixDeploymentFromStore, so the PRD-to-deployment-to-metric chain is incomplete in practice.
findings.recommendation: Implement the deployment store scanning logic to complete the chain.

findings.requirement: FR-001/FR-002/FR-003 — Grafana, Prometheus, OpenSearch MCP connections (P0)
findings.description: Full adapter implementations exist for all three P0 monitoring sources with budget enforcement, timeout handling, graceful degradation, and connectivity validation.
Sentry adapter with listIssues, getIssueEvents, getReleaseHealth, PII scrubbing on events, and budget enforcement is implemented. Integration with the runner is wired via sentryAdapter option.
Schedule config supports both cron and interval (4h, 30m) expressions, with intervalToCron conversion. Default cron is 0 */4 * * * in the actual intelligence.yaml.
Services are individually configured in IntelligenceConfig.services; the runner filters by service name when scope != 'all'.

findings.requirement: FR-008 — MCP connectivity validation at startup
findings.description: validateConnectivity is called at runner init; results are stored in ConnectivityResult.results keyed by source name. Run aborts cleanly if all_unreachable.

findings.requirement: FR-010/FR-011 — Error type detection and severity classification
findings.description: Five error types (crash, exception, timeout, degraded, data inconsistency) and P0–P3 severity classification are implemented in the error detector and severity scorer.
Three-window deduplication engine with intra-run, inter-run (7 day), and post-triage (30 day) windows using fingerprint hashing.
Filter chain covers maintenance windows (recurring and one-time), excluded error patterns (regex), and load test markers.

findings.requirement: FR-015/FR-016 — Configurable error rate and duration thresholds
findings.description: ThresholdConfig schema supports error_rate_percent and sustained_duration_minutes with per-service overrides. Threshold check with sustained duration is implemented in error-detector.ts.

findings.requirement: FR-017 — Key usage metrics (p50/p95/p99, throughput, error rate, availability)
findings.description: All six standard metric queries are templated in PrometheusAdapter (error_rate, latency_p50/p95/p99, throughput, availability).
Anomaly detection with z-score and IQR methods, consecutive-runs requirement, and baseline comparison is implemented in anomaly-detector.ts. Integrated into ObservationRouter.
Trend analyzer with configurable windows and slope threshold is implemented and integrated in ObservationRouter.
Adoption tracker for newly deployed endpoints is implemented and integrated in ObservationRouter. Skipped during learning mode.
Baseline manager uses EWMA, enters learning_mode: true on first run, exits after 7 days AND 6+ runs. Anomaly/trend/adoption skipped during learning mode.

findings.requirement: FR-022/FR-023 — Observation report format and storage path
findings.description: Reports have full YAML frontmatter matching PRD spec (including confidence, cooldown_active, linked_prd, effectiveness, etc.) and are stored at .autonomous-dev/observations/YYYY/MM/OBS-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<short-id>.md.
Observations are not automatically fed to the pipeline. The triage processor requires triage_decision to be set by human (or auto-promote with safeguards) before executePromote is called.
Three-factor weighted confidence scoring (evidence strength 0.50, dedup quality 0.25, historical FP rate 0.25) is implemented.
Weekly digest generator aggregates observations, triage decisions, effectiveness results, recurring patterns, and recommendations. Stored at digests/DIGEST-YYYYWNN.md.

findings.requirement: FR-027/FR-028 — Human triage gate (promote/dismiss/defer/investigate) via file editing
findings.description: triage-processor.ts scans all observation files, detects PM Lead edits (triage_decision set, triage_status still pending), validates, and dispatches to promote/dismiss/defer/investigate action handlers. Deferred re-triage is also implemented.
Notification module supports Slack and Discord webhooks with health check, retry, rate-limit handling, and severity filtering.

findings.requirement: FR-030 — PRD generation from promoted observation with source: production-intelligence
findings.description: prd-generator.ts and prd-template.ts generate PRDs tagged with source: production-intelligence and observation_id. PRD ID uses PRD-OBS-<obs-id> format.
Append-only JSONL triage audit log at .autonomous-dev/logs/intelligence/triage-audit.log records all triage actions with actor, timestamp, reason, generated_prd, and auto_promoted flag.

Cooldown logic is correctly implemented in governance/cooldown.ts. However, the store lookup that provides the most recent fix deployment always returns null (stubbed), so cooldown_active will always be false in production.

Oscillation check logic is correctly implemented in governance/oscillation.ts and wired into the runner. However, the store lookup that retrieves recent observations always returns [] (stubbed), so oscillation can never be detected.

findings.requirement: FR-035/FR-036 — Effectiveness metric and aggregate rate
findings.description: Effectiveness evaluator compares 7-day pre/post Prometheus averages, classifies as improved/degraded/unchanged/pending, and writes back to observation frontmatter. Weekly digest includes aggregate effectiveness rate.

findings.requirement: FR-037/FR-038 — PII scrubbing and secret detection
findings.description: 11 PII patterns and 15+ secret patterns (including AWS, GitHub, GCP, Stripe, Slack, Bearer, Basic Auth, env var patterns, and Shannon entropy) are implemented with post-scrub validation and nuclear fallback.
applyRetentionPolicy archives after observation_days (default 90) and deletes from archive after archive_days (default 365), with exemptions for promoted observations with active PRDs.

findings.requirement: NFR-004 — Graceful degradation with partial monitoring
findings.description: MCP adapters return empty results (not errors) when their source is unreachable. withMcpRetry retries once then returns null. Observations proceed with available data; data_source_status is included in run metadata and observation frontmatter.

findings.requirement: NFR-006 — Zero write operations to production monitoring systems
findings.description: All MCP adapters use only read-type tool calls (prometheus_query, grafana_list_alerts, opensearch_search, sentry_list_issues). No write tool calls are invoked.
findings.evidence: All four adapters reviewed — callTool calls are exclusively query/list/search operations

findings.requirement: NFR-008 — Safe concurrent observation runs via lock manager
findings.description: LockManager creates advisory lock files per service. If a lock is held, the service is skipped with a warning. Stale locks (>60 min) are cleaned at run start.

findings.requirement: NFR-010 — Audit log all MCP queries, LLM invocations, file writes
findings.description: Run-level audit logger records MCP query counts per source, scrub events, and file write completions. LLM invocations are not individually logged (only token totals in run metadata). The audit log is stored at .autonomous-dev/logs/intelligence/RUN-<id>.log as required.

title: PRD-006 Intake & Communication Layer — Conformance Audit
summary: The intake and communication layer is substantially implemented. All three channel adapters (Claude App, Discord, Slack) exist and expose the required command vocabulary. Core subsystems — NLP parsing, ambiguity detection, prompt injection sanitization with externalized YAML rules, priority queue, starvation monitor, semantic duplicate detection, RBAC with hot-reload YAML, audit logging, rate limiting, notification engine with verbosity filtering, daily digest scheduler, conversation manager with timeout handling, and per-platform rich formatters — are all present in code. Five concrete gaps and one wrong default stand out: (1) the cancel handler never cleans up git artifacts (branches, worktrees, draft PRs) as FR-38 requires; (2) the status handler omits time-in-phase, overall elapsed time, and PR artifact links required by FR-36; (3) the list handler omits the requester and age columns required by FR-37; (4) the kill handler never sends a notification to all admins as FR-43 requires; (5) the FR-49 exponential backoff with jitter and max-5-retries described in discord_rate_limiter docs is not fully wired — only a single retry on 429 is coded in executeWithRateLimit; (6) the default human-response timeout is 1 hour (3600 s) not the 4-hour default mandated by FR-26.
