Additionally, the duplicate detector in SubmitHandler is wired with a stub EmbeddingRepository that always returns an empty list, making duplicate detection inert at runtime despite the subsystem existing.

findings.description: The cancel handler marks the request as cancelled and logs the event, but performs no cleanup of associated git worktrees, branches, or draft PRs. FR-38 requires deleting worktrees, closing draft PRs, and deleting feature branches (with confirmation before destructive ops).
findings.recommendation: Inject a git/GitHub cleanup service into CancelHandler and call it after the CONFIRM step. At minimum, emit a structured event (e.g. request_cancelled with cleanupRequested: true) so the pipeline core can handle it; currently IntakeEvent already has cleanupRequested on request_cancelled but CancelHandler hardcodes cleanupRequested: false — check event_types.ts.

findings.description: The /status response includes currentPhase, phaseProgress, blocker, and ageMs, but omits: (a) time in current phase (requires a phase_started_at timestamp), (b) overall elapsed time (separate from age), and (c) links to generated artifacts (prdPr, tddPr, codePr URLs). The doc comment says 'including artifact links' but the return object has none.
findings.recommendation: Add a phase_started_at column to the requests table (or derive from activity_log), compute timeInPhase = now - phase_started_at, and look up artifact URLs from the notification_deliveries or a dedicated artifacts table.

findings.description: The /list response omits the requester field and the age field that FR-37 mandates: 'request ID, title (truncated to 50 chars), priority, current phase, requester, and age'. The SQL query selects only request_id, title, priority, status, created_at, target_repo — no requester_id and no age computation.
findings.recommendation: Add requester_id to the SELECT and compute age (now - created_at) in the results mapping. Also title truncation to 50 chars is not enforced in the handler.

findings.description: The /kill handler requires admin role, prompts for confirmation, and pauses all active requests — but does NOT send a notification to all admins as the PRD requires ('send a notification to all admins'). The emitter fires a kill_all event but no in-process notification is dispatched.
findings.recommendation: After pausing all requests, look up all users with admin role from the AuthzEngine config and dispatch a notification to each via the NotificationEngine or directly via adapters.

findings.description: FR-26 requires a default human-response timeout of 4 hours. The TimeoutHandler hardcodes a default of 1 hour (timeout_seconds: 3600). The config key human_response_hours from intake-config.yaml (which would be 4) is not read at runtime; the handler falls back to 1 hour.
findings.recommendation: Change the default from 3600 to 14400, or — better — actually load intake-config.yaml in getTimeoutConfig() and read timeouts.human_response_hours.

findings.description: FR-49 requires exponential backoff with jitter, max 5 attempts, max 60 s for Discord and Slack API calls. The DiscordRateLimitHandler.executeWithRateLimit only retries once on 429 (no exponential backoff, no jitter, no attempt counter). The docstring describes a withRetry utility with '5 attempts, base 1s, max 60s, ±25% jitter' but that utility is never imported or called — only referenced in comments.
findings.recommendation: Implement a withRetry(fn, {maxAttempts:5, baseMs:1000, maxMs:60000, jitter:0.25}) helper and call it in executeWithRateLimit instead of the single retry. Do the same for Slack.

findings.description: The DuplicateDetector class is fully implemented with MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings and cosine similarity, and SubmitHandler calls detectDuplicate. However, SubmitHandler wires the EmbeddingRepository with a stub that always returns an empty array (getRequestEmbeddings returns []) and never calls storeRequestEmbedding, making duplicate detection inert at runtime — it will never find a duplicate because it never stores or retrieves embeddings.
findings.recommendation: Replace the inline stub with the real Repository instance (which implements getRequestEmbeddings and storeRequestEmbedding). Also call encodeToBuffer after a request is accepted and store the embedding.

findings.description: FR-14 requires '/list' to expose queue depth AND estimated wait time per priority level. The list handler returns totalCount and queueDepth (queued count), but does not compute or return estimated wait time per priority level. The digest scheduler returns queueDepthByPriority, but the list handler does not.
findings.recommendation: Add queueDepthByPriority and estimatedWaitByPriority (using StarvationMonitor / getAveragePipelineDuration) to the list response.

findings.description: Claude App adapter registers all 10 slash commands (autonomous-dev:submit, status, list, cancel, pause, resume, priority, logs, feedback, kill) with correct argument schemas.
Discord bot adapter is fully implemented with slash command registration, interaction handling, rich embeds, threaded prompts, and startup recovery.
Slack bot adapter is fully implemented with Socket Mode, slash command handling, Block Kit formatting, and graceful shutdown.
IntakeAdapter interface is defined with start(), sendMessage(), promptUser(), and shutdown() methods. All three channel adapters implement it.
Ambiguity detection checks confidence < 0.6, missing target_repo, and word count < 15 without technical terms. Generates up to 3 clarifying questions via Claude API.

Prompt injection sanitizer loads rules from injection-rules.yaml, supports block/flag/escape actions, and is externally configurable per NFR-06. Rules file exists at intake/config/injection-rules.yaml.
Request IDs are generated in REQ-NNNNNN format (6-digit zero-padded) via an atomic UPDATE...RETURNING on id_counter table.
Three priority queues (high/normal/low) with FIFO within tier using SQL ORDER BY CASE priority / created_at ASC ordering.
StarvationMonitor promotes low→normal and normal→high on 48-hour threshold, gated on last_promoted_at to prevent double-promotion.
SQLite database with WAL mode is enabled in migrator.ts (pragma journal_mode = WAL) and verified in schema.sql.
NotificationEngine.onPhaseTransition handles phase transitions and delivers to configured channels with retry.
Four verbosity levels (silent/summary/verbose/debug) are implemented in shouldNotify(); default is summary.
Discord rich embed formatter with color-coded status, Slack Block Kit formatter (3000-char limit enforced), and CLI formatter with ANSI codes are all implemented.
DigestScheduler generates daily digest at configurable HH:MM time with drift mitigation, covering active counts by state, blocked requests, completions last 24h, and queue depth.
ConversationManager enforces MAX_CLARIFICATION_ROUNDS = 5 and throws ClarificationLimitError on the 6th round.
AuthzEngine implements 4-role hierarchy (viewer/contributor/operator/admin) with ACTION_REQUIRED_ROLE matrix and AUTHOR_ALLOWED_ACTIONS special case.
Per-repository role overrides are supported in AuthzEngine via resolveEffectiveRole() using repo_permissions map from intake-auth.yaml.
Auth config is stored in intake-auth.yaml and hot-reloaded via fs.watchFile polling every 5 seconds without daemon restart.
All authorization decisions (grants and denials) are logged to authz_audit_log table and written as structured JSON to stdout.
Per-user rate limits: 10 submissions/hour (rolling window), 60 queries/minute, with role-based overrides from intake-auth.yaml.
Both Discord and Slack adapters implement startupRecovery() that queries getPendingPrompts() and re-sends any unanswered, unexpired prompts to the appropriate channels.
Typed discriminated union EventMap (IntakeEvent, PipelineEvent) forms the structured boundary between intake and pipeline core — no string concatenation at the boundary.

title: PRD-007 Escalation & Trust Framework — Conformance Audit
summary: The codebase has substantial, well-structured implementation of the PRD-007 escalation and trust framework. The core subsystems — trust resolution, gate matrix, escalation engine, escalation routing, human response handling, kill/pause/resume controls, append-only audit trail, and notification framework — are all present and reasonably complete. However, several gaps and one clear contradiction were found: (1) The Section 7 gate matrix for L2 Deployment Approval is contradicted in code (code says "human", PRD says "System"). (2) The trust scoring and promotion suggestion engine (FR-06, FR-07) is explicitly a Phase 3 stub with no active computation. (3) Auto-demotion (FR-09) has config structure but no active trigger logic wired into the trust engine. (4) The escalation message schema (FR-17) is missing the `what_was_attempted`, `recommendation` (with option_id/rationale), and `response_instructions` fields defined in the PRD schema. (5) Gate decision audit events (FR-43) lack reviewing-agent, score, verdict, and artifact-version fields. (6) Notification event type names (FR-48) diverge from the PRD specification — phase_transition, gate_approved, gate_rejected, error, and daily_digest are missing as typed event categories. Phase 3 requirements (FR-06/FR-07/FR-09/FR-26/FR-46 active mode) are correctly deferred and should not be flagged as failures given the phased delivery plan.

findings.requirement: FR-10 / Section 7 Gate Matrix — L2 Deployment Approval
findings.description: PRD Section 7 specifies that Deployment Approval at trust level L2 is handled by 'System' (autonomous). The code implements `deployment_approval: "human"` at L2, requiring human approval. This means L2 operators do not get the autonomous deployment behaviour the PRD promises.
findings.recommendation: Determine the intended behaviour from the TDD (the code references SPEC-009-1-4). If the TDD overrides the PRD for L2 deployment, update the PRD table. If the PRD is authoritative, change the matrix cell to `"system"` and update the test expected matrix at gate-matrix.test.ts:44.

findings.requirement: FR-06 — Per-repository delivery history tracking and trust scoring
findings.description: FR-06 (P1) requires the system to track per-repository success/failure counts, gate-pass rates, and escalation frequency, and use this data to compute a trust score. The TrustScore interface at types.ts:61 is explicitly annotated as a Phase 3 forward-compatible stub with a comment 'no scoring logic implemented yet'. No code computes or persists per-repo delivery history anywhere in the trust subsystem.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable deferral if Phase 3 scope is documented and understood. If Phase 1/2 is considered 'done', this P1 requirement needs a Phase 2 ticket. The config structures and type scaffolding are ready.

findings.requirement: FR-07 — Trust promotion suggestions with evidence
findings.description: FR-07 (P1) requires the system to emit a trust-level promotion suggestion to the PM Lead when a repository's trust score crosses the promotion threshold. No promotion suggestion emission or routing code exists; the promotion config (min_successful_runs, cooldown_hours) is parsed but never consumed by any engine.
findings.recommendation: Defer to Phase 3 if agreed. Add a promotion-suggestion engine that reads per-repo delivery history (FR-06) and emits a notification when the threshold is met. Wire to the notification framework as event type trust_promotion_suggested.

findings.requirement: FR-09 — Automatic trust demotion on consecutive gate failures
findings.description: FR-09 (P1) requires automatic trust demotion when consecutive gate failures exceed a configurable threshold. The TrustConfig type and TrustConfigLoader correctly parse and validate auto_demotion.enabled, auto_demotion.failure_threshold, and auto_demotion.window_hours, but the TrustEngine never reads these fields and no failure-counting or demotion-trigger logic exists anywhere in the trust subsystem.
findings.recommendation: Wire a failure counter into the gate-check path: on authority='human' verdict logged as failed, increment a per-repo counter. When counter >= failure_threshold within window_hours, call requestChange() to demote and reset the counter.

findings.requirement: FR-17 — Structured escalation message format (all required fields)
findings.description: FR-17 (P0) requires every escalation message to contain (a) type, (b) summary, (c) what_was_attempted, (d) failure reason, (e) options, (f) recommendation with option_id and rationale, (g) urgency, (h) response_instructions. The EscalationMessage interface and formatter implement type, summary, failure_reason, options, urgency, and artifacts, but are entirely missing `what_was_attempted` (list of approaches tried), `recommendation` (structured object with option_id + rationale), and `response_instructions` string. These were present in the PRD's Section 8 JSON schema.
findings.recommendation: Add what_was_attempted: string[], recommendation: { option_id: string; rationale: string } | undefined, and response_instructions: string to EscalationMessage. Populate what_was_attempted from the FailureContext (approaches tried before this escalation), and add a default recommendation in buildOptionsForType.

findings.requirement: FR-43 — Gate decision audit log with agent, score, verdict, artifact version
findings.description: FR-43 (P1) requires every gate decision to be logged with reviewing agent, score, verdict (pass/fail), human override if any, and artifact version reviewed. The gate_decision event emitted by TrustEngine only contains gate, authority, effectiveLevel, requestId, pendingChangeApplied, and securityOverrideRejected — no reviewing_agent, no score, no pass/fail verdict, no artifact_version.
findings.recommendation: Extend the checkGate() signature or add a logGateVerdict() method to accept reviewing_agent, score, verdict, and artifact_version fields from the pipeline orchestrator and include them in the gate_decision payload.

findings.requirement: FR-48 — Notification event types: phase_transition, gate_approved, gate_rejected, error, daily_digest
findings.description: FR-48 (P0) requires the system to emit structured notification payloads for: phase_transition, gate_approved, gate_rejected, escalation, error, completion, daily_digest. The code's NotificationEventType union uses a different vocabulary: 'gate_approval_needed' instead of gate_approved/gate_rejected, 'pipeline_completed' instead of completion, 'pipeline_failed' for errors. Missing as distinct types: phase_transition, gate_approved, gate_rejected, error, and daily_digest. This makes the payloads non-conformant with the published JSON schema NFR-07 requires.
findings.recommendation: Either add the PRD event types to NotificationEventType and map internal events to them, or update the PRD to reflect the implementation's vocabulary. If the PRD is the contract (NFR-07 requires a published JSON schema), the code must align. Add phase_transition, gate_approved, gate_rejected, error, and daily_digest as event types.

findings.requirement: FR-44 — Autonomous decision logging (what decided, alternatives, confidence, rationale)
findings.description: FR-44 (P1) requires every autonomous decision to be logged with: what was decided, alternatives considered, confidence level, and rationale. The AutonomousDecisionPayload type at audit/types.ts:59-65 defines these fields, and 'autonomous_decision' is a valid AuditEventType. However, no pipeline code actually emits autonomous_decision events — no call to auditTrail.append with event_type 'autonomous_decision' was found outside of tests.
findings.recommendation: Instrument the pipeline orchestrator and gate-execution paths to emit autonomous_decision events at each system-handled gate. Pass the AutonomousDecisionPayload fields from the reviewing agent's output.

findings.requirement: FR-26 — Office-hours routing (Slack during business hours, PagerDuty outside)
findings.description: FR-26 (P2) specifies office-hours-aware routing. No implementation was found. PagerDuty appears only as a channel string in test fixtures. This is a Phase 3 scope item per PRD Section 11.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable as Phase 3 deferral. Document this explicitly in the routing engine as a TODO.

findings.requirement: NFR-03 — Event log 100 events/second throughput without data loss
findings.description: NFR-03 (P0) requires the event log to sustain 100 events/second write throughput without data loss. No throughput benchmark or NFR test exists. The writer uses a synchronous spin-lock which may not sustain 100 events/second under concurrent load.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/src/audit/event-writer.ts:195-223 (sync spin-lock write path); no throughput test found in __tests__/
findings.recommendation: Add a throughput integration test that writes 100 events concurrently and verifies zero data loss. Measure actual latency under load given the spin-lock design.

findings.description: FR-32 (P2) delegate response is fully implemented despite being a Phase 2/3 item. The ResponseType union includes 'delegate', the validator handles it, pipeline-resumption.ts routes to a new target, and an escalation_resolved audit event with resolution 'delegated' is emitted.

findings.requirement: FR-46 — Audit trail integrity verification (hash chaining)
findings.description: FR-46 (P2) hash-chain integrity verification is fully implemented as an opt-in capability. SHA-256 hash chaining (hash-chain.ts), hash verification (hash-verifier.ts), and the GENESIS sentinel are all present. The AuditEventWriter accepts an optional HashChainComputer. findings.requirement: Section 7 Gate Matrix — design_review and implementation_plan gates absent
findings.description: The PRD Section 7 table includes 'Design Review' and 'Implementation Plan' as named pipeline gates. The code's PipelineGate type (trust/types.ts:22-29) does not include design_review or implementation_plan — instead it includes cost_approval and quality_gate, which are defined in the FR text but absent from the Section 7 table. The code appears to implement a different but internally consistent gate vocabulary drawn from the TDD rather than the PRD table. This is drift, not necessarily a bug, but the gate taxonomy is inconsistent with the PRD.

title: PRD-008 Conformance Audit: Unified Request Submission Packaging
summary: The majority of structural scaffolding (CLI dispatcher, command stubs, Discord/Slack service entry points, source metadata schema, package.json) is implemented. However, the critical two-phase commit handoff (FR-824a) is not used by the actual submit flow, the router is missing gate-action commands (FR-820), source is hardcoded to 'cli' regardless of channel (FR-829 partial), per-channel rate limit configuration keys from §8.1/§8.2/FR-837-839 are absent from config_defaults, the intake.channels.* enable/disable config block is undocumented in config_defaults.json, FR-823a server-side source assertion is unimplemented, FR-828 JSON schema validation is partial (source-only, not full state_v1 schema), the setup-wizard Phase 7 does not verify state.json file existence, and the autonomous-dev-assist new eval suites and skills (§11.1, §11.2) from PRD-008 are absent.

findings.description: The full handoff_manager.submitRequest() two-phase commit (temp write → SQLite transaction → atomic rename) exists at intake/core/handoff_manager.ts:496 and is wired through intake/router/request_submitter.ts, but the actual SubmitHandler (intake/handlers/submit_handler.ts) DOES NOT call it. Instead it imports from intake/lib/state_json_writer.ts and performs: db.insertRequest() at line 243 (SQLite first), then writeStateJson() at line 248 (filesystem second). This reverses the FR-824a ordering (temp write → SQLite commit → atomic rename). The handoff_manager code path is a dead letter in normal execution.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/handlers/submit_handler.ts:243-268 (SQLite-first, then state_json_writer); plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/core/handoff_manager.ts:496 (correct path, unused); plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/router/request_submitter.ts:83 (correct path, zero callers outside tests)
findings.recommendation: Replace the SQLite-first pattern in SubmitHandler.execute() with a call to submitFromRouter()/submitRequest() from handoff_manager, or wire the correct two-phase commit so the temp file is written before the SQLite INSERT is committed.

findings.description: FR-820 requires the IntakeRouter to support gate-action commands 'approve', 'request-changes', and 'reject' (for use by PRD-009's portal and future approval clients). The router's registerHandlers() at intake/core/intake_router.ts:181 registers exactly 10 handlers (submit/status/list/cancel/pause/resume/priority/logs/feedback/kill) and has no trace of approve, request-changes, or reject. These do exist in intake/cli/deploy_approve_command.ts and intake/cli/deploy_reject_command.ts but are not registered on IntakeRouter.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/core/intake_router.ts:181-202 (handler registration, no gate-action commands); plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/cli/deploy_approve_command.ts:167 (exists but separate CLI path, not via router)
findings.recommendation: Add ApproveHandler, RequestChangesHandler, and RejectHandler to IntakeRouter.registerHandlers() or expose them on a gate-action-specific router interface that satisfies FR-820.

findings.requirement: FR-829 — Source metadata populated per channel
findings.description: The SQLite 'source' column in RequestEntity is hardcoded to 'cli' at intake/handlers/submit_handler.ts:237, regardless of the actual channel (discord, slack, claude-app). The state.json file (state_json_writer.ts:160) correctly maps source_channel from the command, so the on-disk file gets the right channel. But the SQLite row always records 'cli'. This breaks FR-829's requirement for audit trails and channel-specific debugging in the canonical index.

FR-823a requires the router to reject any IncomingCommand whose declared source does not match the adapter's registered channel identity. No such check exists in IntakeRouter.route(). The router accepts any IncomingCommand.source.channelType without asserting it matches a registered adapter boundary.

Discord has a per-guild rate limit of 30/minute (consumeGuildBudget, BUCKET_CAPACITY=30) at discord_rate_limiter.ts:200, but FR-838 specifies 20 requests/hour per guild — a fundamentally different window. Slack has a per-workspace rate limiter in main.ts:97 (60-second window) but FR-839 specifies 50/hour per workspace. Neither matches the PRD specification. FR-837 (per-session rate limiting for Claude App, 5/hour) is not implemented at all; no session-level rate limiter exists.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/adapters/discord/discord_rate_limiter.ts:200 (BUCKET_CAPACITY=30, window=60s, not 20/hour); plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/adapters/slack/main.ts:182 (perWorkspacePerMinute=60, not 50/hour); no claude_app session rate limiter found

findings.requirement: FR-828 — JSON schema validation on state.json writes
findings.description: FR-828 requires state file writes to validate against the existing state_v1 schema (tests/fixtures/state_v1_intake.json). The state_validator.ts only validates 'source' and 'adapter_metadata' fields — it does not use AJV or any other JSON Schema validator to check required daemon fields (id, status, priority, created_at, title, description, target_repo, phase_history, etc.). The state_v1_intake.json fixture itself lacks source/adapter_metadata fields, confirming no full-schema validation is wired.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/state/state_validator.ts:87-141 (validates source/adapter_metadata only, no AJV import); plugins/autonomous-dev/tests/fixtures/state_v1_intake.json (no source or adapter_metadata fields in fixture)

findings.description: The PRD §8.1 specifies intake.channels.{cli,claude_app,discord,slack}.enabled config keys, plus token_env/application_id_env/guild_id_env for Discord and token_env/signing_secret_env/socket_mode/port for Slack. These keys do not appear in config_defaults.json. Discord service reads DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN directly from env; Slack reads SLACK_BOT_TOKEN directly. The config indirection (token_env config key pointing to env var name) is not implemented.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/config_defaults.json:250-261 (only generic intake keys, no channels block); plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/adapters/discord/main.ts:221-223 (direct env var access, no token_env indirection); plugins/autonomous-dev/intake/adapters/slack/main.ts:879-890 (direct env var access)

title: PRD-009 Web Control Plane — Conformance Audit
summary: The autonomous-dev-portal is substantially implemented — all nine pages are wired, SSE infrastructure exists, security layers (CSRF, CSP, audit-logger, path-validator, regex-sandbox, git-verifier) are present, and OAuth/Tailscale auth modes are coded. However, several P0 requirements are contradicted or missing: (1) FR-925 says all config mutations must go through the intake router, but the portal writes config files directly via atomicWriteJson; (2) FR-915 says gate actions must call the intake router, but the gate-store writes marker files directly instead; (3) FR-903 requires 5-second SSE auto-update for the dashboard, but the dashboard polls every 10 seconds; (4) FR-S33 mandates Referrer-Policy: same-origin but the implementation uses strict-origin-when-cross-origin; (5) FR-929 (12-month bar chart), FR-932 (top-10 expensive requests), FR-904 (three repo sort modes), and FR-938 (production intelligence status) are not implemented. The audit verify CLI tool (FR-S41) is also absent.

findings.description: FR-925 requires all configuration mutations to go through the intake router (a config-set command) and explicitly states the portal SHALL NOT write configuration files directly. However, the portal writes config files directly via atomicWriteJson in settings-store.tsx.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/settings-store.tsx:250 — `await atomicWriteJson(this.path, next)`. The IntakeRouterClient exists with a config-set command type but is never called from settings-store. IntakeCommand.command has 'config-set' defined at intake-router-client.ts:27 but the store bypasses it.
findings.recommendation: Replace the direct atomicWriteJson calls in FileSettingsStore.saveFromForm() and addAllowlist() with IntakeRouterClient.submitCommand({command: 'config-set', ...}) calls. Validate server-side on the intake router side.

findings.description: FR-915 requires gate action submissions to issue intake router commands (approve/request-changes/reject per PRD-008 FR-820) and carry source: 'portal'. Instead, gate-store.tsx writes marker files directly to the daemon state directory, bypassing the intake router entirely.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/gate-store.tsx:1-13 — 'No daemon-RPC channel exists. The portal writes a per-id marker file'. The applyGateDecision function calls atomicWriteJson(path, marker) at line ~98, never calling IntakeRouterClient.submitCommand. IntakeRouterClient.submitCommand() is only used by operations-handlers.ts for kill-switch/circuit-breaker ops.
findings.recommendation: Wire gate-store.tsx to call IntakeRouterClient.submitCommand with command:'approve'/'request-changes'/'reject' and targetRequestId set, carrying source:'portal' and the operator identity. The marker-file fallback may be kept as a secondary mechanism if the daemon is unreachable, but the intake router must be the primary path.

findings.description: FR-903 requires the homepage to auto-update via SSE (or polling) every 5 seconds. The dashboard template hard-codes a 10-second polling interval via HTMX.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/views/dashboard.tsx:39 — `const DASHBOARD_POLLING_TRIGGER = 'every 10s [document.visibilityState === "visible"]'`. The SSE infrastructure exists at /portal/events but the dashboard comment at line 185-186 acknowledges 'SSE infrastructure exists at /portal/events but no template subscribes yet'.
findings.recommendation: Either subscribe the dashboard to SSE events and trigger fragment swaps on SSE messages, or reduce the polling interval to 5 seconds to match FR-903. The configurable sse_update_interval_seconds userConfig key (default 5) should drive this value.

findings.description: FR-S33 mandates Referrer-Policy: same-origin on all responses. The implementation uses strict-origin-when-cross-origin as the default.
findings.recommendation: Change the DEFAULT_SECURITY_HEADERS_CONFIG.referrerPolicy to 'same-origin' to match FR-S33.

findings.description: FR-929 requires a monthly spend time series for the last 12 months rendered as an SVG bar chart. The costs page only renders a daily spend SVG line chart for the last 30 days (CostChart). No monthly bar chart exists.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/views/costs.tsx:133-143 — only a '30-day daily spend' line chart (CostChart component). No monthly bar chart component is imported or rendered. The server/charts/ directory has stacked_bar_chart.ts but it is not wired to the costs page.
findings.recommendation: Add a MonthlySpendChart component using the existing stacked_bar_chart.ts, wire it to a 12-month cost series from readCostsData(), and add it to the costs view between the daily chart and the projection section.

findings.description: FR-932 requires the cost analysis page to display the top-10 most expensive requests of all time with request ID, title, total cost, completion date, and repository. No such table exists in the costs view.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/views/costs.tsx — no top-10 requests table. Searching for 'top.*10', 'most.*expensive', 'top_10' returns no results in cost-related server files.
findings.recommendation: Add a top-10 expensive requests table to the costs view, deriving data from the request ledger reader, sorted by total cost descending.

findings.description: FR-904 requires three dashboard sort modes: alphabetical (default), last activity descending, and needs-attention-first. The dashboard route does not read a sort query parameter, and no sort controls exist in the dashboard view.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/routes/dashboard.ts:78-86 — dashboardHandler reads no sort query param; readDashboardData() returns repos in a fixed order. No sort UI or HTMX trigger is present in dashboard.tsx.
findings.recommendation: Add a ?sort= query parameter to the dashboard route, implement sort logic in readDashboardData() or computeDashboardAggregates(), and add sort control buttons to the dashboard view.

findings.description: FR-938 requires the operations page to show production intelligence cycle status: last observation run timestamp, next scheduled run, and observation count from the last cycle. The readOpsHealth() function does not include this data.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/ops-readers.ts:111-143 — readOpsHealth() returns no production intelligence fields. The OpsHealth type at render.ts:642 has circuitBreaker?: CircuitBreakerState but no intelligence cycle fields. The ops view at ops.tsx also shows no intelligence section.
findings.recommendation: Add production intelligence state reading to readOpsHealth() from the daemon's state files, expose it on OpsHealth type, and render it in the ops view.

findings.description: FR-935 requires the ops page to show circuit breaker state and provide a reset button. The reset route exists (POST /ops/circuit-breaker/reset via operations-routes.ts), but the ops reader does not populate circuitBreaker state — readOpsHealth() returns no circuitBreaker field, so the UI renders an EmptyState.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/ops-readers.ts:128-143 — the returned object has no circuitBreaker field. ops.tsx:93 checks `const cb = health.circuitBreaker` and falls back to EmptyState. The reset POST route exists at operations-routes.ts:122.
findings.recommendation: Read circuit breaker state from the daemon state files in readOpsHealth() and populate the circuitBreaker field on OpsHealth.

findings.description: FR-918 requires the approval queue to be sortable by waiting time (default), repository name, phase type, and requester. The queue is only sorted by waitedMin descending (a single hardcoded sort). No sort controls or multi-mode sorting exist.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/fragments/approval-queue.tsx:5 — 'sorted by waitedMin desc (longest wait first).' No sort control buttons in approvals.tsx; the segmented filter is by gate type, not a sort.
findings.recommendation: Add sort controls to the approvals page and implement multi-mode sorting in the approvals reader.

findings.description: FR-919 requires the approval queue to be filterable by repository AND phase type using dropdown controls. The current filter is a segmented button group filtering only by gate type (reviewer-chain/standards-violation/cost-cap), not by repository. No repository dropdown exists.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/views/approvals.tsx:86-118 — segmented filter has All/Reviewer/Standards/Cost buttons only; no repository filter dropdown.
findings.recommendation: Add a repository dropdown filter to the approvals view, pass the filter to the approvals reader, and render filtered results.

findings.description: FR-930 requires a per-repository cost breakdown table for the current month (repo name, total spend, request count, avg cost/request). The cost view has no per-repo table; only phase spend and reviewer spend tables are shown.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/views/costs.tsx:153-215 — the 2-col grid shows PhaseSpendTable and reviewer-spend only. No per-repo table. The repo-aggregation-reader.ts exists but is not used in costs.
findings.recommendation: Add a per-repo cost breakdown table to the costs view using data from repo-aggregation-reader.ts.

findings.description: FR-941 requires log filters for level, request ID (exact match), and time range (last 1h/4h/24h). The logs route only reads the level query param; request ID and time range filters are not wired in the logs route.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/routes/logs.ts:18-27 — only `level` and `limit` query params are read. The LogsReadOptions interface has `since` but no requestId field. The log filter infrastructure in server/logs/filter.ts supports time_range but it is not called from the logs route.
findings.recommendation: Add requestId and timeRange query params to the logs route handler and wire them through to the filter infrastructure.

findings.description: FR-942 requires a download button on the logs page to retrieve the last 24 hours of logs as a gzipped text file. No download endpoint exists in the logs route.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/routes/logs.ts — no GET /logs/download endpoint. The logs parser notes it is 'for the gzip download endpoint' (parser.ts:6) but no route implements it.
findings.recommendation: Add a GET /logs/download route that streams daemon.log filtered to the last 24 hours as a gzip-compressed text file with Content-Disposition: attachment.

findings.description: FR-S41 requires a `autonomous-dev-portal audit verify` CLI tool to verify HMAC chain tampering detection. The HMAC chain implementation (hmac-chain.ts, audit-logger.ts) and a StaticAuditChainVerifier class exist, but no standalone CLI verify command is found.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/services/audit-log-reader.ts:37 — StaticAuditChainVerifier exists. No /bin/ or /scripts/ CLI tool named 'audit-verify' or 'audit verify' found. The spec explicitly requires a CLI tool for tamper detection.
findings.recommendation: Create a bin/audit-verify.ts (or similar) that accepts the audit.jsonl path, reads the chain, and verifies each entry's HMAC against its predecessor, reporting any gaps or mismatches.

findings.description: FR-S32 specifies CSP `style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'`. The production CSP includes 'unsafe-inline' on style-src by default (matching FR-S32). However, this is only enforced in production mode; in development the entire CSP is report-only. More importantly, FR-S32 specifies a strict script-src 'self' with no unsafe-eval — this is met. The PRD specifies `img-src 'self' data:` which matches PRODUCTION_DIRECTIVES_BASE. Conformant on the directive content, but only enforced (not report-only) in production.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/csp-config.ts:68-79 — PRODUCTION_DIRECTIVES_BASE. csp-config.ts:101-110 — defaultCSPConfig returns reportOnly:true for development, which means CSP is not enforced locally, only reported.
findings.recommendation: This is a low-risk partial: the production config is correct. Consider documenting that the report-only mode in development means local testing does not enforce CSP violations.

findings.description: FR-916 requires that after a gate action submission, the page shows a confirmation message and automatically redirects to the approval queue after 3 seconds. The gate action handler returns an HTML fragment (the gate banner) but does not include a 3-second redirect.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/routes/gate-and-request-actions.tsx:149-158 — returns c.html(result.fragment) on success. No HX-Redirect header or meta-refresh is sent. gate-store.tsx:50-61 renders a gateBannerFragment with no redirect logic.
findings.recommendation: Add an HX-Redirect header to the 200 response pointing to /approvals, triggered after a short delay, or include an HTMX hx-trigger with delay in the response fragment.

findings.description: NFR-04 requires a stale data banner when the daemon is stopped. The DegradationBanner component and DaemonHealthMonitor exist. This is implemented.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/fragments/degradation-banner.tsx — DegradationBanner renders warning/error banners. /server/health/daemon-health-monitor.ts handles polling and status transitions. /server/events/types.ts includes 'daemon-down' event type.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.description: CSRF protection with Origin header validation (FR-S10) and per-session CSRF token (FR-S11) is fully implemented via CSRFProtection class and csrfMiddleware factory.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/csrf-protection.ts — OriginValidator validates Origin/Referer (FR-S10); CSRFProtection implements HMAC-signed double-submit cookie token (FR-S11). Middleware registered in middleware/index.ts.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.description: Path canonicalization via realpath() and git repository verification are implemented.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/path-validator.ts — PathValidator uses fs.realpath() and checks canonical path against allowed_roots. /server/security/git-verifier.ts — GitVerifier runs git rev-parse via execFile (no shell) with 2s timeout.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.description: RegexSandbox runs user-supplied regex in a worker thread with 100ms timeout, defending against ReDoS. Input is capped at 10KB, pattern at 512 bytes.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/regex-sandbox.ts:57-84 — pre-flight size checks; worker thread with 100ms timeout enforced via worker.terminate().
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.description: Markdown sanitization pipeline using marked + DOMPurify is implemented with post-sanitization scans for URL-scheme bypass payloads.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/sanitization-pipeline.ts — MarkdownSanitizationPipeline (marked render → DOMPurify sanitize → post-scan). Dependencies pinned: marked ~5.1.2, dompurify ~3.0.5 per package.json:22-25.

title: PRD-010 GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline — Conformance Audit
summary: The core CI infrastructure (ci.yml, security-review.yml, release.yml, budget-gate.yml, four document-review workflows, agent-meta-review.yml, claude-assistant.yml, assist-evals.yml) is implemented and represents substantial progress through Phases 1–4. Most P0 structural requirements are met. However, 11 distinct gaps and contradictions were found: the observe.yml.example file is missing entirely (FR-9001–FR-9006); the prd-reviewer.md and tdd-reviewer.md agent files referenced by prd-review.yml and tdd-review.yml do not exist on disk (document review workflows will fail at runtime); claude-assistant.yml uses --max-turns 5 and omits the model flag instead of --max-turns 10 --model claude-opus-4-7 (FR-3004 contradicted); claude-assistant.yml has no job-level timeout-minutes (FR-3007 missing); assist-evals.yml does not trigger on v* tag pushes or workflow_dispatch (FR-2001 partial); assist-evals.yml does not differentiate release-mode (hard-fail) vs PR-mode (advisory) thresholds (FR-2003 partial); FR-2006 (portal security cases at 100%) is not enforced; the plugin schema omits license from the required[] array despite PRD FR-7002 requiring it; CLAUDE_CLI_VERSION defaults to latest rather than the PRD-specified 2.x pin; ci.yml does not call ci.yml via workflow_call in release.yml (FR-8002 missing); and the five new autonomous-dev-assist skills (github-actions-setup, ci-troubleshoot, release-workflow-guide, ci-budget-guide, ci-migration-guide) from PRD §11 are not present.

findings.requirement: FR-4003/FR-4004/FR-4005 — prd-review.yml and tdd-review.yml reference nonexistent agent files
findings.description: prd-review.yml references prompt-template-path: plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/prd-reviewer.md and tdd-review.yml references plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/tdd-reviewer.md. Neither file exists in the agents/ directory (only doc-reviewer.md, quality-reviewer.md, agent-meta-reviewer.md, etc. are present). The document-review composite action will fail the 'Load reviewer prompt' step with '::error::prompt-template-path ... does not exist', causing both required status checks (docs/prd-review and docs/tdd-review) to fail on every PR touching those documents.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/prd-review.yml:81, .github/workflows/tdd-review.yml:83 — referenced paths plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/prd-reviewer.md and plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/tdd-reviewer.md verified absent via find
findings.recommendation: Create prd-reviewer.md and tdd-reviewer.md in plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/ with appropriate reviewer prompts, or change the prompt-template-path to an existing agent such as doc-reviewer.md (as plan-review.yml and spec-review.yml already do).

findings.description: The PRD requires .github/workflows/observe.yml.example (shipped inactive, operators rename to activate). No such file exists anywhere under .github/. The entire §5.9 scheduled observation workflow (FR-9001 through FR-9006) is unimplemented.
findings.recommendation: Create .github/workflows/observe.yml.example with the schedule/workflow_dispatch triggers, run command, webhook post, 15-minute timeout, and non-cancellable concurrency per FR-9002–FR-9006.

findings.description: claude-assistant.yml sets claude_args: '--max-turns 5' and omits the model entirely. The PRD explicitly requires --max-turns 10 --model claude-opus-4-7 as a cost/latency cap. Both the turn limit (5 vs 10) and the model specification contradict the PRD.
findings.recommendation: Change claude_args to '--max-turns 10 --model claude-opus-4-7' to match FR-3004.

findings.requirement: FR-3007 — claude-assistant.yml job SHALL have timeout-minutes: 10
findings.description: Neither the respond job nor any job in claude-assistant.yml sets timeout-minutes. Without this, a runaway Claude invocation has no workflow-level time cap, contradicting the cost-control intent of FR-3007 and NFR-1009.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/claude-assistant.yml — grep for timeout-minutes returned no output
findings.recommendation: Add timeout-minutes: 10 to the respond job.

findings.requirement: FR-2001 — assist-evals SHALL trigger on push of v* tags and workflow_dispatch
findings.description: assist-evals.yml only triggers on pull_request (paths filter on assist files) and a nightly schedule cron. It does not trigger on push of tags matching v* (release mode) and does not include workflow_dispatch. This means the release workflow (release.yml) cannot invoke assist-evals to enforce release-mode thresholds.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/assist-evals.yml:18-27 — on: block has only pull_request and schedule triggers
findings.recommendation: Add on.push.tags: ['v*'] and on.workflow_dispatch to assist-evals.yml, and implement release-mode vs PR-mode threshold differentiation.

findings.requirement: FR-2003 — release mode SHALL fail on threshold breach; PR mode SHALL warn only
findings.description: check-eval-threshold.sh applies the same hard-fail logic regardless of event context (PR vs tag push). There is no conditional logic distinguishing release mode (hard fail) from PR advisory mode (warn but pass). FR-2003 requires PR-triggered runs to post a warning without blocking merge.
findings.evidence: scripts/ci/check-eval-threshold.sh — exits 1 on any breach with no event_name conditional
findings.recommendation: Add a github.event_name check: for pull_request, emit ::warning:: and exit 0; for push to tags, exit 1 on breach.

findings.requirement: FR-8002 — release.yml SHALL call ci.yml via workflow_call
findings.description: release.yml does not call ci.yml as a reusable workflow. The PRD requires that all baseline CI checks (typecheck, lint, test, shell, plugin-validate) pass before any release step runs. The current release.yml only runs budget-gate, verify-version, verify-evals, generate-changelog, and create-release.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/release.yml — no uses: ./.github/workflows/ci.yml reference present; ci.yml has no workflow_call trigger
findings.recommendation: Add workflow_call to ci.yml's on: block, and add a job in release.yml that calls ci.yml and gates the rest of the pipeline on its success.

findings.requirement: FR-2006 — portal security cases SHALL pass at 100% or release is blocked
findings.description: No step in assist-evals.yml or check-eval-threshold.sh enforces the PRD-009 §13.7 rule that portal security cases (CSRF, XSS, path traversal, ReDoS) must pass at 100%. The current eval results are aggregated as a single overall pass_rate with no per-suite or security-subset enforcement.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/assist-evals.yml and scripts/ci/check-eval-threshold.sh — no security-suite or per-case 100% logic present
findings.recommendation: Extend check-eval-threshold.sh and the eval results schema to include per-suite scores and a security-suite field; add a separate check that fails unconditionally if security-suite < 1.0.

findings.requirement: FR-7002 — plugin.json SHALL fail if license field is missing
findings.description: The plugin.schema.json at .github/schemas/plugin.schema.json defines license as an optional property — it is present in properties but absent from the required[] array (which only lists name, version, description, author). FR-7002 explicitly requires that license be a required field for manifest validation to pass.
findings.evidence: .github/schemas/plugin.schema.json:8 — required: ["name", "version", "description", "author"] — license is absent from required
findings.recommendation: Add "license" to the required[] array in plugin.schema.json.

findings.requirement: FR-1008 — CLAUDE_CLI_VERSION default SHALL be pinned to 2.x
findings.description: The PRD specifies the CLAUDE_CLI_VERSION workflow env var shall default to 2.x (pinned major). The actual value in ci.yml is latest, which floats across all major versions and may break the plugin-validate job on a major Claude CLI release.
findings.recommendation: Change to CLAUDE_CLI_VERSION: "@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2" or the appropriate 2.x NPM tag.

findings.requirement: FR-1007 — markdown job SHALL run markdownlint on plugins/*/docs/**/*.md
findings.description: The markdown job in ci.yml runs only lychee link checking. The DavidAnson/markdownlint-cli2-action (listed in the dependency pins table §9) is absent — there is no markdownlint step in the workflow. Only link checking is performed, leaving markdown style violations uncaught.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:79-100 — markdown job contains only lycheeverse/lychee-action; no markdownlint step
findings.recommendation: Add a step using DavidAnson/markdownlint-cli2-action@v18 targeting plugins/*/docs/**/*.md.

findings.requirement: FR-1006 — shell job SHALL run shfmt -d on bin/*.sh
findings.description: The shell job runs shellcheck on bin/*.sh and installers/*.sh but has no shfmt -d step. FR-1006 explicitly requires both shellcheck and shfmt format checking.
findings.recommendation: Add a shfmt -d step after shellcheck in the shell job.

findings.description: security-review.yml has no claude-code-security-review step. Only gitleaks and trufflehog are run. The claude-code-security-review action is a P1 requirement in the PRD but is completely absent.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/security-review.yml — grep for claude-code-security-review returned no output
findings.recommendation: Add a job using anthropics/claude-code-security-review@v1 with timeout-minutes: 20 and SARIF output.

findings.description: The PRD's dependency pin table specifies trufflehog/trufflehog-actions-scan@v3, but the actual action used is trufflesecurity/trufflehog@v3.63.2 (a pinned-SHA variant of the direct binary). These are different action packages with different interfaces.
findings.recommendation: This deviation may be intentional (trufflesecurity/trufflehog is the maintained repo); document the deviation or update the PRD dependency pin table to reflect the actual action.

findings.requirement: FR-5006 — agent-meta-review SHALL support override ONLY via agents:meta-override-approved label with admin check, Reason: ≥50 chars, and audit.jsonl
findings.description: agent-meta-review.yml has no override label handling, no admin permission check, no Reason: justification requirement, and no audit.jsonl logging. The workflow simply delegates to the document-review composite action with no override path at all.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/agent-meta-review.yml — no agents:meta-override-approved label check, no gh api permission check, no audit.jsonl step
findings.recommendation: Add a post-review step that checks for the agents:meta-override-approved label, verifies org-admin identity via gh api, validates a comment contains 'Reason:' with ≥50 chars, and appends to audit.jsonl.

findings.requirement: FR-5002 — agent-meta-review SHALL run a schema-validation step FIRST
findings.description: The PRD requires a dedicated schema validation step (verify frontmatter includes name/description/model/tools, reject unknown fields, reject tools additions beyond the allowlist without a rationale comment) before the Claude invocation. agent-meta-review.yml has no such step — it goes directly to the document-review composite which invokes Claude.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/agent-meta-review.yml — no schema validation step before document-review composite invocation
findings.recommendation: Add a pre-review step that parses agent frontmatter YAML, validates required fields, rejects unknown fields, and checks tools field additions against an allowlist.

findings.requirement: FR-3001 — claude-assistant SHALL trigger on pull_request_review_comment.created
findings.description: claude-assistant.yml only triggers on issue_comment.created. The PRD requires it also triggers on pull_request_review_comment.created. Review-level comments on a PR diff will not trigger Claude responses.
findings.recommendation: Add pull_request_review_comment: types: [created] to the on: block.

findings.requirement: FR-10003 — budget-gate SHALL read CLAUDE_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD with default 500
findings.description: The budget-gate workflow requires CLAUDE_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD as a mandatory secret (no default). aggregate-spend.js fails with ::error:: if the secret is unset. The PRD says default: 500. The current implementation has no fallback — teams that omit the secret will see an error rather than the PRD's intended $500 default behavior.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/budget-gate.yml:33 — CLAUDE_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD: required: true (no default); scripts/ci/aggregate-spend.js:392 — exits on unset/invalid value with no fallback
findings.recommendation: Either change budget-gate.yml to make CLAUDE_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD optional with a default input value of '500', or handle the unset case in aggregate-spend.js by defaulting to 500.

findings.requirement: FR-4008 — review workflows SHALL NOT run on automated pushes from known bots
findings.description: None of the four document review workflows (prd-review.yml, tdd-review.yml, plan-review.yml, spec-review.yml) or agent-meta-review.yml have a check to skip runs triggered by claude[bot], dependabot[bot], renovate[bot], or NONE-association bots. This can cause infinite loops and unnecessary Claude spend.
findings.evidence: prd-review.yml, tdd-review.yml, plan-review.yml, spec-review.yml, agent-meta-review.yml — no actor-association or login exclusion filters in any on: or if: clause
findings.recommendation: Add if: github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]' && github.actor != 'renovate[bot]' to each document-review job, or check actor association before invoking the budget-gate.

findings.requirement: FR-6007 — gitleaks SHALL exclude **/__mocks__/** from scanning
findings.description: gitleaks.toml excludes plugins/autonomous-dev/tests/fixtures/.* and tests/fixtures/security/.* but does not exclude **/__mocks__/**. The PRD explicitly requires both fixtures and __mocks__ directories be excluded to reduce false positives.
findings.recommendation: Add '''.*/__mocks__/.*''' to the allowlist.paths array in .github/security/gitleaks.toml.

findings.requirement: FR-8001 — release.yml first step SHALL validate strict semver tag regex
findings.description: release.yml's verify-version job strips the leading 'v' and compares to plugin.json version, but does not validate the full tag against the PRD's strict regex ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+(-[a-z0-9.]+)?$. A tag like 'v0.2.0.extra' would pass the strip-and-compare check while failing the spec.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/release.yml:62-76 — only strips leading 'v' and compares string to manifest version; no regex guard step
findings.recommendation: Add a regex validation step before the version comparison that matches GITHUB_REF_NAME against the strict semver pattern from FR-8001.

findings.requirement: FR-8005/FR-8006 — release SHALL build per-plugin zips with sha256 checksums
findings.description: release.yml has no plugin zip build step. The create-release job conditionally attaches dist/* if that directory exists, but no step creates the per-plugin zip archives or .sha256 checksum files that FR-8005/FR-8006 require.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/release.yml:269-285 — create-release uses files: ${{ steps.artifacts.outputs.files }} which is empty unless dist/ pre-exists; no zip build job present
findings.recommendation: Add a build-artifacts job that creates autonomous-dev.zip and autonomous-dev-assist.zip (excluding node_modules/, tests/, __mocks__/) and generates .sha256 files, then pass these as release assets.

findings.requirement: PRD §11 — five new autonomous-dev-assist skills (github-actions-setup, ci-troubleshoot, release-workflow-guide, ci-budget-guide, ci-migration-guide)
findings.description: The PRD §11 specifies five new skills for the autonomous-dev-assist plugin. None of them exist in plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/. The current skills directory contains only: config-guide, help, setup-wizard, troubleshoot. The ci-guide eval suite (22 cases) is also absent from tests/evals/assist/.
findings.evidence: ls /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/ — only config-guide, help, setup-wizard, troubleshoot; tests/evals/assist/ has no ci-guide cases
findings.recommendation: Create the five skills per PRD §11 and add the 22 ci-guide eval cases. Note: setup-wizard has CI Phase 12 scaffolding at phase-12-ci-setup.md (not Phase 9 as PRD §13 specifies) — this renumbering should be reconciled.

findings.requirement: FR-1004 — prettier SHALL check TS + JSON + YAML
findings.description: The prettier step checks src/**/*.ts, tests/**/*.ts, package.json, and tsconfig*.json but does not check YAML files. The PRD requires prettier on TS + JSON + YAML.
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:209 — npx prettier --check covers TS and JSON but no YAML glob
findings.recommendation: Add .github/workflows/*.yml or equivalent YAML glob to the prettier --check command.

findings.requirement: PRD §13 — CI setup in setup-wizard SHALL be Phase 9
findings.description: The PRD §13 coordination table places CI setup as Phase 9. The actual implementation in the setup-wizard places it as Phase 12 (phase-12-ci-setup.md). The wizard also lacks the .github/.wizard-ci-phase-done completion marker and --reconfigure flag described in PRD §11.
findings.recommendation: This may be an intentional expansion of the wizard's phase count (phases 8–16 vs PRD's 13). Reconcile the phase number in the PRD or update the wizard to use Phase 9 numbering. Add the .github/.wizard-ci-phase-done marker and --reconfigure idempotency per PRD §11.

title: PRD-011 Pipeline Variants & Extension Hooks — Conformance Audit
summary: The core request-type taxonomy (FR-1101/FR-1102), bug report schema (FR-1105/FR-1107), state schema extension (FR-1122/FR-1123/FR-1124), hook registry (FR-1108/FR-1109), hook discovery (FR-1127/FR-1129), trust/allowlist (FR-1111/FR-1112), sequential execution (FR-1114/FR-1116), hook output schema validation (§19.2), dynamic reload (FR-1128), cryptographic signature verification (FR-1112/§19.3), and reviewer-slot integration (FR-1125/FR-1126) are solidly implemented. However, several significant gaps remain: (1) per-hook timeout enforcement (FR-1115) is absent from the hook executor — the timeout_seconds field in hook manifests is declared but never enforced; (2) the Worker Thread sandbox described in §19.1 is not wired to the hook executor — hooks run with require() in the daemon process, not isolated V8 contexts; (3) the phase_overrides field is written into state.json but there is no runtime orchestrator that reads it to actually skip phases — pipeline routing enforcement is missing; (4) four new assist-plugin skills (FR section 11.1: pipeline-variants-guide, extension-hook-developer, hook-troubleshooting, request-type-selection) are absent; (5) operator-level enable/disable of request types (FR-1101/US-1101) is not in the config schema; (6) the hook point set is missing plan-pre-author and spec-pre-author from FR-1108 (10 defined vs. 11 specified). The hotfix variant skips only prd/prd_review/plan_review instead of the PRD-specified prd+tdd skip, which is a partial contradiction of FR-1102.

findings.description: Hook executor (intake/hooks/executor.ts) has no timeout enforcement. The invokeOne method calls require(hook.resolvedEntryPoint) and awaits the result with no timeout race. The hook manifest schema defines timeout_seconds and ExtensionsConfig.global_resource_limits.max_execution_time_seconds (intake/hooks/types.ts:303) exist in config, but neither is wired into the executor. A runaway hook will block indefinitely.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/executor.ts:241-259 — invoke block has no setTimeout/AbortController/Promise.race
findings.recommendation: Wrap the hook invocation promise in a Promise.race with a timer derived from Math.min(hook.timeout_seconds ?? Infinity, config.global_resource_limits.max_execution_time_seconds) * 1000. On timeout, terminate and record invocation-error with failure_mode semantics.

findings.description: Post-review §19.1 mandated that each hook run in a Node.js Worker Thread with a fresh V8 isolate, 256MB RSS cap, CPU-time budget, secret env-var stripping, and terminate() on timeout/crash. The hook executor uses a bare require() in the daemon process — no Worker is created, no isolation is applied, no memory limit is enforced. The only Worker-thread sandbox in the codebase (intake/standards/redos-sandbox.ts) is for ReDoS regex evaluation, not for hooks.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/executor.ts:243-249 — uses require(hook.resolvedEntryPoint) with no worker_threads import anywhere in the hooks module
findings.recommendation: Implement a WorkerPool for hooks per §19.1: each invocation spawns (or reuses pooled) Worker thread with resourceLimits, strips secret env vars, enforces timeout via worker.terminate(), and maps worker stdout/stderr to the audit log.

findings.requirement: FR-1102 Phase Override Matrix / Pipeline Routing Enforcement
findings.description: The PHASE_OVERRIDE_MATRIX is correctly defined (intake/types/phase-override.ts) and phase_overrides is written into state.json (intake/lib/state_json_writer.ts:147). However, no orchestrator or daemon code was found that reads phase_overrides from state to actually skip phases at runtime. The phase_overrides field is validated on load (intake/state/state_loader.ts:172) but not consumed to control execution. Additionally, the HOTFIX type only skips ['prd', 'prd_review', 'plan_review'], while FR-1102 specifies hotfix skips PRD and TDD — tdd/tdd_review are not skipped.
findings.evidence: intake/types/phase-override.ts:104-111 — HOTFIX skippedPhases: ['prd', 'prd_review', 'plan_review'] — missing tdd/tdd_review. No runtime consumer of phase_overrides found in non-test, non-type code.
findings.recommendation: Wire the orchestrator/daemon to read state.phase_overrides and skip phases not listed. Also add 'tdd' and 'tdd_review' to HOTFIX.skippedPhases to match FR-1102.

findings.requirement: FR-1108 Hook Point Definition (plan-pre-author, spec-pre-author missing)
findings.description: FR-1108 specifies 11 hook points including plan-pre-author and spec-pre-author. The implemented HookPoint enum has 10 points: intake-pre-validate, prd-pre-author, tdd-pre-author, code-pre-write, code-post-write, review-pre-score, review-post-score, deploy-pre, deploy-post, rule-evaluation. The plan-pre-author and spec-pre-author hook points are absent from both intake/hooks/types.ts and schemas/hook-manifest-v1.json.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/types.ts:28-39 — HookPoint enum lists 10 values, no PlanPreAuthor or SpecPreAuthor. schemas/hook-manifest-v1.json hookEntry.hook_point enum: same 10 values.
findings.recommendation: Add PlanPreAuthor = 'plan-pre-author' and SpecPreAuthor = 'spec-pre-author' to the HookPoint enum, HOOK_POINTS array, and hook-manifest-v1.json schema.

findings.requirement: FR-1101 / US-1101 Operator Enable/Disable of Request Types
findings.description: FR-1101 and US-1101 require operators to be able to enable/disable specific request types via configuration. The PRD's config section 8.1 specifies a request_types.enabled array in autonomous-dev.json. The autonomous-dev-config.schema.json only has an extensions block — there is no request_types.enabled/disabled field. All five types are always enabled with no operator gate.
findings.evidence: schemas/autonomous-dev-config.schema.json — only 'extensions' property exists at top level, no 'request_types' key
findings.recommendation: Add a request_types block to the config schema with an enabled array defaulting to all five types. Enforce in the intake path so disabled types are rejected at submission.

findings.description: PRD-011 §11.1 specifies four new skills for the autonomous-dev-assist plugin: pipeline-variants-guide, extension-hook-developer, hook-troubleshooting, and request-type-selection. None of these skill directories or SKILL.md files exist in plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/. The existing assist plugin has setup-wizard (which covers request types in phase-13), config-guide, help, and troubleshoot — but not the four dedicated skills specified.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/ — directories: config-guide, help, setup-wizard, troubleshoot. No pipeline-variants-guide, extension-hook-developer, hook-troubleshooting, or request-type-selection.
findings.recommendation: Create the four skill SKILL.md files per §11.1 specifications, including the conversation_flow, inputs/outputs, and trigger patterns.

findings.requirement: FR-1113 Privilege Escalation Prevention / Capability Enforcement
findings.description: The hook manifest schema declares a capabilities array (filesystem-write, network, child-processes, privileged-env). The TrustValidator checks capability declarations for meta-review triggers (stepCapabilityValidation). However, the executor does not enforce these capability restrictions at runtime — a hook declaring no capabilities can still perform filesystem writes or network calls because it runs in the daemon process via require(). The comments in executor.ts and types.ts acknowledge this: 'Enforcement lands in PLAN-019-3/4's sandbox layer.'
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/executor.ts:86-107 — constructor accepts optional trustValidator but has no capability enforcement. intake/hooks/types.ts:87-98 — capabilities declared but comment says 'sandbox enforces in a future plan'
findings.recommendation: Enforce capability restrictions by implementing the Worker Thread sandbox (§19.1) which is the prerequisite. Until then, at minimum gate on capabilities at registration time in strict mode.

findings.requirement: FR-1119 Rule Injection Points / FR-1120 Business Logic Adapter
findings.description: The rule-evaluation hook point is defined in the HookPoint enum (intake/hooks/types.ts:38) per §19.4's canonical update. However, no implementation of the RuleSetHook interface (FR-1118) or BusinessLogicHook interface (FR-1120) exists in the codebase. These are hook points that can be registered by plugins, but the specialized interfaces, result schemas, and the intake/pre-spec/pre-deploy evaluation triggers described in FR-1119 are not implemented.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/types.ts:38 — RuleEvaluation = 'rule-evaluation' exists. No RuleSetHook, BusinessLogicHook, TransformResult, or RuleSetResult types found in grep across all non-test TS files.
findings.recommendation: Implement the RuleSetHook/RuleSetResult and BusinessLogicHook/TransformResult interfaces per FR-1118/FR-1120, and wire rule-evaluation calls at intake, pre-spec, and pre-deploy phases.

findings.description: The ReloadController class (intake/hooks/reload-controller.ts) correctly implements debounced atomic registry swap. The cli_adapter_evaluators.ts sends SIGUSR1 to the daemon PID (line 159). However, no process.on('SIGUSR1', ...) handler was found in any TypeScript source file — only a reference in cli_adapter.js line 1128 that lists SIGUSR1 in a signals array (likely for cleanup, not reload). The reload() call on SIGUSR1 receipt by the daemon is not evidenced in non-compiled source.
findings.evidence: intake/adapters/cli_adapter_evaluators.ts:159 — sends SIGUSR1. intake/hooks/reload-controller.ts — ReloadController.reload() exists. No process.on('SIGUSR1', ...) found in .ts files.
findings.recommendation: Add a SIGUSR1 handler in the daemon entry point that calls reloadController.reload() and logs the diff.

findings.description: The tdd-author agent (agents/tdd-author.md) implements BUG MODE when --bug-context-path flag is passed, reads bug_context from state file, uses templates/tdd-bug.md as skeleton, and skips PRD. Bug context is passed via the BugReport interface (intake/types/bug-report.ts), bug-report.json schema validates required fields. This satisfies FR-1106.
findings.evidence: agents/tdd-author.md:49-60 — BUG MODE description. intake/types/bug-report.ts:46-72 — BugReport interface. templates/tdd-bug.md exists.

findings.requirement: FR-1105/FR-1107 Bug Report Schema and Validation
findings.description: BugReport schema (schemas/bug-report.json) requires title, description, reproduction_steps (minItems:1), expected_behavior, actual_behavior, error_messages, environment (os/runtime/version). The isBugReportShape() guard and BugReportSchema AJV export in intake/types/bug-report.ts provide runtime validation. Bug modal enforces required fields. This satisfies FR-1105 and FR-1107.
findings.evidence: schemas/bug-report.json:8-16 — required array with 7 fields. intake/types/bug-report.ts:99-114 — isBugReportShape type guard.

findings.description: All five request types (feature, bug, infra, refactor, hotfix) are defined in RequestType enum (intake/types/request-type.ts), accepted in the CLI via --type flag (intake/handlers/submit_handler.ts:168-172), and stored in both SQLite and state.json. DEFAULT_REQUEST_TYPE = 'feature' for backward compat.

findings.requirement: FR-1109/FR-1129 Hook Registration and Manifest Validation
findings.description: hooks.json manifest schema (schemas/hook-manifest-v1.json) validates id, name, version, hooks array with hook_point enum, entry_point, priority, failure_mode. PluginDiscovery scans plugin directories and validates via injected SchemaValidator. HookRegistry.register() stores and sorts hooks by priority.
findings.evidence: schemas/hook-manifest-v1.json — full schema. intake/hooks/discovery.ts:54-293 — PluginDiscovery. intake/hooks/registry.ts:61-101 — register().

findings.requirement: FR-1111/FR-1112 Extension Allowlist and Trust Validation
findings.description: ExtensionsConfig.allowlist in autonomous-dev-config.schema.json controls plugin trust. TrustValidator implements seven-step pipeline with allowlist mode (only allowlisted plugins pass), permissive mode (trust by default with optional signature), and strict mode (allowlist + signature + privileged reviewer check). SignatureVerifier implements Ed25519/RSA-PSS detached signature verification.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/trust-validator.ts:238-325 — three trust mode implementations. intake/hooks/signature-verifier.ts:43-71 — Ed25519/RSA-PSS verify().

findings.requirement: FR-1114/FR-1116 Sequential Execution and Failure Modes
findings.description: HookExecutor.executeHooksChained() runs hooks in priority order sequentially. failure_mode='block' throws HookBlockedError halting the pipeline. failure_mode='warn' logs and continues. failure_mode='ignore' silently continues. Previous results are threaded through HookContext.previousResults per hook invocation.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/executor.ts:344-443 — executeHooksChained(). HookBlockedError thrown at line 427.

findings.requirement: FR-1123/FR-1124 State Schema Extension and Migration
findings.description: state-v1.1.json schema adds request_type, bug_context, phase_overrides, type_config to v1.0. migrateStateV1_0ToV1_1() defaults request_type to 'feature'. requireV1_1() handles both versions. State loader validates phase_overrides field type.
findings.evidence: schemas/state-v1.1.json — properties include request_type, phase_overrides, type_config. intake/types/request-state.ts:118-130 — migration function.

findings.requirement: FR-1125/FR-1126 Custom Reviewer Registration and Aggregation
findings.description: ReviewerSlot interface with agent_name, review_gates, expertise_domains, minimum_threshold is defined (intake/hooks/types.ts:504-521). HookRegistry maintains a secondary reviewerIndex keyed by ReviewGate. runReviewersForGate() in intake/reviewers/aggregate.ts enforces minimum reviewer count, stamps fingerprints, and falls back to built-in PRD-004 reviewers. FR-1126 review aggregation is satisfied. §19.3's multi-reviewer minimum and privileged_reviewers allowlist are also implemented.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/types.ts:504-521 — ReviewerSlot. intake/reviewers/aggregate.ts:57-104 — runReviewersForGate(). intake/hooks/registry.ts:46-75 — reviewerIndex.

findings.description: PluginDiscovery.scan() walks one level deep in rootDir, reads hooks.json from each subdirectory, validates schema, and returns DiscoveryResult[]. Concurrent I/O via Promise.all for performance. Hidden directories and non-directory entries are skipped.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/discovery.ts:65-99 — scan() method with concurrent processing.

findings.description: ValidationPipeline validates both input (gates execution) and output (sanitizes, strips extra fields via removeAdditional). Output validation failures downgrade status to 'success-with-warnings' rather than blocking. SchemaNotFoundError surfaces as invocation-error for misconfiguration detection.
findings.evidence: intake/hooks/executor.ts:261-295 — output validation block. intake/hooks/validation-pipeline.ts (referenced but not fully read).

title: PRD-012 Quality Reviewer Suite — Conformance Audit
summary: The core specialist reviewer infrastructure is substantially implemented: all four agent definitions exist (qa-edge-case-reviewer, ux-ui-reviewer, accessibility-reviewer, rule-set-enforcement-reviewer) with correct behaviors, the reviewer chain scheduler/runner/aggregator machinery is in place, frontend-detection caching works, auto-detection scanner covers 6 signal types, and the four eval sub-suites (25/20/30/15 cases) are present. Key gaps are: (1) real Claude Agent SDK wiring is still stub code (TODO SPEC-020-2-04); (2) specialist reviewers bypass the PRD-011 extension hook registry entirely — they are wired directly into the chain config; (3) per-invocation resource limits (timeout, cost_cap, token_limit) are not part of the ReviewerEntry schema or runner; (4) the setup-wizard writes reviewer-chains.yaml but the chain-resolver reads reviewer-chains.json causing a format mismatch; (5) FR-1255 --update merge flag is not implemented; (6) Go/Rust stack detection is absent from the auto-detection scanner.

findings.requirement: FR-1271 / FR-1272 — per-invocation resource limits and usage tracking
findings.description: The ReviewerEntry type (intake/reviewers/types.ts) and reviewer-chains-v1.json schema have no timeout, cost_cap, or token_limit fields. The runner (intake/reviewers/runner.ts) imposes no timeout on invoke() calls and records only duration_ms — not cost or token count. Per-reviewer daily/monthly spending caps (FR-1274) are also absent. Cost controls exist for the deploy subsystem but are completely absent from the reviewer chain subsystem.
findings.evidence: intake/reviewers/types.ts:50-57 (ReviewerEntry interface — no timeout/cost fields); intake/reviewers/runner.ts:111-143 (runOne — no timeout wrapping); schemas/reviewer-chains-v1.json (no timeout/cost_cap properties); intake/deploy/cost-cap.ts exists for deploy, nothing equivalent for reviewers
findings.recommendation: Add timeout_ms, cost_cap_usd, and token_limit to ReviewerEntry and the reviewer-chains schema. Implement Promise.race timeout in runner.runOne. Add a cost ledger per reviewer type mirroring deploy/cost-ledger.ts.

findings.requirement: FR-1261 / FR-1262 — extension hook registration with PRD-011
findings.description: All four specialist reviewers (qa-edge-case, ux-ui, accessibility, rule-set-enforcement) are wired directly into the reviewer chain config (config_defaults/reviewer-chains.json) and the REVIEWER_REGISTRY in invoke-reviewer.ts. None of them register via the HookRegistry reviewer-slot mechanism (intake/hooks/registry.ts). The hook registry's ReviewGate index (getReviewersForGate) is never populated with these agents. This means the extension hook discovery path documented in FR-1261/FR-1262 and the PRD-011 integration does not exist for the specialist reviewers.
findings.evidence: intake/reviewers/invoke-reviewer.ts:80-87 (REVIEWER_REGISTRY — hardcoded stubs); intake/hooks/registry.ts:47-76 (reviewerIndex — no specialist entries); grep for 'qa-edge-case|ux-ui-reviewer|accessibility-reviewer|rule-set-enforcement' in intake/hooks/ returns no results
findings.recommendation: Register the four specialist reviewers via HookManifest entries with ReviewerSlot objects pointing at 'code-review' (and 'document-review-spec' for ux-ui). This satisfies FR-1261/FR-1262 and makes them discoverable to plugin authors per FR-1263.

findings.description: The PRD §9.2 specifies the per-repo override file as .autonomous-dev/review-chains.yaml (YAML). The chain-resolver reads .autonomous-dev/reviewer-chains.json (JSON). The setup-wizard Phase 15 writes .autonomous-dev/reviewer-chains.yaml (YAML). This is a three-way inconsistency: PRD says review-chains.yaml, resolver reads reviewer-chains.json, wizard writes reviewer-chains.yaml. An operator following the wizard's output will never have their override picked up by the runtime.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on one format. Either (a) update chain-resolver to read reviewer-chains.yaml and parse YAML, or (b) update the wizard to write reviewer-chains.json, or (c) add YAML support and accept both extensions. Document the canonical path in one place.

findings.requirement: FR-1242 — default chains for Spec requests include ux-ui-reviewer
findings.description: FR-1242 requires that Spec requests run [spec-reviewer] + frontend-conditional [ux-ui-reviewer]. The config_defaults/reviewer-chains.json only contains code_review gates per request type; there is no spec_review gate defined anywhere in the default config. This means the ux-ui-reviewer never fires for Spec-phase documents, missing the requirement.
findings.evidence: config_defaults/reviewer-chains.json (entire file — only 'code_review' gate per request type, no 'spec_review' gate); PRD-012 §5.5 FR-1242: 'Spec requests run [spec-reviewer] + frontend-conditional [ux-ui-reviewer]'
findings.recommendation: Add a spec_review gate to the default chain for 'feature' (at minimum) containing spec-reviewer as built-in and ux-ui-reviewer with trigger: 'frontend'.

findings.requirement: invoke-reviewer.ts — real agent SDK wiring (all P0 reviewers)
findings.description: The production InvokeReviewerFn in intake/reviewers/invoke-reviewer.ts returns a hardcoded stub (score: 85, verdict: APPROVE, stub: true) for all six reviewers including the four specialist agents. The agent .md definitions exist under plugins/autonomous-dev/agents/ but are never invoked. This means the specialist reviewer suite as a whole is structurally present but not actually executing in production — every code review will always 'pass' the specialist reviewers.
