findings.evidence: intake/reviewers/invoke-reviewer.ts:58-68 (stubDispatcher always returns score:85/APPROVE); intake/reviewers/invoke-reviewer.ts:80-87 (REVIEWER_REGISTRY maps all 6 reviewers to stubDispatcher); comments: 'TODO(SPEC-020-2-04 production wiring): replace per-reviewer entries with real SDK invocations'
findings.recommendation: Implement SPEC-020-2-04 production wiring: resolve each reviewer name to its agents/*.md definition, invoke the Claude Agent SDK, parse the reviewer-finding-v1.json response, and return the actual score/verdict. This is the highest-priority gap for the suite to provide real value.

findings.description: FR-1255 requires an 'autonomous-dev standards detect --update' command that re-runs detection and merges new findings with the existing .autonomous-dev/standards.yaml, preserving manual customizations. The CLI adapter implements 'standards scan [--diff]' which writes to standards.inferred.yaml and separately shows additions vs the existing standards.yaml, but there is no --update flag that actually merges new detections into the canonical standards.yaml.
findings.evidence: intake/adapters/cli_adapter_standards.ts:5-8 (available commands: 'standards scan [--repo] [--diff] [--json]', 'standards show', 'standards validate'; no 'detect' verb, no '--update' flag); PRD-012 FR-1255: 'system SHALL support autonomous-dev standards detect --update to re-run detection and merge new findings with existing .autonomous-dev/standards.yaml'
findings.recommendation: Add a 'standards scan --update' flag (aliased as 'detect --update' per PRD wording) that reads standards.yaml, calls AutoDetectionScanner.scan(), and writes rules whose IDs are not already present in standards.yaml, preserving existing manual rules.

findings.requirement: FR-1281 / FR-1282 — precision ≥80% / recall ≥70% validation for specialist reviewers
findings.description: The auto-detection scanner has a rigorous precision-report harness (tests/standards/precision-report.ts) with known-good/known-bad fixture repos and a ≥0.80 threshold enforced by SPEC-021-1-05. But the four specialist reviewer agents (qa-edge-case, ux-ui, accessibility, rule-set-enforcement) have no equivalent precision/recall testing harness. The eval YAML files (qa-reviewer-eval.yaml etc.) are test case definitions for the assist plugin's conversational eval framework — they do not measure model precision/recall against ground truth or enforce the ≥80%/≥70% thresholds before the reviewers are considered for required-status-check promotion.
findings.evidence: tests/standards/precision-report.ts (exists for auto-detection); plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/evals/test-cases/qa-reviewer-eval.yaml (25 conversational eval cases, no recall measurement); PRD-012 FR-1281: 'Each specialist reviewer SHALL meet quality thresholds through fixture-based testing: precision ≥80%, recall ≥70%, before promotion to required status check'
findings.recommendation: Build a reviewer-specific fixture corpus runner analogous to precision-report.ts. For each eval case provide known-expected and known-not-expected findings lists; compute TP/FP/FN per-reviewer; enforce 0.80 precision and 0.70 recall at gate-promotion time. This is prerequisite to FR-1281.

findings.requirement: FR-1251 — auto-detection for Go (go.mod) and Rust (Cargo.toml)
findings.description: FR-1251 requires scanning for go.mod (Go) and Cargo.toml (Rust) project files. The AutoDetectionScanner only handles package.json, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml for framework detection, plus .eslintrc.json, .prettierrc*, tsconfig.json, jest.config.*, and README.md. go.mod and Cargo.toml are not in FRAMEWORK_MAP or JEST_CONFIG_FILES or PRETTIER_FILES. A Go or Rust project will yield zero detected rules from the framework-dep signal.
findings.evidence: intake/standards/auto-detection.ts:54-63 (FRAMEWORK_MAP — only fastapi, flask, django, express, react, vue, @angular/core; no Go/Rust entries); PRD-012 FR-1251: 'go.mod → Go project, Cargo.toml → Rust project'
findings.recommendation: Add go.mod and Cargo.toml detection to FRAMEWORK_MAP (or a separate LANGUAGE_FILE_MAP). At minimum emit an 'auto:go-module' or 'auto:rust-cargo' rule when these files are present.

findings.requirement: FR-14 (§14.1 security) — read-only tool isolation for reviewers
findings.description: PRD-012 §14.1 states: 'All specialist reviewers run with read-only permissions (Read, Glob, Grep only) — no file modification, shell execution, or network access'. All four specialist reviewer agents have 'Write' in their tools frontmatter (needed to emit the phase-result envelope). The rule-set-enforcement-reviewer also has 'Bash(node *)'. While Write is operationally necessary for the verdict envelope pattern, it directly contradicts the PRD's stated security control. Additionally, ux-ui-reviewer and accessibility-reviewer were specified to have WebFetch for design system / WCAG reference lookup (§8.2/§8.3) but this tool is absent from their frontmatter.
findings.evidence: agents/qa-edge-case-reviewer.md:8-12 (tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Write); agents/accessibility-reviewer.md:8-12 (same, no WebFetch); agents/rule-set-enforcement-reviewer.md:8-14 (tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Write, Bash(node *)); PRD-012 §14.1: 'Read, Glob, Grep only'; PRD-012 §8.2/§8.3: 'WebFetch for design system/WCAG references'
findings.recommendation: Either (a) update the PRD to reflect that Write is needed for the phase-result envelope (deliberate deviation with documented rationale), or (b) implement a dedicated out-of-band verdict channel that does not require Write access. Also decide whether to add WebFetch per §8.2/§8.3 or formally remove that requirement.

findings.requirement: §19.2 setup wizard — advisory-mode rollout and corpus validation before promotion
findings.description: PRD-012 §19.2 specifies that Phase 15 of the setup wizard should: (1) enable specialist reviewers in advisory mode by default, (2) configure per-request-type reviewer chains, and (3) validate precision/recall against a 20-PR fixture corpus before promoting to required-status-check. The setup wizard Phase 15 file implements steps (2) with chain weight/threshold configuration and a dry-run verification, but has no advisory-mode default, no 20-PR fixture corpus evaluation, and no precision/recall gate before promotion.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/setup-wizard/phases/phase-15-reviewer-chains.md (full file — no advisory mode default, no corpus validation step, no precision/recall threshold check); PRD-012 §19.2: 'enabling specialist reviewers (advisory mode default)' and 'reviewing precision/recall for the 20-PR fixture corpus before promotion to required-status-check'
findings.recommendation: Add a step to phase-15 that (a) defaults all new specialist reviewers to blocking: false (advisory) and (b) documents the corpus validation requirement as a follow-up action before operator sets blocking: true.

findings.requirement: FR-1204 / FR-1205 — QA reviewer: test spec coverage validation and static security patterns
findings.description: FR-1204 requires the QA reviewer to validate that test specifications include edge-case coverage. FR-1205 requires detection of SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, and buffer overflow patterns. The agent's prompt covers 'input validation' with SQL injection and path traversal examples (categories 1-6), but 'test specification coverage' (FR-1204 — flag tests that only cover happy paths) is not a documented category in the agent's methodology. The agent is language-and-stack-agnostic per its own prompt and runs against diffs rather than test-spec documents.
findings.evidence: agents/qa-edge-case-reviewer.md:59-117 (six categories: Input Validation, Boundary Conditions, Race Conditions, Error Paths, Null Handling, Resource Leaks — no 'test specification coverage' category); PRD-012 FR-1204: 'validate that test specifications include edge case coverage'; FR-1205: SQL injection, XSS, path traversal covered implicitly in category 1
findings.recommendation: Add a Category 7 'Test Coverage Gaps' to the qa-edge-case-reviewer agent that checks associated test files (via Glob) for presence of edge-case, boundary, and error-path test cases alongside changed source files.

findings.requirement: FR-1214 / FR-1215 — UX reviewer: button/link text clarity and design pattern consistency
findings.description: FR-1214 (button/link text clarity) is covered by Heuristic 6 in ux-ui-reviewer.md. FR-1215 (consistent design patterns: spacing, colors, typography deviation) is not covered; the agent explicitly states it does not evaluate 'visual aesthetics, brand alignment' and its six heuristics do not include design-pattern consistency or typography.
findings.evidence: agents/ux-ui-reviewer.md:50-51 ('You do not evaluate visual aesthetics, brand alignment, or copy tone'); agents/ux-ui-reviewer.md:93-102 (Heuristic 6 covers button labels); no heuristic covers typography/spacing consistency per FR-1215
findings.recommendation: Add a Heuristic 7 for design-pattern consistency (e.g., mixed spacing units, inconsistent breakpoint constants) or explicitly mark FR-1215 as deferred to avoid scope confusion.

findings.requirement: FR-1234 — standards reviewer: severity levels (blocking / warning / advisory)
findings.description: FR-1234 requires the standards reviewer to respect blocking/warning/advisory severity levels from the standards file. The agent's verdict mapping uses a 4-level system (low/medium/high/critical → APPROVE/CONCERNS/REQUEST_CHANGES) that does not directly correspond to the blocking/warning/advisory taxonomy the standards DSL may define. The mapping table converts rule severity to verdict, but blocking vs advisory behavior at the gate level is determined by the ReviewerEntry.blocking field in reviewer-chains.json, not by individual rule severity.
findings.evidence: agents/rule-set-enforcement-reviewer.md:113-122 (Verdict Mapping: all findings low → APPROVE; any medium → CONCERNS; high/critical → REQUEST_CHANGES); PRD-012 FR-1234: 'blocking (fails status check), warning (comment + label), advisory (logged only)'; intake/reviewers/types.ts:46 (ReviewerEntry.blocking — a single flag for the whole reviewer, not per-rule)
findings.recommendation: The per-rule advisory/warning/blocking behavior requires either the reviewer to emit separate 'advisory-findings' vs 'blocking-findings' in its output schema, or the aggregator to inspect individual finding severities. Current design collapses all rules to a single reviewer verdict.

findings.requirement: FR-1241/FR-1243/FR-1246 — reviewer chain config model and aggregated verdict
findings.description: The reviewer chain configuration model (ChainConfig, ReviewerEntry), scheduler (ReviewerScheduler with parallel frontend group, sequential others, rule-set-last), runner (ReviewerRunner with error capture), and aggregator (ScoreAggregator with built-in-min rule, blocking threshold, advisory warnings) are all fully implemented. The GateVerdict includes outcome, reason, per_reviewer breakdown, warnings, and built_in_count_completed.
findings.evidence: intake/reviewers/types.ts (ChainConfig, ReviewerEntry, GateVerdict); intake/reviewers/scheduler.ts (ReviewerScheduler); intake/reviewers/runner.ts (ReviewerRunner); intake/reviewers/aggregator.ts (ScoreAggregator); config_defaults/reviewer-chains.json (default chains)

findings.description: AutoDetectionScanner implements 6 signal types (framework-dep, linter-config, formatter-config, tsconfig-strict, test-runner-pattern, readme-mention), writes standards.inferred.yaml with the required 'auto-generated' header comment. The standards show/validate CLI commands are also present. The 'standards scan --diff' flag shows additions vs existing standards.yaml.
findings.evidence: intake/standards/auto-detection.ts (AutoDetectionScanner, writeInferredStandards); intake/standards/auto-detection-types.ts (ScanResult, DetectedRule, SignalKind); intake/adapters/cli_adapter_standards.ts (scan/show/validate verbs)

findings.description: The four eval sub-suites exist with the exact case counts specified in PRD-012 §19.3: qa-reviewer-eval.yaml (25 cases), ux-reviewer-eval.yaml (20 cases), a11y-reviewer-eval.yaml (30 cases), standards-reviewer-eval.yaml (15 cases). Security-critical cases are present in qa-reviewer-eval (5 cases tagged security_critical, 100% pass rate required).

title: PRD-013 Engineering Standards & Plugin Chaining — Conformance Audit
summary: The codebase implements the core standards infrastructure (schema, loader, resolver, auto-detection, evaluators, sandbox, chain executor, fix-recipe) comprehensively. However, several requirements are only partially or not implemented: the standards injection pipeline is incomplete (only prd-author has the placeholder; tdd-author and code-executor do not, and the daemon spawner never substitutes it); the max chain length default (10) contradicts the PRD's required limit of 5; per-chain wall-clock timeout is declared but not enforced in the executor; two of four required artifact schemas are absent; the two-person approval flag from the meta-reviewer is emitted by the agent but never consumed/enforced by any aggregator; cryptographic signature verification for custom evaluators is absent (path-based allowlist only); and the 'web' service_type from the PRD was replaced with 'frontend' in the schema without acknowledgment.

findings.description: Only agents/prd-author.md contains the {{STANDARDS_SECTION}} placeholder (line 40). agents/tdd-author.md and agents/code-executor.md have no such placeholder. PLAN-021-3 explicitly required all three agents. Furthermore, bin/spawn-session.sh (the actual agent launcher) does not perform {{STANDARDS_SECTION}} substitution anywhere — no call to renderStandardsSection is wired into the session spawn path.
findings.evidence: agents/prd-author.md:40 (has placeholder); agents/tdd-author.md (no placeholder); agents/code-executor.md (no placeholder); bin/spawn-session.sh (no standards substitution); intake/standards/prompt-renderer.ts:151 (function defined but called only from tests)

findings.description: FR-1317 specifies 'Maximum chain length: 5 plugins'. The DEFAULT_CHAIN_LIMITS constant sets max_length to 10, doubling the PRD limit. The TDD-022 spec itself references 'default: 5' (docs/tdd/TDD-022-plugin-chaining-engine.md:1096) making this a deliberate drift between the executor defaults and both the PRD and TDD.
findings.recommendation: Change DEFAULT_CHAIN_LIMITS.max_length from 10 to 5, or document the intentional relaxation in the PRD as a post-review update.

findings.description: The standards-meta-reviewer agent (agents/standards-meta-reviewer.md:115) correctly sets requires_two_person_approval: true in its output JSON. However, no aggregator in src/review-gate/score-aggregator.ts or intake/reviewers/aggregator.ts reads or enforces this flag — neither file contains 'two_person' or 'requires_two_person' logic. The SPEC-021-3-02 spec (line 137) explicitly requires 'src/reviewers/aggregator.ts' to gate on this field, but that code does not exist.
findings.evidence: agents/standards-meta-reviewer.md:115 (emits flag); src/review-gate/score-aggregator.ts (no two-person logic); intake/reviewers/aggregator.ts (no two-person logic); docs/specs/SPEC-021-3-02-standards-meta-reviewer-and-two-person-approval.md:137

findings.description: FR-1317 requires a 'Maximum total execution time: 30 minutes'. The DEFAULT_CHAIN_LIMITS.per_chain_timeout_seconds is 600 (10 minutes, not 30). More critically, the field is declared in the type and constants but is never referenced inside executeChain() or runChainBody() — there is no actual wall-clock enforcement of the total chain timeout in the executor code.
findings.evidence: intake/chains/executor.ts:71 (field declared), intake/chains/executor.ts:94 (value: 600), intake/chains/executor.ts:409-1137 (executeChain/runChainBody — no per_chain_timeout_seconds reference found)

findings.description: FR-1318 requires four artifact schemas: security-findings, code-patches, test-requirements, and documentation-updates. Only security-findings (schemas/artifacts/security-findings/1.0.json) and code-patches (schemas/artifacts/code-patches/1.0.json) are implemented. test-requirements and documentation-updates schemas are absent.
findings.evidence: schemas/artifacts/ (contains only code-patches/ and security-findings/ subdirectories)

findings.requirement: FR-1330 — cryptographic signature verification for custom evaluators
findings.description: FR-1330 states 'The system SHALL maintain an allowlist of approved custom evaluators with cryptographic signature verification to prevent execution of unauthorized code.' Section 19.4 of the PRD also specifies evaluators be added to 'extensions.evaluators_allowlist'. The sandbox.ts implementation uses a path-based allowlist (checking if the absolute path is in the provided allowlist array) but has no cryptographic signature verification, hash validation, or code signing check.
findings.evidence: intake/standards/sandbox.ts:60-73 (path-based allowlist only, no signature check)

findings.description: FR-1304 specifies service_type matches 'web, api, worker, library'. The PRD DSL examples also use service_type: 'web'. The actual schema enum is ['api', 'worker', 'cli', 'library', 'frontend'] — 'web' was replaced with 'frontend' and 'cli' was added. Any organization standards.yaml using service_type: 'web' (as shown in every PRD example) will fail schema validation.
findings.evidence: schemas/standards-v1.json (enum: api, worker, cli, library, frontend); intake/standards/types.ts:28 (same enum); PRD-013 §5.1 and §8 examples use 'web'

findings.requirement: FR-1325 — machine-readable fix recipes consumable by code-fixer plugins
findings.description: The fix-recipe schema and emitFixRecipe() helper are implemented. However, the rule-set-enforcement-reviewer agent (agents/rule-set-enforcement-reviewer.md:97) emits only a 'suggested_fix' text field rather than structured machine-readable fix recipes. There is no integration in the reviewer agent that calls emitFixRecipe() or writes to fix-recipes/ — that wiring between the reviewer's output and the fix-recipe persister is absent.

findings.requirement: FR-1313 — standards cache for task duration
findings.description: FR-1313 requires 'Standards inheritance resolution SHALL be performed at task start and cached for the duration of the task execution.' No request-scoped or task-scoped standards cache exists. The CLI adapters call resolveStandards() on each invocation, and there is no mechanism in spawn-session.sh or any daemon code to cache the resolved standards and share them across agent invocations within a single request.

FR-1305 specifies the assertion type 'custom_evaluator: Delegates to registered custom evaluator'. The PRD DSL example (§8) uses 'custom_evaluator: { name: ..., config: {} }'. The actual implementation in types.ts uses 'custom_evaluator_args: Record<string, unknown>' instead, a renamed field. Standards files following the PRD schema examples with 'custom_evaluator' key will fail validation.

FR-1307 specifies 7 signal types including deployment configs (Dockerfile, k8s manifests) and CI/CD security steps (gitleaks, bandit, semgrep). The implementation covers 6 signals: framework-dep, linter-config, formatter-config, tsconfig-strict, test-runner-pattern, readme-mention. Deployment patterns and CI/CD security tooling signals are explicitly out of scope per the auto-detection module comment ('out of scope for v1').
findings.evidence: intake/standards/auto-detection.ts:18-21 (lists 6 signals, notes v1.1 scope exclusions)

findings.requirement: FR-1339 — intelligent caching with mtimes and hash
findings.description: FR-1339 requires 'intelligent caching of standards evaluation results based on file modification timestamps and standards file hashes.' No such cache exists anywhere in the standards subsystem. The Ajv validator is cached at compile time (loader.ts:55) but evaluated artifacts are not cached.
findings.evidence: intake/standards/loader.ts:55 (Ajv compile cache only); no mtime or hash-based evaluation cache found

findings.description: FR-1340 requires 'Standards processing SHALL be optional and configurable, allowing teams to disable standards enforcement for exploratory or emergency work with appropriate approval gates.' No skip/disable flag for standards enforcement is implemented in the CLI, daemon, or spawner.
findings.evidence: intake/adapters/cli_adapter_standards.ts (no disable flag); bin/spawn-session.sh (no standards skip logic)

findings.description: FR-1309 requires a promotion workflow for converting inferred standards to active standards. The only implementation is the CLI --diff flag that shows inferred rules not in active standards.yaml. There is no interactive promotion UI, no confirm/reject flow, and no command to move a rule from standards.inferred.yaml to standards.yaml.
findings.evidence: intake/adapters/cli_adapter_standards.ts:241 (--diff flag only); auto-detection.ts:498 (writes inferred file, no promotion logic)

title: PRD-014 Deployment Backends Framework — Conformance Audit
summary: The core framework is substantially implemented. The TypeScript interface (DeploymentBackend), all four bundled backends (local, static, docker-local, github-pages), the BackendRegistry with tool-probe validation, configuration loader/validator, environment resolver, orchestrator with approval gates, cost-cap enforcement, HMAC-signed deployment records, artifact store with checksum integrity, structured per-deploy JSONL logger with rotation, and HealthMonitor with auto-rollback are all present and well-tested. All four cloud backend plugins (gcp, aws, azure, k8s) exist with CredentialProxy integration satisfying SEC-004. However, 11 requirements are missing or only partial: (1) the DeploymentBackend interface omits the optional monitor() method and costModel/BackendCapabilities properties specified in the PRD interface; (2) the manual rollback CLI command (autonomous-dev deploy rollback REQ-NNNNNN) is not implemented — only auto-rollback exists; (3) deployment ID format uses ULIDs instead of the PRD-specified DEP-{request-id}-{environment}-{timestamp} format; (4) multi-environment sequential pipeline orchestration (dev→staging→prod chain) is not implemented — runDeploy() handles one environment at a time; (5) project-type auto-detection (FR-1425) is missing from the deploy stack; (6) environment config inheritance (FR-1421, the 'inherits' key) is absent from the schema and resolver; (7) config hot-reload (FR-1424) is not implemented for deploy.yaml; (8) artifact garbage collection (FR-1430) is absent; (9) per-backend costModel property (FR-1410) is not on the implemented interface; (10) external log forwarding adapters (FR-1472) are absent; (11) the three named assist skills (deploy-framework-guide, deployment-troubleshooting, cloud-backend-setup consolidation per §20.5) are not individually discoverable as named skills — covered by broader help/runbook content instead. Per-process egress firewall for backend processes (§20.1) has a firewall subsystem but no evidence of per-backend-process enforcement wired into the deploy path.

findings.description: The implemented DeploymentBackend interface at intake/deploy/types.ts:145 omits the optional monitor() method (FR-1408) and the costModel/'pay-per-build'/'pay-per-runtime'/'free'/'custom' property (FR-1410). The PRD §8.2 spec includes both. The BackendCapabilities boolean struct (supportsRollback, supportsMonitoring, etc.) is also absent — replaced by a flat BackendMetadata.capabilities string-literal array.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/types.ts:145-155 — interface has build/deploy/healthCheck/rollback only; no monitor?, no costModel, no BackendCapabilities
findings.recommendation: Add optional monitor?(record: DeploymentRecord): Promise<MonitorHandle> to the DeploymentBackend interface, add costModel property, or document the deliberate divergence.

findings.description: FR-1454 and US-19 require autonomous-dev deploy rollback REQ-NNNNNN [environment] to be exposed as a CLI command for operators to trigger rollback within the retention period. No such CLI file exists. Only auto-rollback via HealthMonitor is implemented.
findings.evidence: intake/cli/ directory contains deploy_approve_command.ts, deploy_reject_command.ts, deploy_backends_command.ts, deploy_cost_command.ts, deploy_estimate_command.ts, deploy_logs_command.ts, deploy_plan_command.ts — no deploy_rollback_command.ts
findings.recommendation: Implement intake/cli/deploy_rollback_command.ts that calls backend.rollback() on the given deployment record and registers it under 'deploy rollback <deployId> [env]'.

findings.description: FR-1468 requires deploymentId format DEP-{request-id}-{environment}-{timestamp}. The implementation generates ULIDs (26-char Crockford base32) for both artifact IDs and deploy IDs, not the prescribed format.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/id.ts:18 generateUlid(); intake/deploy/backends/local.ts:203 deployId: generateUlid(); intake/deploy/artifact-store.ts:24
findings.recommendation: Either adopt the DEP- prefix format from the PRD, or update the PRD to reflect the ULID-based approach (which is arguably better) and close the gap.

findings.description: FR-1440 requires multi-environment deployments to execute in sequence (dev→staging→prod), with each environment requiring success of the previous. runDeploy() handles a single envName per call. The types-config has auto_promote_from but no orchestration logic acts on it.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/orchestrator.ts:237 runDeploy() — takes single envName; intake/deploy/types-config.ts:28 auto_promote_from exists as field but intake/deploy/environment.ts:175 only resolves it without acting on it; no multi-env loop exists
findings.recommendation: Implement a runMultiEnvDeploy() orchestrator that reads the environment order from config, calls runDeploy() sequentially, and gates each stage on the prior success.

findings.requirement: FR-1441 — Trust level gate integration (L0/L1/L2/L3 matrix)
findings.description: FR-1441 requires trust-level-based automatic approval gates per environment (dev proceeds at L2+, staging at L0-L1 requires human, prod always requires human). The deployment approval system uses an abstract ApprovalLevel enum ('none','single','two-person','admin') that operators must map manually — no PRD-007 trust-level (L0/L1/L2/L3) integration exists in the deploy orchestrator.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/types-config.ts:15 ApprovalLevel = 'none' | 'single' | 'two-person' | 'admin'; intake/deploy/orchestrator.ts:253 checks resolved.approval, no L0-L3 lookup
findings.recommendation: Wire PRD-007 trust engine output to map trust levels to ApprovalLevel, or document the mapping convention operators should use.

findings.description: FR-1421 requires staging to inherit dev settings and prod to inherit staging with explicit overrides (the 'inherits' key from PRD §11.2). The deploy-config-v1.json schema and resolver do not support an 'inherits' key.
findings.evidence: schemas/deploy-config-v1.json — no 'inherits' property in environment $defs; intake/deploy/environment.ts:163 only does shallow merge of repo parameters and env parameters
findings.recommendation: Add optional 'inherits' key to the JSON schema and implement inheritance chain resolution in the environment resolver.

findings.requirement: FR-1424 — Configuration hot-reload without daemon restart
findings.description: FR-1424 requires deploy.yaml changes to take effect at the next deployment without daemon restart. The environment loader reads config fresh on each runDeploy() call (no stale cache), but there is no watcher or explicit hot-reload mechanism documented or tested.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/environment.ts:79 loadConfig() reads file on each call — effectively stateless, which achieves hot-reload-by-default, but no test or documentation asserts this property and the module-level cachedValidator is a compile-time concern not a config-reload concern
findings.recommendation: Document that loadConfig() re-reads on every call (achieving hot-reload semantics) and add a test asserting that a config change is visible on the next runDeploy() without restart.

findings.requirement: FR-1425 — Project type auto-detection for build context
findings.description: FR-1425 requires the system to detect project type (static site, Node.js, Python, Go, Rust, Java) and provide it in BuildContext. BuildContext at types.ts has no detectedLanguage/detectedFramework fields; no detection logic exists in the deploy stack.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/types.ts:78-91 BuildContext interface — no detectedLanguage or detectedFramework fields; no file scanning logic in any deploy module
findings.recommendation: Implement a project-type detector (check for Dockerfile, package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod, Cargo.toml, pom.xml) and add detectedLanguage/detectedFramework fields to BuildContext.

findings.description: FR-1430 requires artifact garbage collection removing unused artifacts older than a configurable retention period (default 30 days). No GC implementation exists in artifact-store.ts or any related module.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/artifact-store.ts — no purge/GC functions; no retention-day configuration in deploy-config-v1.json
findings.recommendation: Implement a purgeOldArtifacts(repoPath, retentionDays) function in artifact-store.ts and hook it into daemon startup or a scheduled maintenance task.

findings.description: FR-1410 requires each backend to declare its cost characteristics as 'free', 'pay-per-build', 'pay-per-runtime', or 'custom'. Neither the BackendMetadata interface nor any backend implementation includes this property.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/types.ts:33-44 BackendMetadata — name, version, supportedTargets, capabilities, requiredTools; no costModel
findings.recommendation: Add costModel: 'free' | 'pay-per-build' | 'pay-per-runtime' | 'custom' to BackendMetadata and update all four bundled backends plus the four cloud backends.

findings.description: FR-1472 requires log streaming to external systems (ELK, Splunk, CloudWatch) through configurable log forwarding adapters. The DeployLogger has a telemetry adapter seam (DeployTelemetryAdapter) but no concrete ELK/Splunk/CloudWatch adapters are implemented.
findings.evidence: intake/deploy/logger.ts:62 DeployTelemetryAdapter interface (fire-and-forget emit only); no concrete forwarding adapters in intake/deploy/ or intake/adapters/
findings.recommendation: This is a P1 requirement; implement at least one concrete log forwarding adapter (e.g. HTTP POST to configurable endpoint) or document the adapter extension point clearly.

findings.requirement: §20.1 SEC-004 — Per-process egress firewall for backend plugins
findings.description: The post-review security requirement (§20.1) specifies that backend processes run with a per-process firewall allowing only the cloud provider's API endpoints and the local daemon socket. The firewall subsystem exists (intake/firewall/) but no wiring connects backend process spawning to per-process firewall enforcement.
findings.evidence: intake/firewall/ directory exists with nftables.ts and pfctl.ts; no code in intake/deploy/ or cloud backend src/ references firewall/ modules
findings.recommendation: Wire the firewall module into the backend invocation path so a per-process network policy is applied when a cloud backend starts a subprocess.

findings.description: PRD §13.1 specifies named skills deploy-framework-guide, deployment-troubleshooting, cloud-backend-setup (consolidated per §20.5 from four per-cloud skills). These are not implemented as individually invocable named skills. Coverage exists via the help/SKILL.md knowledge base and deploy-runbook.md, and a deploy eval suite (36 test cases) exists.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/ — only config-guide, help, setup-wizard, troubleshoot directories; no deploy-framework-guide or deployment-troubleshooting skill directories
findings.recommendation: Either create named skill directories or document that these are subsumed by the help and troubleshoot skills with explicit routing logic.

findings.requirement: FR-1411/FR-1414/FR-1412 — Bundled backend registration with validation
findings.description: All four bundled backends (local, static, docker-local, github-pages) are implemented and registered via registry-bootstrap.ts. BackendRegistry.register() validates required interface methods and probes tool availability at registration time.

findings.requirement: FR-1413 — Cloud backends as separate plugins (gcp, aws, azure, k8s)
findings.description: All four cloud backend plugins exist as separate plugin directories with full DeploymentBackend implementations using CredentialProxy for credential isolation.
The CredentialProxy interface and ScopedCredential types are fully defined, and all four cloud backends use proxy.acquire() for every cloud API call instead of reading long-lived credentials.

findings.requirement: SEC-006/§20.3 — HMAC-signed DeploymentRecord for rollback integrity
findings.description: DeploymentRecord includes an hmac field. signDeploymentRecord() and verifyDeploymentRecord() use HMAC-SHA256 with a key from env var or file (0o600 permissions enforced). All backends sign records before persisting.
When no deploy.yaml exists, resolveEnvironment() returns backend='local', approval='none', costCapUsd=0, source='fallback' — exactly the existing behavior.

findings.requirement: FR-1445/FR-1447 — Health checks with automatic rollback on failure
findings.description: HealthMonitor polls backends at configurable intervals, tracks consecutive failures via SlaTracker, and triggers backend.rollback() when the threshold is reached. Failed rollback escalates with severity 'critical'.

findings.requirement: FR-1459/FR-1460 — Cost tracking integrated into orchestrator
findings.description: The orchestrator performs per-env cost-cap pre-check, records cost via recordCost(), and also invokes CostCapEnforcer for operator-wide daily cap. Cloud backends implement estimateDeployCost() returning structured EstimateResult with line items.

findings.requirement: FR-1466/FR-1469 — Per-deploy structured JSONL logs and deployment record persistence
findings.description: DeployLogger writes JSONL lines to .autonomous-dev/deploy-logs/<deployId>/<component>/<component>.log with log rotation. writeDeploymentRecord() persists signed JSON to .autonomous-dev/deployments/<deployId>.json.
'autonomous-dev deploy backends list' and 'deploy backends describe <name>' commands are implemented with --json flag support.

findings.requirement: FR-1427/FR-1431 — Content-addressed artifact storage with integrity checksum
findings.description: Artifacts are stored at .autonomous-dev/builds/<artifactId>/manifest.json with a sibling checksum.sha256. verifyArtifactChecksum() validates the SHA-256. Note: storage is keyed by ULID (not content-hash) so true content-addressing and artifact deduplication (FR-1428) are not supported.

prd: PRD-015: Extend autonomous-dev-assist for Plugin Chains, Deploy Framework, Cloud Backends, Credential Proxy, Egress Firewall, and Cost Estimation
title: PRD-015 Conformance Audit — autonomous-dev-assist Plugin
summary: The autonomous-dev-assist plugin has implemented most P0 requirements from PRD-015. The six new SKILL.md top-level sections are partially complete: Plugin Chains, Deploy Framework, Cloud Backends, and Credential Proxy are fully present in help/SKILL.md; however, the Egress Firewall section (FR-1506) and Cost Estimation section (FR-1507) are absent — the file ends after Credential Proxy at line 622. The config-guide/SKILL.md has 23 sections including the new chains (19), deploy (20), and cred_proxy (23) sections, but is missing the firewall section (FR-1511) and cost_estimation section (FR-1512). The troubleshoot/SKILL.md has 15 scenarios including new cred-proxy scenarios (14, 15), but is missing the four PRD-mandated new scenarios: chain cycle detected, deploy approval stuck, cost-cap-tripped (deploy-specific), and firewall denial with DNS-refresh lag (FR-1513); the HMAC mismatch warning scenario (FR-1514) is also absent. Agent files are well-implemented: troubleshooter.md has chain and deploy diagnostics with correct file paths (FR-1517, FR-1518, FR-1519); onboarding.md has the four new pause states table and the First Cloud Deploy appendix (FR-1520, FR-1521). Commands are mostly correct: assist.md has the new chains/deploy/security categories and correct glob targets (FR-1522, FR-1523); quickstart.md has --with-cloud flag (FR-1524); eval.md lists all new suites (FR-1525); README.md is updated (FR-1526). Four new runbooks were required; only three exist: chains-runbook.md, deploy-runbook.md, and cred-proxy-runbook.md are present; firewall-runbook.md is missing (FR-1530). The runbook.md See-also index references firewall-runbook.md with an XFAIL comment, confirming it is intentionally deferred. The eval suites are partially filled: chains-eval.yaml has 26 cases (meets ≥20), deploy-eval.yaml has 36 cases (meets ≥30), but cred-proxy-eval.yaml has 9 cases (needs ≥15, FR-1534) and firewall-eval.yaml has 9 cases (needs ≥15, FR-1535). Both security-critical suites are flagged as seed/partial with authoring guidance comments indicating they are incomplete stubs awaiting TDD-025 completion. The eval-config.yaml is correctly updated with all four new suites and proper invocation order (FR-1537). The negative-case requirement (≥5 per suite) is met for chains (multiple) and deploy, but cred-proxy and firewall have 8 and 8 negative/warning cases respectively — technically meeting FR-1538 in count, but below the case_minimum floor of 15 total means the suites are incomplete regardless. No SHA pinning violations found (FR-1540 satisfied). The setup-wizard SKILL.md has the new phase orchestrator covering phases 8, 11-16 including the cloud deploy phases, satisfying FR-1515, but phase 16 is default-skip (opt-in), satisfying FR-1516.

findings.description: skills/help/SKILL.md is missing the 'Egress Firewall' top-level section. The file ends at line 622 after the Credential Proxy section. There is no section explaining nftables/pfctl backends, DNS-refresh loop, per-plugin allowlist, or firewall test command.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/help/SKILL.md:622 (EOF after Credential Proxy; grep for 'nftables|pfctl|DNS.refresh|firewall test' returns 0 matches)
findings.recommendation: Add a '## Egress Firewall' section after Credential Proxy explaining nftables (Linux) and pfctl (macOS) backends, DNS-refresh interval, per-plugin allowlist path, and the firewall test command.

findings.description: skills/help/SKILL.md is missing the 'Cost Estimation' top-level section. No section explains per-cloud estimators, pricing fixture format, override path, or the 'deploy estimate' CLI beyond a single table row in the Deploy Framework section.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/help/SKILL.md: grep 'Cost Estimation|cost_estimation|fixture' returns 0 top-level section matches; only line 170 references 'deploy estimate' as a table row
findings.recommendation: Add a '## Cost Estimation' section explaining per-cloud estimators, pricing fixture schema, override path, and the deploy estimate CLI workflow.

findings.description: skills/config-guide/SKILL.md has only 23 sections. The 'firewall' section (nftables|pfctl backend selection, DNS-refresh interval, per-plugin allowlist file path) is absent. The file goes directly from cred_proxy (Section 23) to Full Example Configuration.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Section 24: firewall' to config-guide/SKILL.md documenting backend selection (nftables|pfctl|disabled), DNS-refresh interval, and per-plugin allowlist file path.

findings.description: skills/config-guide/SKILL.md has no 'cost_estimation' section. The pricing fixture override path, fixture schema, and per-environment cap interaction are not documented.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Section 25: cost_estimation' documenting the pricing fixture override path, per-cloud fixture schema, and how cost_cap_usd interacts with the estimator.

findings.description: skills/troubleshoot/SKILL.md has 15 scenarios but only 2 of the 5 PRD-mandated new scenarios are present. Scenario 14 covers cred-proxy socket permission denied and Scenario 15 covers cred-proxy TTL expiry mid-deploy. Missing: chain cycle detected, deploy approval stuck (awaiting-approval), cost-cap-tripped (deploy-level), and firewall denial with DNS-refresh lag.
findings.recommendation: Add 3 new scenarios to troubleshoot/SKILL.md: (a) chain cycle detected with chains graph recovery, (b) deploy awaiting-approval with deploy approve REQ-NNNNNN command, (c) firewall EHOSTUNREACH from DNS-refresh lag with firewall refresh-dns workaround. These are separate from the existing cost-cap scenario (Scenario 4) which covers the pipeline governance cap, not the deploy-level per-environment cap.

findings.description: skills/troubleshoot/SKILL.md has no scenario for HMAC mismatch in 'chains audit verify' with the explicit 'do not delete the audit log' warning. The warning appears elsewhere (agents/troubleshooter.md, help/SKILL.md) but the standalone troubleshoot scenario is absent.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/skills/troubleshoot/SKILL.md: grep 'HMAC mismatch|do not delete the audit log|chains audit verify' returns 0 matches in troubleshoot SKILL.md
findings.recommendation: Add a scenario titled 'chains audit verify reports HMAC mismatch' with explicit warning: do NOT delete the audit log. Recovery path references chains-runbook.md §3.

findings.description: instructions/firewall-runbook.md does not exist. The PRD mandates a new file covering backend selection (Linux vs macOS), DNS-refresh tuning, allowlist authoring, simulating a deny, reading denied.log, and fail-open disabled mode. The README.md and runbook.md both reference this file but annotate it as pending TDD-025.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/instructions/: ls shows chains-runbook.md, deploy-runbook.md, cred-proxy-runbook.md, runbook.md, cloud-prompt-tree.md — firewall-runbook.md absent. README.md line 93: 'firewall-runbook.md <!-- pending: TDD-025 -->'
findings.recommendation: Create instructions/firewall-runbook.md covering: backend selection, DNS-refresh tuning, allowlist authoring, deny simulation, denied.log reading, and the fail-open development-only mode warning.

findings.description: evals/test-cases/cred-proxy-eval.yaml contains only 9 cases (8 negative/warning seed cases + 1 worked-example command-syntax case). The PRD requires at least 15 cases. The file itself declares case_minimum: 15 and includes authoring guidance comments for TDD-025 to complete it, indicating the suite is intentionally incomplete.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/evals/test-cases/cred-proxy-eval.yaml: grep -c '^    question:' returns 9; file contains comment 'Target: 15 cases total'
findings.recommendation: Complete cred-proxy-eval.yaml to at least 15 cases. Add the 4 missing command-syntax cases and 2 troubleshoot-scenario cases following the id-reservation scheme documented in the file (cred-proxy-command-syntax-002 through -005, cred-proxy-troubleshoot-scenario-001 through -002).

findings.description: evals/test-cases/firewall-eval.yaml contains only 9 cases (8 negative/warning seed cases + 1 worked-example command-syntax case). The PRD requires at least 15 cases. The file declares case_minimum: 15 and contains authoring guidance for TDD-025.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-assist/evals/test-cases/firewall-eval.yaml: grep -c '^    question:' returns 9; file contains comment 'Target: 15 cases total'
findings.recommendation: Complete firewall-eval.yaml to at least 15 cases. Add 4 command-syntax cases and 2 troubleshoot-scenario cases per the id-reservation scheme in the file.

findings.description: skills/help/SKILL.md Agent Factory section lists exactly 18 agents including the 5 TDD-020 additions (standards-meta-reviewer, qa-edge-case-reviewer, ux-ui-reviewer, accessibility-reviewer, rule-set-enforcement-reviewer), each with a one-line description.

skills/help/SKILL.md has a '## Plugin Chains' section covering produces/consumes/egress_allowlist manifest fields, the topological resolver, the four chains CLI commands, and the HMAC audit log with the do-not-delete warning.

skills/help/SKILL.md has a '## Deploy Framework' section covering backends discovery, all 8 deploy CLI commands, the 6-state approval state machine, cost-cap ledger, HealthMonitor, and the prod-always-approval rule.
skills/help/SKILL.md has a '## Cloud Backends' section listing all four plugin names with backend names, target capabilities, tool dependencies, and egress allowlist defaults.
skills/help/SKILL.md has a '## Credential Proxy' section explaining per-cloud scopers, SCM_RIGHTS Unix-socket transport, 15-minute default TTL, auto-revoke, and per-issuance audit hash.

title: PRD-016 Test-Suite Stabilization & Jest Harness Migration — Conformance Audit
summary: The core harness migration (FR-1601–1605) and TDD-014 security test backfill (FR-1620–1628) are fully implemented. The TDD-019 plugin-reload CLI is mostly implemented but the `autonomous-dev plugin reload` subcommand is not wired into `autonomous-dev.sh` as FR-1642 requires. TDD-015 pipeline files were landed at `server/integration/` rather than `server/live-data/` as PRD specified (the SPECs were revised to match, so code follows the revised SPEC). The entire test-failure triage section (FR-1610–1614) has no deliverable — the required `docs/triage/PRD-016-test-failures.md` does not exist. The SPEC reconciliation (FR-1650–1653) is materially incomplete: 25 SPEC files still carry `src/portal/` references, 27 carry `vitest` tokens, and 45 carry Bats references; the CI gate fires red intentionally on the production tree. The ESLint `process.exit` lint rule (FR-1660) is absent from `.eslintrc.cjs`. CI does not use `--runInBand --ci` (FR-1661 partial). JUnit XML reporter (FR-1662) is absent.

findings.description: The required triage matrix `docs/triage/PRD-016-test-failures.md` does not exist. FR-1610 requires this document listing every FAIL suite from the post-migration run with disposition, owner, and linked SPEC/issue. Without it, FR-1611 (FIX ETA buckets), FR-1613 (DELETE approvals), and FR-1614 (minimum suite coverage) are also unsatisfied.
findings.recommendation: Produce the triage matrix at `docs/triage/PRD-016-test-failures.md` covering every FAIL suite per FR-1614 requirements.

findings.description: Numerous `describe.skip` and `it.skip` blocks exist across test files but none carry the required annotation `// SKIP per PRD-016 triage row N: <reason>`. The acceptance criterion for FR-1612 explicitly requires this inline comment format so rationale travels with the code.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/tests/parallel/conflict-classifier.test.ts:382, tests/parallel/retry-handler.test.ts:379, plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/integration/__tests__/cost-pipeline.test.ts:180 (uses 'PRD-016 triage: SKIP-WITH-NOTE' but omits required row number)
findings.recommendation: Annotate every skip introduced by this PRD with `// SKIP per PRD-016 triage row N: <reason>`, linking to the triage matrix row.

findings.description: The ESLint config `.eslintrc.cjs` has no rule banning `process.exit(` in test files. The `overrides` block for `tests/**/*.ts` and `**/*.test.ts` relaxes existing rules but adds no `no-process-exit` rule with severity `error`. The CI lint step runs ESLint but will not catch test files that reintroduce `process.exit`.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/.eslintrc.cjs:40-49 — overrides block for test files has no process.exit constraint
findings.recommendation: Add `'no-restricted-syntax': ['error', { selector: "CallExpression[callee.object.name='process'][callee.property.name='exit']", message: 'process.exit() is forbidden in test files (PRD-016 FR-1660)' }]` to the test-file overrides block.

findings.description: FR-1642 requires that `autonomous-dev plugin reload` (invoked via the main `autonomous-dev.sh` dispatcher) resolves to the plugin reload handler. The `autonomous-dev.sh` case statement has no `plugin` subcommand — it will fall through to the `*` unknown-command error. The feature is only reachable via the standalone `reload-plugins plugin reload <name>` binary, not via `autonomous-dev plugin reload`. The SPEC-030-3-01 acknowledged the `src/cli/ → intake/cli/` path change but did not update the `autonomous-dev` entrypoint.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/autonomous-dev.sh:886-971 — case statement lists install-daemon, daemon, kill-switch, circuit-breaker, config, request, reconcile, agent, override-verification; no `plugin` case
findings.recommendation: Add a `plugin` case to `autonomous-dev.sh` that routes to `node bin/reload-plugins.js plugin reload "$@"`, satisfying the acceptance criterion `autonomous-dev plugin reload resolves`.

findings.description: The CI workflow runs `npm test -- --coverage` which maps to `jest --passWithNoTests --coverage`. It does not pass `--runInBand` or `--ci` as FR-1661 requires. The `--ci` flag disables watch mode, adds stricter snapshot handling, and signals test infrastructure; `--runInBand` is the serialization mode the PRD mandates for a clean, reproducible gate. The old 'known failures' carve-out appears to be absent (not found in the workflow file).
findings.recommendation: Change the CI step to `npx jest --runInBand --ci --coverage` (or update the `test` script in package.json to include `--runInBand --ci`).

findings.description: SPEC reconciliation is materially incomplete. The spec-reconciliation script reports: 25 SPEC files still carry `src/portal/` path references, 27 still contain `vitest` tokens, 45 still contain Bats references. The `verify-spec-reconciliation.sh` script confirms this by exiting non-zero on the production tree. The triage doc acknowledges only PRs #95/#97 amended ~10 SPECs; the PRD target was 0 residual for all three token classes.
findings.evidence: Running `bash scripts/verify-spec-reconciliation.sh` from repo root exits with `FAIL: src/portal/ references remain`, `FAIL: vitest references remain`, `FAIL: bats references remain`; PRD-016-spec-reconciliation.md:5 states reconciliation is partial

findings.description: FR-1630 specifies three pipeline files at `plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/live-data/{cost,heartbeat,log}-pipeline.ts`. This is a PRD-to-code path discrepancy that was not reconciled back into the PRD.
findings.evidence: `ls /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/live-data/` → 'Directory does not exist'; files found at `server/integration/cost-pipeline.ts`, `server/integration/heartbeat-pipeline.ts`, `server/integration/log-pipeline.ts`

findings.description: No JUnit XML reporter is configured in `jest.config.cjs` or referenced in the CI workflow. FR-1662 (P2) requires the CI workflow to publish a JUnit XML report for GitHub Actions UI per-suite result display.
