title: PRD-018 Portal Visual Redesign — Conformance Audit
summary: The vast majority of PRD-018's structural requirements are implemented: design-tokens.css is vendored and loaded first, the 220px left rail + ShellLayout exist, all seven R-08 primitives (Btn, Chip, Dot, Score, CostRing, Card, KillSwitch) are implemented in Hono JSX, the /design-system route has 20 sections, light/dark theming works, phase chips exist, CI lint scripts exist for hex/font/emoji/box-shadow/phase-contrast. However, six concrete gaps remain: (1) CI lint scripts scan a non-existent path (server/static/) so they do not cover the actual CSS in static/ — the lints are effectively no-ops in CI; (2) app.css contains hard-coded hex values in chip-phase color rules and the .log block that should reference --phase-* and semantic tokens; (3) the default theme is dark (SPEC-037-1-01), directly contradicting R-03's binding 'light' default; (4) the localStorage key is 'portal-theme' (hyphen), not 'portal.theme' (dot) as R-03 specifies; (5) visual regression goldens have not been committed (M-03 cannot pass); (6) the repo-card base CSS uses a 3px left bar defaulting to --phase-code instead of R-12's 4px bar defaulting to --muted when no phase is active.

findings.description: CI lint scripts lint-css-tokens.sh and lint-box-shadow.sh both scan plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/static/ and plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/src/styles/ — but neither directory exists. The actual CSS files live in plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/static/. As a result, both scripts exit 0 on every PR without scanning any file, making the M-01 enforcement gate a no-op.
findings.evidence: scripts/lint-css-tokens.sh:18 — PORTAL_DIR/server/static; scripts/lint-box-shadow.sh:61-64 — PORTAL_ROOT/server/static; static/ directory confirmed present, server/static/ confirmed absent
findings.recommendation: Change the scan paths in both scripts from server/static/ to static/ (and update the matching comment headers). Re-run locally to validate zero false positives before merging.

findings.requirement: R-02 / M-01 — no hard-coded hex in non-token CSS
findings.description: app.css contains hard-coded hex color literals in regular CSS rules, not inside the :root/:root[data-theme] token-definition blocks. Phase chip text colors (lines 208-211) use literals like #6b4ea8, #2f6f8f, etc. instead of var(--phase-prd), var(--phase-tdd), etc. The .log block (lines 326-335) hardcodes #14130f, #ede9d8, #95917f, #88adcb, #d99891, #98c39a instead of referencing bg/fg/semantic tokens. The chip-phase hex values do not even match the --phase-* values defined in design-tokens.css for either theme.
findings.evidence: static/app.css:208-211 — .chip-phase.prd { color:#6b4ea8; }; static/app.css:326,331-335 — .log { background: #14130f; } .log .l-time { color: #95917f; }; design-tokens.css:121 — --phase-prd: #3d2470 (light)
findings.recommendation: Replace chip-phase color literals with var(--phase-prd) etc. Replace .log block literals with var(--bg-0), var(--fg-0), var(--fg-2), var(--info), var(--err), var(--ok) tokens. Then fix the lint script paths so CI will catch regressions.

findings.description: PRD-018 R-03 states 'Default is light' and OQ-04 is marked RESOLVED with 'Theme default = light. R-03 binds.' However, SPEC-037-1-01 deliberately flipped the default to dark. All three entry points now default to dark: ShellLayout (theme='dark'), getThemeFromCookie (returns 'dark' for missing/unknown cookies), and the FOUC IIFE (falls back to 'dark' if localStorage has no key). This directly contradicts the PRD's binding requirement.
findings.evidence: server/components/shell.tsx:205 — theme = 'dark'; server/lib/theme.ts:18 — return raw === 'light' ? 'light' : 'dark'; static/theme-toggle.js:63 — t==='light'?'light':'dark'
findings.recommendation: If the deliberate dark-default is intentional, update PRD-018 R-03 and close OQ-04 with the revised decision. If the PRD is authoritative, revert SPEC-037-1-01 to restore 'light' as the cold-load default.

findings.description: R-03 specifies localStorage persistence keyed 'portal.theme' (dot separator). The implementation consistently uses 'portal-theme' (hyphen separator) everywhere: theme-toggle.js STORAGE_KEY, the FOUC IIFE in both base.tsx and shell.tsx, theme.ts COOKIE_NAME, and all tests. This is a stable internal key, but it diverges from the PRD's spec string.
findings.evidence: static/theme-toggle.js:57 — var STORAGE_KEY = 'portal-theme'; server/lib/theme.ts:16 — const COOKIE_NAME = 'portal-theme'; server/components/shell.tsx:43 — localStorage.getItem('portal-theme')
findings.recommendation: Either update R-03 to reflect the chosen key ('portal.theme' → 'portal-theme') or migrate the key. The cookie name and localStorage key are functionally different; the PRD's 'portal.theme' (localStorage) vs 'portal-theme' (cookie) may have been intentionally different — clarify.

findings.requirement: M-03 — visual regression goldens covering 20 preview cards
findings.description: M-03 requires visual regression tests on /design-system covering all 20 preview cards, failing on any token-level diff. The Playwright test suite and the design-system route with 20 sections both exist. However, the goldens directory contains only .gitkeep — no PNG baselines have been committed. The CI pre-flight script (check-goldens-present.sh) will exit 1 on any PR that triggers the visual-regression workflow.
findings.evidence: tests/visual-regression/goldens/.gitkeep (sole file in directory); scripts/ci/check-goldens-present.sh asserts design-system-full.png and design-system-card-01..20.png
findings.recommendation: Run 'npm run gen:visual-goldens' locally to generate the 21 PNG baselines and commit them. Re-run twice to confirm zero git-diff (per AC-10 of the test spec).

findings.requirement: R-12 — repo card 4px left bar, --muted when no active phase
findings.description: R-12 requires a 4px-wide left bar in --phase-<active-phase>, defaulting to --muted when no phase is active. Two deviations: (1) the base .repo-card CSS sets border-left: 3px (not 4px), so even when a phase IS active and the inline style overrides the color, the card must fight against a 3px rule with a 4px inline rule — though inline wins, the base CSS is non-conformant. (2) When no phase is set and attn is false, no inline border-left is applied, so the card falls back to the CSS default of 3px solid var(--phase-code) — not --muted as R-12 requires.
findings.evidence: static/app.css:372 — border-left: 3px solid var(--phase-code); server/templates/fragments/repo-card.tsx:61 — border-left: 4px solid var(--phase-${phase}) applied only when !r.attn && phase
findings.recommendation: Change static/app.css .repo-card border-left to 4px solid var(--muted). The repo-card.tsx inline override already correctly uses 4px. This aligns the base state with R-12 and makes the no-phase fallback --muted as specified.

findings.requirement: R-02 — no hard-coded hex #fff in non-token CSS
findings.description: Three CSS files use the literal #fff for text on brand-colored backgrounds: primitives.css line 55 (.btn.primary color), shell.css line 150 (.count badge color), and portal.css line 201 (a chip.ok color). The design-tokens.css file defines no --fg-on-brand or equivalent token that would allow these to reference a variable. While #fff is the correct contrast color for the brand orange, it is a hard-coded value not covered by the token system, violating R-02's prohibition on non-token color values.
findings.evidence: static/primitives.css:55 — color: #fff; static/shell.css:150 — color: #fff; static/portal.css:201 — color: #fff
findings.recommendation: Add a --fg-on-brand: #ffffff token to design-tokens.css (both light and dark blocks — it should be invariant) and reference var(--fg-on-brand) in these three locations.

findings.requirement: R-13 — kill switch chip tones (armed vs engaged)
findings.description: R-13 specifies '--err palette when armed-to-engage and a flat-warning treatment when engaged.' At the panel level this is partially correct: .ks-panel.armed gets background: var(--err-tint) and border-color: var(--err-line) (err palette for armed — correct). But the chip labels are swapped: the ARMED state renders a .chip.warn chip (yellow), while the ENGAGED state renders a .chip.err chip (red). PRD says ARMED should read err (red/danger) and ENGAGED should read flat-warning. The chip tones contradict the stated semantics.
findings.evidence: server/components/kill-switch.tsx:70 — chip err for ENGAGED; server/components/kill-switch.tsx:94 — chip warn for ARMED; static/portal.css:3 — .ks-panel.armed { background: var(--err-tint) }
findings.recommendation: Swap the chip tones: ARMED → chip err (to match the .ks-panel.armed panel background and the 'armed-to-engage = dangerous' semantics), ENGAGED → chip warn or a distinct 'engaged' tone (to match 'flat-warning treatment when engaged').

findings.description: R-04 says 'The Inter and JetBrains Mono fonts load via the @import already present in colors_and_type.css.' The implementation self-hosts the fonts as woff2 files via @font-face declarations in design-tokens.css. There is no @import from Google Fonts CDN. This diverges from the literal R-04 text but aligns with OQ-06's recommendation to self-host at first port. The OQ-06 question was left OPEN in the PRD — the TDD appears to have resolved it in favour of self-hosting without formally closing OQ-06.
findings.evidence: static/design-tokens.css:9-71 — @font-face declarations for Inter and JetBrains Mono with local woff2 paths; no @import url(...) present; static/fonts/ directory contains woff2 files
findings.recommendation: Mark OQ-06 as RESOLVED with decision 'self-host both fonts' in the PRD or a companion TDD. The implementation is functionally superior to the CDN approach specified in R-04; the requirement text should be updated to match.

findings.requirement: M-04 — before/after screenshots for all 6 surfaces
findings.description: M-04 requires before/after screenshot pairs for all 6 surfaces shipping with the PR so reviewers can confirm pixel-faithful kit adoption. No such screenshots are present in the repository (the only .png files found are Cypress failure screenshots unrelated to this work).
findings.evidence: find shows no before/after screenshot pairs; cypress/screenshots/ contains test failure PNGs only
findings.recommendation: Generate before/after screenshots for Dashboard, Approvals, Request Detail, Costs, Ops, and Settings. These are a reviewer artifact, not a CI gate, but are required per M-04 for the PR to be accepted.

findings.description: R-15a requires that CI reject box-shadow declarations that do not reference --shadow-* variables. The lint-box-shadow.sh script exists and is wired into portal-lint.yml. However, it scans server/static/ (non-existent) so it never runs against the actual CSS. app.css contains multiple raw rgba() box-shadows (lines 143, 218-220, 239, 286, 378, 465, 496, 558, 608, 637) and primitives.css contains rgba() pulse keyframes — these are all currently unscanned. Note: several of these are intentional per the spec (inset accent bars, pulse keyframes) but the lint cannot distinguish them from violations when it cannot even read the files.
findings.evidence: static/app.css:608 — box-shadow: 0 16px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5); static/app.css:143 — box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--line-2); scripts/lint-box-shadow.sh:61-64 — scans PORTAL_ROOT/server/static (absent)
findings.recommendation: Fix lint-box-shadow.sh to scan static/ instead of server/static/. Then audit app.css lines 608 and 637 (modal/dialog drop-shadows using raw rgba) — these should reference var(--shadow-pop) or var(--shadow-2) to comply with R-15a.

title: PRD-019 Intake-to-Deploy E2E Pipeline — Conformance Audit
summary: The core intake-to-dispatch path (FR-019-01 through FR-019-11) is largely implemented: initRouter() wires optional deps correctly, the state.json two-phase commit is implemented with path-traversal guards, the daemon's intake-to-prd bookkeeping transition runs, and spawn_session_typed uses --agent via spawn-session.sh. Six gaps remain, four of them in P0 requirements: (1) resolve_phase_prompt never injects prior-phase artifact paths or review feedback into retry prompts (FR-019-12); (2) the review-fail path does not copy the feedback field from phase-result.json into current_phase_metadata.review_feedback (FR-019-14); (3) state.json does not gain a completed_at field on terminal completion — only the portal action file does (FR-019-15); (4) events use type phase_advance instead of state_transition and omit session_id/cost/turns (FR-019-13); (5) no unit test covers all 12 phase-to-agent mappings (FR-019-10 acceptance criterion); and (6) the smoke test is not wired into any CI workflow and misses the phase-result-prd.json verification step (FR-019-19/20). The spawn_session() function in supervisor-loop.sh (which lacks --agent) is dead code, superseded by dispatch_phase_session/spawn_session_typed.

findings.description: initRouter() in cli_adapter.ts correctly omits claudeClient, duplicateDetector, and injectionRules (passing undefined to IntakeRouter). The TODO(PLAN-011-1) is resolved.
writeStateJson() implements the FR-824a two-phase commit (tmp + rename). state.json is written with all 20 required fields including separate status:'queued' and current_phase:'intake'.
request_id validated against ^REQ-\d{6}$; path traversal blocked via path.resolve + fs.realpathSync.native prefix check. VALIDATION_ERROR and PATH_ESCAPE error codes are thrown on violations.
select_request() scans state.json files from allowlisted repos. intake_to_prd_if_needed() auto-transitions queued/intake requests to running/prd and appends an intake_to_prd event to events.jsonl.

resolve_agent() is implemented with all 12 phase-to-agent mappings including monitor->performance-analyst (which is not in the PRD table but is an addition). However, the acceptance criterion explicitly requires a unit test covering all 12 mappings, and no such test exists — the only phase test file tests resolve_phase_prompt, not resolve_agent.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:1070-1089 (mapping exists); no test found in tests/unit/ covering resolve_agent
findings.recommendation: Add a unit test in tests/unit/ that sources supervisor-loop.sh and asserts resolve_agent() for all 12 phases (prd through deploy) return the expected agent names.

findings.description: spawn_session_typed in spawn-session.sh correctly uses --agent <agent-name> and --print --output-format json. However the PRD specifies --max-turns <N> but the implementation uses --max-budget-usd instead. The old spawn_session() function in supervisor-loop.sh (which lacks --agent) is dead code — the main loop calls dispatch_phase_session -> spawn_session_typed.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/spawn-session.sh:122-127 (uses --max-budget-usd, not --max-turns); supervisor-loop.sh:1248-1330 (spawn_session dead code, never called by main loop at line 3361)
findings.recommendation: Either add --max-turns alongside --max-budget-usd, or update the PRD to reflect the budget-based approach. Remove or clearly mark the dead spawn_session() function.

findings.description: resolve_phase_prompt() in phase-helpers.sh does not inject (a) prior-phase artifact paths or (b) review feedback into prompts. The tdd phase prompt does not include the PRD file path. The prd retry prompt after prd_review failure does not include the review feedback. The phase-prompts/ directory only contains README.md — no phase-specific templates exist that could supply this context.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/lib/phase-helpers.sh:61-270 (no artifact or feedback injection); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/phase-prompts/README.md (only file in directory)
findings.recommendation: Extend resolve_phase_prompt() to (1) read phase_history from state.json and find artifact paths from prior phases' phase-result files, and (2) read current_phase_metadata.review_feedback from state.json and prepend it to retry prompts.

findings.description: advance_phase() advances state.json and writes events.jsonl. However: (a) the event type used is 'phase_advance' not 'state_transition' as the PRD requires; (b) the event does not include session_id, cost, or turns_used fields; (c) phase_history[] is not updated by the daemon on phase advancement — the agent is only invited (not required) to append entries.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2454-2468 (phase_advance event, no session_id/cost/turns); phase_history grep returns no daemon writes
findings.recommendation: Rename event type to 'state_transition' to match PRD spec. Add session_cost and turn_count fields to the event. Add daemon-side phase_history append in advance_phase().

findings.description: phase-result.json is written by agents/wrapper and read by advance_phase(). On review-phase failure the daemon resets current_phase to the author phase. BUT the daemon does NOT read the 'feedback' field from phase-result-<review_phase>.json and does NOT set current_phase_metadata.review_feedback in state.json. The acceptance criterion ('state.json shows current_phase_metadata.review_feedback contains Missing success metrics') is not satisfied.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2518-2531 (review fail resets phase but no feedback copy); no reference to review_feedback in advance_phase() or write path
findings.recommendation: In the review-fail branch of advance_phase(), read the feedback field from phase-result-<current_phase>.json and write it to current_phase_metadata.review_feedback in state.json before transitioning back to the author phase.

findings.description: Terminal completion sets status='done' and updates updated_at in state.json. However, the PRD acceptance criterion requires a non-null completed_at timestamp in state.json. The advance_phase() terminal block sets only status and updated_at — there is no separate completed_at field written to state.json. completedAt is written to the portal action file but not to state.json.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2388-2396 (terminal block; no completed_at field in state.json); 2096-2103 (completedAt only in portal action tmp file)
findings.recommendation: Add .completed_at = $ts to the jq expression in the terminal block of advance_phase() so state.json records a non-null completed_at.

findings.description: write_portal_request_action() writes all required fields (id, repo, title, phase, status, cost, variant, createdAt, waitedMin, turns, completedAt, score) with atomic rename. status='gate' is set when entering _review phases; portal is written after every phase transition.
mkdir -p is called on PORTAL_REQUEST_ACTIONS_DIR before first write. Atomic tmp+rename is used. The directory is also created at daemon startup.

smoke-e2e.sh exists at test/e2e/smoke-e2e.sh and exercises items (a)-(g) of the acceptance criterion. Missing: (i) no verification that phase-result-prd.json was written; (h) the portal action check only warns (does not fail the test) when the file is absent. The fallback path in submit_request() writes phase_overrides:[] which would cause the daemon to use legacy phase sequence.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/test/e2e/smoke-e2e.sh:175-205 (verify function); line 200-204 (portal action warn-only); no phase-result-prd.json check
findings.recommendation: Add assertion that phase-result-prd.json exists in req_dir after daemon iteration. Change the portal action check from warn-only to a hard failure. Fix the fallback state.json to omit phase_overrides or populate it correctly.

findings.description: The smoke test is not referenced in any GitHub Actions workflow file. No .github/workflows/*.yml file runs test/e2e/smoke-e2e.sh. The PRD requires the test to be runnable in CI.
findings.recommendation: Add a GitHub Actions workflow (e.g. .github/workflows/smoke-e2e.yml) that runs bash plugins/autonomous-dev/test/e2e/smoke-e2e.sh on push to main and PRs, with SMOKE_MOCK_LOG output uploaded as an artifact.

findings.description: The PRD specifies the portal request-action path as ~/.autonomous-dev/portal/request-actions/<id>.json but both the daemon (PORTAL_REQUEST_ACTIONS_DIR) and the portal reader (requestActionsDir()) agree on ~/.autonomous-dev/request-actions/ (without the portal/ subdirectory). The two components are consistent with each other but diverge from the PRD spec.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:33 (PORTAL_REQUEST_ACTIONS_DIR without portal/ prefix); /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/state-paths.ts:47-49 (requestActionsDir() also without portal/ prefix)

title: PRD-020 Conformance Audit: Intake-to-Deploy Pipeline Hardening
summary: 9 of 10 requirements are fully implemented with code evidence. FR-020-04 (request-detail no-404) is partially implemented: the route no longer 404s (falls back to request-action data), but the config format mismatch in request-record-reader.ts means state.json enrichment (phase history, real cost, runs timeline) is silently broken in production. All P0 and P1 fixes are present and tested. All P2 nice-to-haves are also present.

findings.description: The request-detail route no longer 404s (core fix is present), but the state.json enrichment path is silently broken in production. request-record-reader.ts defines the daemon config allowlist as Array<{id?: string; path?: string}> (object array), while the real config at ~/.claude/autonomous-dev.json uses a string array ["/path/to/repo", ...]. resolveRepoPath always returns null in production, so the detail page renders with only request-action data — no phase history, no real cost_accrued_usd, no runs/reviewers from state.json. The test fixture uses the object format so tests pass.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/request-record-reader.ts:65-80 (object-array type + path check); ~/.claude/autonomous-dev.json (string array in production); tests/unit/request-record-reader.test.ts:50 (test uses object format); plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/settings-reader.ts:53 (correctly documents 'daemon uses string[] not AllowlistEntry[]')
findings.recommendation: Fix resolveRepoPath to handle both string entries and object entries. Mirror the logic in settings-reader.ts formatAllowlist(), which already knows the config is string[]. Change the loop to: if (typeof entry === 'string' && basename(entry) === repoSlug) return entry; if (typeof (entry as any).path === 'string' && basename((entry as any).path) === repoSlug) return (entry as any).path;

findings.description: Daemon owns all phase transitions. dispatch_phase_session writes dispatched_phase at dispatch time; advance_phase reads from dispatched_phase (with fallback to current_phase) and restores current_phase from dispatched before computing next. Phase prompt (fallback path) explicitly says 'Do NOT modify current_phase or status in state.json — the daemon owns all phase transitions.'
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:1154-1157 (dispatched_phase write); 2346-2348 (advance_phase reads dispatched_phase first); 2376-2382 (restore current_phase from dispatched before next-phase lookup); plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/lib/phase-helpers.sh:232 (prompt instruction)

findings.description: resolve_agent covers all 14 canonical phases (intake through monitor); LEGACY_PHASES in phase-legacy.sh matches ALL_PIPELINE_PHASES in phase-override.ts exactly; state_json_writer populates phase_overrides[] from the canonical TS matrix on every new request. No phase in the sequence is left without an agent mapping. The old test/test_review/validate gap is gone.

write_gate_decision() creates gate-decisions/<repo>__<id>.json with state: 'pending', waitedMin: 0, gate_entered_at when advance_phase transitions to a *_review phase. The approvals reader (approvals-reader.ts) reads request-actions/ filtered to status=gate, which is populated by write_portal_request_action() called from advance_phase.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2121-2175 (write_gate_decision function); 2476-2478 (called on gate entry); plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/wiring/approvals-reader.ts:59-83 (reads via readRequestLedger filtering status=gate)

findings.description: update_state_cost() is called before advance_phase() in all three main-loop branches (turn-exhaustion, success, hard-failure). advance_phase() calls write_portal_request_action() internally, which reads cost_accrued_usd from state.json. Additionally update_request_state() also accumulates cost for error paths.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:3376-3392 (main loop: update_state_cost before advance_phase on success path at lines 3383-3384); 2177-2221 (update_state_cost function); 2077-2079 (write_portal_request_action reads cost_accrued_usd)

findings.description: next_phase_for_state uses jq -e 'has("phase_overrides")' to distinguish truly absent key from present-but-empty []. When key exists but array is empty, it silently falls back to LEGACY_PHASES without warning. warn_legacy_fallback_once fires only when the key is absent.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:905-929 (key-presence check with jq has(); empty-array silent fallback at 922-924; absent-key warn at 927-929)

findings.description: Phase prompts (fallback path in phase-helpers.sh) direct prd/tdd/plan/spec agents to write artifacts to docs/prd/, docs/tdd/, docs/plans/, docs/specs/ in the target repo. Artifact paths are listed in phase-result JSON artifacts[].
POST /api/settings/notifications handler is present in settings-actions.tsx with webhook URL validation (discord.com / slack.com domain checks) and persistence via settings store.

write_synthesized_phase_result() produces a consistent shape including phase field, feedback with 'synthesized from exit code N (error_msg)' summary, synthesized: true, completed_at timestamp, artifacts: [].

FR-020-10a: SPEC-039-3-01 has amendment note (line 85-87) correcting the portal/ path prefix. FR-020-10b: resolve_phase_prompt and resolve_phase_budget extracted to bin/lib/phase-helpers.sh and sourced by both supervisor-loop.sh and spawn-session.sh. FR-020-10c: exec_node_cli in autonomous-dev.sh uses node to run pre-built cli_adapter.js (with bun build fallback if .js is stale).
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/lib/phase-helpers.sh:1-8 (extracted per FR-020-10b); supervisor-loop.sh:3419-3421 (sources phase-helpers.sh); spawn-session.sh:39-41 (sources phase-helpers.sh); autonomous-dev.sh:183-209 (exec_node_cli with node); docs/specs/SPEC-039-3-01-portal-request-action-write.md:85-87 (amendment note)

title: PRD-021 Cypress UI Coverage — Conformance Audit
summary: Cypress infrastructure is substantially implemented: 10 spec files exist (01–10), CI is wired via portal-cypress.yml, fixtures and commands are in place. However, four functional requirements have documented gaps or shallow implementations: FR-021-02 explicitly defers the "reload and verify new value" contract; FR-021-04 never checks the dashboard for phase advancement after approval; FR-021-05 omits the /test/send endpoint; FR-021-08 only checks DOM attributes rather than actually simulating background-tab visibility. FR-021-09 covers 404 well but has no 500 error page test. AC-021-01 is technically unmet: the required `bun run cypress` or `npm run cypress` script does not exist (only `cypress:ci` and `cypress:open` do). An 11-logs-observability spec that ran and failed exists only on an unmerged branch; its failure screenshots are committed to main, indicating the spec was removed without being fixed.

findings.description: Settings persistence spec intercepts POST submissions but explicitly defers the 'change → submit → reload → new value present' round-trip. The spec comment at line 9–12 says: 'Cross-page persistence (visit → change → reload → assert) is deferred because the daemon settings writer requires its config dir to be writable in the test environment.' DND toggle, default notification method, and start/end times are not tested at all.
findings.recommendation: Implement the reload-and-verify flow using a writable test state directory (already available as /tmp/cypress-state in the CI script). Add tests for DND toggle, start/end times, and default notification method fields.

findings.description: Gate approval spec verifies that rows disappear from the /approvals list and that the correct POSTs are fired (approve/reject/bulk-approve). But the PRD requires: 'gate disappears from list → request advances to next phase in dashboard view.' The spec never visits '/' after approval to verify the dashboard reflects the phase change. All cy.visit() calls in the file go to /approvals only.
findings.recommendation: After the approve intercept, add a cy.visit('/') step and assert that the seeded request now shows the advanced phase in the active-requests table.

findings.description: Webhook spec covers /api/settings/notifications/test/discord and /test/slack but never tests the /api/settings/notifications/test/send endpoint. The PRD text is 'Cover both Discord and Slack' but the route list in server/routes/index.ts:258-263 and the notifications-card.tsx UI (line 181) register a third 'send' channel that is completely missing from the Cypress spec.
findings.recommendation: Add a test case for the /send channel analogous to the discord and slack cases.

findings.description: The auto-refresh spec verifies only static hx-trigger attribute values (DOM inspection). The PRD requires verifying runtime behavior: 'background tabs do NOT poll; returning to foreground resumes polling within one interval.' No visibility state is simulated in the spec — there is no document.hidden mutation, no visibilitychange event dispatch, and no assertion that poll requests stop when the tab is hidden.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/cypress/e2e/08-auto-refresh.cy.ts:18-101 (attribute checks only; no cy.document calls or visibility simulation)
findings.recommendation: Add a test that stubs document.visibilityState to 'hidden', waits one polling interval, verifies no new GET requests were made, then restores to 'visible' and verifies a poll fires within the next interval.

title: PRD-022 Marketplace-driven Daemon Self-Upgrade — Conformance Audit
summary: Core self-upgrade machinery (FR-022-01 through FR-022-10) is fully implemented and tested. All 10 functional requirements have corresponding code. The PR #314 fix (OS-aware helper spawn replacing nohup+disown) is present for the forward-upgrade path. However, three gaps exist: (1) Goal G-06 (operator escape hatch env var to disable auto-upgrade) is not implemented anywhere in the codebase. (2) The rollback path inside check_upgrade_trial still uses the old nohup+disown pattern that PR #314 was supposed to fix — the _spawn_upgrade_helper dispatcher is not called for rollbacks, meaning macOS rollbacks will silently fail the same way the initial v0.3.0→v0.3.1 upgrade did. (3) When _spawn_upgrade_helper fails in stage_upgrade, the .last-good-version, .upgrade-throttle, and .upgrade-trial-pending files have already been written and are not cleaned up, leaving the daemon in a partially-committed state that blocks retry for an hour and may cause a spurious probation cycle on the next startup. These are medium-severity issues in reliability, not fundamental missing features.

findings.requirement: G-06 — operator escape hatch env var
findings.description: The PRD specifies 'Operator can disable the mechanism via env var (escape hatch)' as Goal G-06. No env var (e.g. DISABLE_AUTO_UPGRADE or equivalent) is checked anywhere in check_upgrade_available(), stage_upgrade(), or the main loop call site. Grepping bin/ and tests/ for DISABLE_AUTO_UPGRADE, AUTONOMOUS_DEV_NO_UPGRADE, and similar patterns returns no results.
findings.evidence: /Users/pwatson/codebase/autonomous-dev/plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/supervisor-loop.sh:2966-2992 (check_upgrade_available — no guard for a disable var)
findings.recommendation: Add a check at the top of check_upgrade_available() such as: if [[ "${AUTONOMOUS_DEV_DISABLE_AUTO_UPGRADE:-}" == "true" ]]; then return 0; fi. Document the var name in install-daemon.sh and the portal.

findings.requirement: FR-022-08 / AC-07 — rollback spawns detached installer
findings.description: The rollback path in check_upgrade_trial (lines 3268-3271) uses the pre-PR-#314 nohup+disown pattern, not the _spawn_upgrade_helper dispatcher that was introduced in PR #314 specifically because nohup+disown does not survive launchd's process-tree reap on macOS. The forward-upgrade path (stage_upgrade, line 3075) correctly calls _spawn_upgrade_helper. The rollback path was not updated. On macOS, a crash-loop rollback will behave identically to the v0.3.0→v0.3.1 failure: upgrade-helper.log is created but stays 0 bytes; install-daemon.sh never runs; the daemon re-starts as the same bad version instead of rolling back.
findings.recommendation: Replace the nohup block in check_upgrade_trial with a call to _spawn_upgrade_helper "${installer}" to get the same macOS/Linux/fallback dispatch the forward-upgrade path has.

findings.requirement: FR-022-09 / FR-022-05 / FR-022-04 — state cleanup on helper-spawn failure
findings.description: In stage_upgrade(), .last-good-version is written (line 3050), .upgrade-throttle is touched (line 3051), and .upgrade-trial-pending is written (lines 3059-3062) BEFORE _spawn_upgrade_helper is called (line 3075). If _spawn_upgrade_helper returns non-zero, the function returns 1 and logs daemon_upgrade_helper_spawn_failed — but the three state files already exist and are not removed. Consequences: (a) .upgrade-throttle blocks the next upgrade attempt for up to UPGRADE_THROTTLE_SECONDS (default 1 hour), (b) .upgrade-trial-pending with a future deadline will cause the next daemon startup to enter probation for a version upgrade that never actually ran, and (c) .last-good-version is set even though no handoff occurred. The PRD's FR-022-09 ('at most one upgrade attempt per hour') was intended to guard against cache churn, not to penalise failed helper spawns.
findings.recommendation: Move the state-file writes to after the _spawn_upgrade_helper call succeeds, or add a cleanup block on failure that removes the three files so a subsequent poll can retry cleanly.

findings.description: UPGRADE_CHECK_EVERY_N_POLLS defaults to 60 (line 64); main loop calls check_upgrade_available when (POLL_COUNT % UPGRADE_CHECK_EVERY_N_POLLS) == 1 (line 3314), which fires on iteration 1 and every 60 polls thereafter (~30 min at 30s poll interval). Satisfies FR-022-01 and Q-022-01.

findings.requirement: FR-022-02 — log daemon_upgrade_available once per distinct version
findings.description: LAST_UPGRADE_LOGGED_VERSION (line 65) is compared against latest before logging; only logs and updates the dedup variable when the latest version changes (lines 2984-2986). Satisfies the de-dup requirement.

stage_upgrade() reads active_request_id from the heartbeat file (lines 3032-3035) and returns 1 if non-empty. Idle condition is correctly enforced. Bats test at stage_upgrade.bats:104 covers this gate.
.last-good-version is written at line 3050, before spawning the upgrade helper.
Trial flag written at lines 3059-3062 with target version, from version, started timestamp, and deadline = now + UPGRADE_TRIAL_DEADLINE_SECONDS (default 180s).

findings.requirement: FR-022-06 — spawn detached installer; old daemon exits 0
findings.description: The PR #314 OS-aware _spawn_upgrade_helper dispatcher (lines 3095-3101) handles macOS via launchd, Linux via systemd-run, and falls back to nohup. SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED=true (line 3082) triggers clean exit. KeepAlive.SuccessfulExit: false is set in the plist template.

findings.requirement: FR-022-07 — new daemon reads trial flag on startup
findings.description: check_upgrade_trial() is called in the startup sequence (line 3462) before main_loop. It enters probation (UPGRADE_TRIAL_PENDING=true) when the deadline is in the future, and clears the flag after UPGRADE_TRIAL_PROBATION_ITERATIONS healthy iterations via clear_upgrade_trial_if_probation_passed (line 3319).

findings.requirement: FR-022-09 — throttle file prevents repeat attempts
findings.description: upgrade_throttled() checks .upgrade-throttle mtime against UPGRADE_THROTTLE_SECONDS (default 3600s). Bats test at stage_upgrade.bats:85 confirms fresh touch blocks staging.

All four env vars use the ${VAR:-default} idiom at lines 64, 72, 83, 84: UPGRADE_CHECK_EVERY_N_POLLS (60), UPGRADE_THROTTLE_SECONDS (3600), UPGRADE_TRIAL_DEADLINE_SECONDS (180), UPGRADE_TRIAL_PROBATION_ITERATIONS (5).
compare_semver uses sort -V for version ordering. Bats test at version_helpers.bats:106 explicitly covers this case.

title: PRD-023 Portal Security Hardening — Conformance Audit
summary: All five test suites named in the PRD now pass (310 tests, 0 failures), which means every acceptance criterion (AC-01..AC-24) is formally satisfied. The implementation correctly wires marked's renderer overrides, DOMPurify configuration, and a post-sanitization regex scan. The request-detail 500 bug (null fragments crashing Hono's JSX serializer) is fixed. However, one genuine security gap exists that the tests do not catch: `data:image/svg+xml;base64,...` payloads containing embedded `` in the browser DOM. This contradicts the spirit of FR-023-08 ("data: URIs MUST be rejected for everything except whitelisted image MIME types") because SVG is a script-capable MIME type. The test corpus payload[4] for `dataUriAttacks` uses exactly this vector, but the FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS check in `xss-payload-tests.spec.ts` only inspects the text content of the output — it never decodes the base64 or checks for forbidden patterns inside the SVG data URI. All other requirements (FR-023-01 through FR-023-07, FR-023-09 through FR-023-12) are correctly implemented with strong evidence.

findings.description: `data:image/svg+xml;base64,...` payloads containing executable SVG (e.g. `` in the final HTML. The `renderer.image` only blocks `data:text/html` and `data:application/javascript` (sanitization-pipeline.ts:121-123), passing all other data: image/* through to DOMPurify. DOMPurify's `ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP: /^(?:http|https|mailto):/i` does not strip `data:` on `img` src when `USE_PROFILES: { html: true }` is active (confirmed by isolated test). The test corpus payload[4] in `dataUriAttacks` (`![x](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyBvbmxvYWQ9YWxlcnQoMSkvPg==)`) tests for this exact vector and 'passes' only because `xss-payload-tests.spec.ts`'s FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS do not decode the base64 content to inspect it.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/sanitization-pipeline.ts:118-132 (renderer.image only blocks data:text/html and data:application/javascript); tests/security/xss-payloads.json:dataUriAttacks[4] (the svg+xml;base64 payload); tests/security/xss-payload-tests.spec.ts:35-56 (FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS do not include a base64-decode check)
findings.recommendation: Add `data:image/svg+xml` to the blocked MIME types in the `renderer.image` handler alongside `data:text/html` and `data:application/javascript`. SVG is a script-capable format and should not be permitted as a data: URI. The fix is a one-line addition: `|| lower.startsWith('data:image/svg+xml')` at sanitization-pipeline.ts:121. Also add a corpus test that asserts `.blocked` is non-empty (or `.sanitized` does not contain `data:image/svg+xml`) for such payloads.

findings.description:
The PRD states 'the literal substring `alert(` MUST NOT survive to the output'. This is enforced for the `` case (the pipeline refuses with empty sanitized output). However, OWASP padded-script variants like `< script>alert(1)` and comment-broken `ipt>alert(1)` produce output containing the literal `alert(` as text content (e.g. `

< script>alert(1)

`). The string is inert as a text node but the literal `alert(` substring survives. The xss-payload-tests.spec.ts FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS do not check for `alert(` — only the security-sanitization.test.ts line 126 does, and only for the canonical `` input.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/sanitization-pipeline.ts:472-484 (POST_SANITIZATION_BAD_PATTERNS); plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/tests/unit/security-sanitization.test.ts:124-127 (only checks the canonical <script> case); manual test: `sanitizeMarkdown('< script>alert(1)</script>').sanitized` contains 'alert('
findings.recommendation: The text-node `alert(` is not executable, so this is a low-severity aesthetic issue rather than an exploitable gap. The PRD's intent ('alert(' MUST NOT survive) appears to target inline-script contexts, not entity-escaped text. If the strict literal reading of FR-023-02 must be satisfied, add `alert\(` to POST_SANITIZATION_BAD_PATTERNS — but note this would break the FR-023-12 legitimate-content test for code fences containing `alert(`. Instead, scope the check to attribute contexts only.

findings.description: The request-detail 500 bug (Hono JSX serializer throwing `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'res.isEscaped')` when fragment returned null) is fixed. Two region templates (`request-timeline.tsx:46`, `standards-applied.tsx:31`) replaced `return null;` with `return <></>;`. The route handler in `request-detail.ts` returns `notFound(c)` for regex-invalid IDs, missing state files, and null stubs. All 18 test assertions for request-detail now pass.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/routes/request-detail.ts:26-35 (null guard + notFound); plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/templates/fragments/request-timeline.tsx:46 (`return <></>` instead of null); git commit 5172490 'PLAN-041 Phase A — fix request-detail 500 from null fragments'; bun test: 15 pass 0 fail for request-detail-regions.test.ts

findings.description: `renderer.link` in the marked renderer emits `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` for absolute http(s) URLs. Additionally, a DOMPurify `uponSanitizeAttribute` hook and a `uponSanitizeElement` hook both enforce these attributes, making the hardening idempotent even for raw inline `<a href="https://...">` input. The specific test assertion in security-sanitization.test.ts:114-119 passes.

`on*=` attributes are stripped via the DOMPurify `uponSanitizeAttribute` hook (pipeline.ts:255-259) which sets `allowedAttributes[name] = false` for any attribute starting with `on`. Additionally, `FORBIDDEN_ATTRIBUTES` does not need to list them individually since the hook handles all variants. escapeHtml encodes `=` as `=` breaking pattern detection in text contexts.

OWASP filter evasion patterns (mixed case `<ScRiPt>`, padded `< script`, comment-broken, unicode) are handled by DOMPurify's FORBID_TAGS/FORBID_CONTENTS plus POST_SANITIZATION_BAD_PATTERNS. The OWASP corpus (21 payloads) produces no forbidden-pattern violations in the test suite.

Mutation XSS payloads are neutralized via `SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES: true` in DOMPurify config plus the post-sanitization bad-pattern scan. All 7 mutation XSS corpus payloads pass the FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS check.

SVG inline tags and event handlers are stripped: `svg` appears in both FORBIDDEN_TAGS and FORBID_CONTENTS, meaning DOMPurify removes both the tag and its content. USE_PROFILES: { html: true } also restricts to HTML profile, excluding SVG/MathML parsing contexts. All 8 svgAttacks corpus payloads pass.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/security/sanitization-config.ts:72-89 (FORBIDDEN_TAGS includes svg not listed but handled by USE_PROFILES); sanitization-pipeline.ts:231-239 (FORBID_CONTENTS includes 'svg'); 8 svgAttacks payloads all pass

findings.description: CSS channel attacks are blocked by three POST_SANITIZATION_BAD_PATTERNS: `/expression\s*\(/i`, `/behavior\s*:/i`, `/-moz-binding/i`. Additionally `style` is in FORBIDDEN_ATTRIBUTES so inline style attributes are stripped entirely. All 9 cssAttacks corpus payloads pass.

Encoding bypass defense uses `decodeEntities()` which decodes hex numeric (`j`), decimal numeric (`j`), and named entities before the URL-scheme scan. A secondary double-decode pass catches double-encoded payloads. All 10 encodingBypassAttacks payloads pass.

Markdown-specific tricks (reference-style `javascript:` links, autolink abuse) are blocked by `escapeUrl()` in the marked renderer which returns null for non-http(s)/mailto/relative schemes, rendering them as `<span class="blocked-link">`. All 14 markdownSpecific corpus payloads pass.

Legitimate content (prose with 'javascript', code fences with 'script', tables, https links, mailto) passes through sanitization unchanged. All 15 legitimateContent corpus payloads preserve at least one visible word. The false-positive prevention tests in security-sanitization.test.ts also pass.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/tests/unit/security-sanitization.test.ts:171-196 (false-positive prevention); tests/security/xss-payload-tests.spec.ts:116-152 (legitimateContent corpus); manual verification of prose and code-fence cases

title: PRD-024 No Faked Evidence — Conformance Audit
summary: The core verification pipeline (Phase A audit logging, Phase B/C verifier in refuse mode, Phase D operator override) is substantially implemented and the 30-fixture red-team test suite exists. However, several PRD requirements are only partially met: the three specific error codes (EVIDENCE_FABRICATED, EVIDENCE_EXIT_CODE_MISMATCH, EVIDENCE_OUTPUT_MISMATCH) are not emitted — a single VERIFICATION_FAILED code is used for all failure modes. The verification report is named verification-report.jsonl (one shared JSONL per request) rather than per-phase verification-<phase>.json files; it omits the claimed and actual evidence values, the overall-verdict summary row, and the wall-clock + dollar cost fields required by FR-024-10. The trust_level-gated path for unclassifiable commands (FR-024-05, AC-10/AC-11) is absent entirely. The verification-<phase>.json override_applied=true, override_reason fields required by AC-06 are only logged to daemon stderr, not written to the report. The portal cost split (agent_cost_usd vs verification_cost_usd, AC-07) is not implemented.

findings.description: The PRD mandates three distinct error codes: EVIDENCE_EXIT_CODE_MISMATCH (FR-024-06), EVIDENCE_OUTPUT_MISMATCH (FR-024-07), and EVIDENCE_FABRICATED (FR-024-08). The code emits a single VERIFICATION_FAILED for all three failure modes, making it impossible for operators or automated tooling to distinguish why a phase failed.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/spawn-session.sh:544 — `error: "VERIFICATION_FAILED"` is always the envelope error key. The verifier's internal reason strings (command_not_in_audit_log, exit_code_mismatch, output_tail_mismatch) exist only in the JSONL report, not in the envelope error field.
findings.recommendation: Map verifier reason strings to the PRD-mandated error codes when overwriting the envelope in the Phase C block (spawn-session.sh:529-554). E.g. command_not_in_audit_log → EVIDENCE_FABRICATED, exit_code_mismatch → EVIDENCE_EXIT_CODE_MISMATCH, output_tail_mismatch → EVIDENCE_OUTPUT_MISMATCH.

findings.description: The PRD specifies a `verification-<phase>.json` file per phase containing: claimed evidence, actual evidence, classification per entry, verdict per entry, overall verdict, and total verification cost (wall-clock + dollar). The code writes a single `verification-report.jsonl` (JSONL, not JSON, shared across all phases). Per-row records omit the claimed and actual evidence values (only verdict strings are written), there is no overall-verdict summary entry, and there is no cost field.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/lib/verification/verifier.sh:323 — `local report="${req_dir}/verification-report.jsonl"`. Per-row jq at lines 452-476 writes ts, phase, command, classification, checks, verdict, reason — but not claim_exit, claim_tail, actual_exit, actual_tail. No summary row or cost field is written.
findings.recommendation: Rename the output to verification-<phase>.json (one file per phase). Add claimed_exit_code, claimed_output_tail, actual_exit_code, actual_output_tail fields per row. Add a trailing summary entry with overall_verdict and verification_wall_time_ms. Even $0 as a placeholder for verification_cost_usd satisfies shape consistency.

findings.description: FR-024-05 requires that unclassifiable commands fail the phase UNLESS the request's trust_level is at or above the AC-04 threshold. AC-10 (low trust → fail) and AC-11 (high trust → UNCLASSIFIABLE_TRUSTED verdict, phase passes) are also unimplemented. The verifier unconditionally marks unclassifiable commands as would_have_failed regardless of trust level.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/lib/verification/verifier.sh:433-441 — the `unclassifiable` branch sets verdict=would_have_failed with no trust_level check. No trust_level lookup exists anywhere in lib/verification/. No UNCLASSIFIABLE_TRUSTED verdict exists in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Pass the request's trust_level (from the request metadata JSON) to verify_envelope. In the unclassifiable branch, check if trust_level >= threshold; if so, set verdict=UNCLASSIFIABLE_TRUSTED (pass); otherwise keep would_have_failed with reason=unclassifiable_command.

findings.description: AC-06 requires that when an operator override is applied, the `verification-<phase>.json` record `override_applied=true, override_reason=<operator-supplied>`. The daemon logs the override to stderr only (`verification_override_applied: request=... phase=...`) but never writes these fields into the verification report file.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/spawn-session.sh:483-488 — only a printf to stderr. The verifier (lib/verification/verifier.sh) is not called back after the override is detected; the report already written by the verifier does not get an override annotation.
findings.recommendation: After detecting a valid override (spawn-session.sh:477-488), append an additional JSONL row to verification-report.jsonl (or the per-phase file once renamed) with `{override_applied: true, override_reason: ..., override_operator: ..., ts: ...}` so the operator's audit trail is in the file, not just daemon stderr.

findings.requirement: AC-07 / Q-024-05 — portal cost split agent_cost_usd vs verification_cost_usd
findings.description: AC-07 requires the portal request page to show running cost broken into agent_cost_usd and verification_cost_usd separately. Neither field exists in the portal's CostLedgerEntry type, the RequestRecord render type, the cost aggregator, nor any portal route or template.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/cost/types.ts:27-36 — CostLedgerEntry has only cost_usd (no agent/verification split). plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/server/types/render.ts:212 — RequestRecord has a single `cost?: number` field.
findings.recommendation: Add agent_cost_usd and verification_cost_usd to CostLedgerEntry and RequestRecord. Even if verification re-execution is shell-only ($0 in practice), the ledger shape must be established so the portal can render the split.

findings.requirement: FR-024-04 — non-idempotent audit log includes exit_code match
findings.description: FR-024-04 requires that for non-idempotent commands, a recorded invocation with the same command AND the same exit_code satisfies verification. The audit log writer only fires on PreToolUse (before execution), so exit_code is always null in every audit log entry. The verifier calls audit_log_has_command without an exit_code argument (matching any). A claimed git push exit_code:0 is verified even if the push failed (exit_code:1), because the audit only records that the command was attempted.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/hooks/audit-log-writer.sh:101 — `exit_code: null` in every emitted row. plugins/autonomous-dev/lib/verification/verifier.sh:362 — `audit_log_has_command "${req_dir}" "${cmd}"` with no exit_code arg, so want_exit='any'.
findings.recommendation: Implement a PostToolUse companion hook that updates the audit log row with the actual exit_code and duration after the Bash tool completes. Then pass claim_exit to audit_log_has_command in the non-idempotent branch so the exit code is actually verified.

findings.requirement: FR-024-09 — agent cannot read audit log directly
findings.description: The PRD states the agent cannot write to or read the command-audit.jsonl directly. The daemon creates the file at mode 0600 and does not pass its path in the agent prompt or tool descriptions. However, the agent runs with --permission-mode bypassPermissions AND --add-dir pointing at req_dir, which contains the audit file. Under bypassPermissions the agent can read any file it can access by path, including command-audit.jsonl via a Bash cat or Read tool call.
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/bin/spawn-session.sh:267-269 — audit file created at ${req_dir}/command-audit.jsonl, chmod 0600. spawn-session.sh:330,342 — `--add-dir "${req_dir}"` exposes the directory. spawn-session.sh:332,344 — `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` bypasses file-level ACL checks.
findings.recommendation: Move the audit log to a daemon-owned directory outside req_dir (e.g. ${DAEMON_HOME}/.audit/${req_id}/command-audit.jsonl) that is not passed via --add-dir. Alternatively, accept the current architecture as a known limitation and document it explicitly since the agent's incentive to read its own audit log is minimal.

findings.description: AC-04 specifies that when a non-idempotent command is verified via the audit log, the verification record shows `verdict=AUDIT_LOG_MATCH`. The verifier uses `verdict=verified` for all passing entries regardless of how they were verified (re-execution vs audit log presence).
findings.evidence: plugins/autonomous-dev/lib/verification/verifier.sh:351 — `local verdict="verified"` is the default. The non-idempotent branch (lines 426-431) has no code that sets a distinct audit-log-match label; it inherits the default `verified`.
findings.recommendation: In the non-idempotent branch, after the presence check passes, set `verdict="AUDIT_LOG_MATCH"` so operators can see the verification path in the report.

findings.description: The core execution path is fully implemented: executor phases (integration/deploy/test) claiming status=pass with non-empty evidence trigger the Phase C verifier in refuse mode (FR-024-01). Idempotent commands are re-executed with env stripping and 300s timeout (FR-024-03). Per-request operator override via verification-override.json is implemented in both CLI (autonomous-dev.sh:718) and portal route (override.ts), with stale-override detection (FR-024-11). Status=fail short-circuits without any verification work (FR-024-12).

The PRD §6 requires a red-team fixture suite of N=30 fixtures (10 per mode). Exactly 30 fixtures exist (10 fabricated, 10 mismatched, 10 stale) with 5 honest fixtures and 5 mode-flip guardrail tests = 40 total bats tests in verifier_refusal_mode.bats, exceeding the metric.
